The owner's chat + the sandbox still honor the tier as set. Net: the ops cascade floor is Sonnet; `default`/`sonnet` tiers are untouched.

**DoD.** (1) `cascade-silent` warns on the June-16 shape (completed trace-emitting series + zero `subagent_progress` in-window) and stays `ok` when traces landed / nothing fired — unit-tested both ways; the all-green report is unaffected. (2) `applyOpsSpawnEnv` floors OPUS/SONNET + `config.model` to Sonnet for the ops session under dev+haiku, keeps Haiku for the Haiku alias, and is a no-op for non-ops sessions, the sandbox, non-dev, and the `default`/`sonnet` tiers — unit-tested. (3) `health_cascade_silent_window_hours` resolves via `getConfig`; both suites + tscs + format green. (4) Box: `dev_model_tier` reverted to `sonnet` (done 2026-06-17 02:20Z); the next cascade emits traces + a digest (time-gated — verify on the 11:00/14:00 UTC fires).

#### 24.69 Portal enhancements consuming the telemetry/health data model (Deep Dive 3, 2026-06-12)

The third and last §24.66-registered deep dive. **Problem:** §24.68's `request_telemetry` + `runHealthChecks()` are invisible on the portal and reachable only from a terminal. This dive surfaces them — the public showcase gets real, transparent per-class cost + provider-health signals, and the owner/Claude-facing `/dev` page gets the triage runbook in-browser. The §24.66 register sketched "per-class cost sparklines on /live, session topology on /architecture"; exploration widened it (owner-approved) to four features.

**Decision register (owner, 2026-06-12 plan-mode conversation):**

- **D-A — Scope = four features.** (1) per-class cost sparklines on `/live`; (2) light up the architecture diagram's previously-structural integration nodes from real telemetry; (3) a health panel on `/dev`; (4) session topology in the Orchestrator node modal on `/architecture`.
- **D-B — Public depth = status + aggregate stats.** Public node modals may show real aggregate numbers (requests-24h, error rate, last-success age, p50 latency) — never raw error text, session ids, or `next_step`.
- **D-C — §9 boundary mechanism = aggregate-only direct read (not a materialized rollup).** `request_telemetry` is a private table and §9 (PORTAL) locks "the portal API SELECTs only public tables, enforced by structure." Owner's call: a read-only **aggregate-only** projection (sums/counts/percentiles, GROUP BY class/provider) carries no PII and no real attack surface, so a materialized public rollup isn't worth the machinery. This is a **deliberate, narrowly-scoped** relaxation of §9's wording for aggregate telemetry only — guarded structurally by **D2** and a projection-safety regression test. (The rollup alternative would have been pure SQL in the existing host-sweep — no LLM spend either way; this choice is about code volume, not cost.)
- **D1 — One new public aggregator + endpoint.** `src/modules/portal/observability.ts` → `getObservability()` (30 s-cached, mirrors `portkey-analytics.ts`), served at `GET /api/observability` → `{ spend_by_class, providers, session_topology }`. One endpoint feeds both consumers. `/api/telemetry`'s contract is untouched.
- **D2 — PII-free projection is the §9 guarantee.** Every SELECT lists only aggregate columns — `SUM(cost_microusd)`, `COUNT(*)`, `SUM(ok=0)`, `MAX(ts) WHERE ok=1`, a JS percentile over a `latency_ms` stream. It NEVER selects `error` / `session_id` / `trace_id` / `details_json`. A regression test asserts the served JSON contains none of those keys at any depth.
- **D3 — `spend_by_class`**: `Record<'ops'|'chat'|'sandbox'|'host', { microusd_24h, buckets: number[] }>`; `buckets` = 24 hourly `SUM(cost_microusd)` over the last 24 h (`strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H', ts)`, zero-filled), NULL cost → 0. Drives the sparklines and makes host-side spend (sim prose, sanitizer) publicly visible for the first time.
- **D4 — `providers`**: `Array<{ provider, requests_24h, errors_24h, error_rate, last_success_age_sec|null, p50_ms|null }>` over 24 h, GROUP BY provider (`last_success_age_sec` = now − MAX(ts WHERE ok=1)). Drives node status + node-modal facts.
- **D5 — `session_topology`**: `{ chat, ops, sandbox }` counts of *active* sessions, classified host-side by reusing the §24.68 helpers (`isOpsSession`; non-owner group → sandbox; else chat). Non-PII counts; enriches the Orchestrator-node modal — no new SVG geometry.
- **D6 — Node→provider mapping (honesty rule satisfied, not bent).** Newly-lit nodes are honest because `request_telemetry` IS a real probe. `cont-portkey` → `[portkey]`; `cont-jobs` → `[serpapi,greenhouse,lever]`; `trig-google` → `[gmail,calendar,drive]`; `host-onecli` → the union (gateway-down shape: `down` only when every provider with recent attempts is failing). `cont-anthropic` stays structural (we probe Portkey, not Anthropic directly). Nodes with no mapping keep `probe: 'structural'`. **Δ §24.122 (2026-06-18): the `host-onecli` "union" was dishonest** — greenhouse/lever are host-side, key-less ATS fetches that never traverse the gateway, so folding them into the gateway node misattributed non-gateway traffic to it. Corrected to `host-onecli` → `[gmail,calendar,drive,serpapi,portkey]` (the credential-injected providers that actually ride the proxy); `cont-jobs` keeps the three-source aggregate but its prose broadens to name the key-less fallback tier.
- **D7 — Provider→status**: `down` = every recent attempt failed; `degraded` = error rate ≥ `arch_provider_error_rate_degraded` (0.25, `getConfig`); else `healthy`; no rows in window → `idle` (honest: no recent call, no claim). **Staleness is deliberately NOT a node-color factor** (amended 2026-06-13 after the box surfaced quiet-but-clean providers reading "degraded"): a low-frequency provider whose last success is old only because it's called infrequently is *quiet, not impaired*. Staleness is surfaced via the owner-facing `stale-surface` health finding (`health_surface_stale_hours`, 48 h) instead — keeping the public node color honest. (The earlier `arch_provider_stale_success_sec` 1 h knob is removed.)
- **D8 — `/dev` health panel** reads `runHealthChecks({ skipLiveProbes: true })` via new dev-gated `GET /api/dev/health` (404 off-dev, like every `/api/dev/*`), returning the `HealthReport` verbatim with `next_step` rendered in a copy-pasteable mono block — the in-browser triage runbook. Live probes (exec/spend) stay CLI-only.
- **D9 — Sparkline = custom inline SVG** (`frontend/src/components/Sparkline.tsx`); no chart lib (repo has zero by convention). Static polyline over normalized buckets in a fixed viewBox → deterministic Playwright baselines, no animation; `aria-hidden` + a text label for a11y.

**Endpoints.** `GET /api/observability` (public, 30 s cache, aggregate-only) and `GET /api/dev/health` (dev-gated, `skipLiveProbes`). Both gain `emptyPayloadFor` entries for the §24.36 forced-state seam.

**Config keys (defaults.json):** `portal_observability_cache_ms` 30000, `arch_provider_error_rate_degraded` 0.25, `sandbox_session_reap_idle_sec` 900. No new inspector knobs (stable infra thresholds; add later only if a tuning need surfaces).

**Δ 2026-06-17 (B2 — sandbox session leak; `session_topology.sandbox` grew without bound).** The box accumulated 28 `status='active'` sandbox sessions (and climbing) that the topology count tallies, because `teardownSimulatorSession` (simulator.ts) only `killContainer`s the run's container — it never retired the session ROW. Worse, `finalizeSimulatorRun` is keyed on the in-memory `runs` accumulator, so a run cut off by a host restart never finalizes → never tears down → leaks regardless. Two fixes: (1) **teardown also closes the session** (`updateSession(id, { status: 'closed' })`, direct — `killContainer`'s `onExit` never fires when the container entry is already gone); (2) **a host-sweep reaper** `reapStaleSandboxSessions()` (MODULE-HOOK `career-pilot-sandbox-reap`, before the session loop) closes any sandbox-group active session once `COALESCE(last_active, created_at)` is older than `sandbox_session_reap_idle_sec` (900 s — 3× the 5-min run ceiling, so a live run is never touched). `'closed'` is the canonical retired status (`getActiveSessions` filters `status='active'`). The reaper also retroactively drains the existing leak on its first ticks (no separate box op). **DoD:** the reaper marks a stale sandbox session inactive while leaving a fresh sandbox session and any owner-group session untouched (unit-tested); teardown retires the session it kills; `sandbox_session_reap_idle_sec` via `getConfig`; suites + tsc + format green; box: the sandbox topology count drains to the live-run count within a few sweep ticks after deploy.

**Definition of done.**
1. `GET /api/observability` returns `spend_by_class` (4 classes, 24 zero-filled hourly buckets each), `providers` (per-provider 24 h counts/error-rate/last-success-age/p50), and `session_topology` — aggregate-only; the projection-safety test confirms no `error`/`session_id`/`trace_id`/`details_json` in the payload.
2. `/live` renders a `SPEND BY CLASS` panel with per-class 24 h cost + sparkline; layout-stable (§24.36); loading/empty/error branches present.
3. `/architecture` lights the four mapped nodes from real telemetry (`cont-portkey`, `cont-jobs`, `trig-google`, `host-onecli`) per D6/D7; their modals show aggregate provider facts; `cont-orch` modal shows session topology; un-mapped nodes stay structural with the honesty note.
4. `/dev` shows a health panel (`runHealthChecks` findings grouped by severity, each with `next_step`), dev-gated; `GET /api/dev/health` 404s off-dev.
5. All thresholds via `getConfig()`; no magic numbers. Both suites + tscs + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend) green; Playwright baselines for the three new surfaces.
6. Box verification: live `/api/observability` shows non-zero `host`-class spend + real `last_success_age_sec`; `/api/dev/health` returns the report on dev and 404s on a non-dev stack; manual portal pass over the Access-gated dev URL confirms sparklines, lit nodes, and the health panel; an induced failure flips a node to degraded/down honestly. Closes the §24.66 three-dive arc. **Box-verified 2026-06-12 (same-day):** deploy-backend + deploy-frontend + frontend-e2e (semantic + a11y, 66 green) all passed; live `/api/observability` returned real data — `spend_by_class` chat $0.07 (the §24.67 CLI smoke turn) + host 34 µUSD (the live Haiku probe), `session_topology {chat:2, ops:1, sandbox:15}`, and providers honestly **degraded** by the honesty rule (portkey last-success ~2 h old > the 3600 s stale threshold; gmail error-rate ≥ 0.25 in-window) — not fake green; projection-safety re-confirmed on the live payload (no `error`/`session_id`/`trace_id`/`details_json`). `/api/dev/health` returned a 7-finding all-ok report (the live Gmail/gateway probe correctly skipped on the polled path — `skipLiveProbes`). The a11y contrast trap (white-on-red `destructive` badge, 3.48:1) was caught by the dev.spec axe gate and fixed to dot-based severity before push. Remaining: owner's manual browser pass over the Access-gated dev URL.

---

#### 24.70 Production abuse-hardening — the layered public-edge defense (Phase 9.4a, 2026-06-14)

**Problem.** The public showcase's one money-spending path — the Recruiter Simulator (`POST /api/simulator` spawns a sandbox container + real LLM tokens per visitor run) — has **zero** abuse protection today: `checkSimulatorAllowed()` is a NOT_WIRED stub that only reads `simulator_enabled`; the Worker BFF proxy (`frontend/src/routes/api/$.ts`) is a pure pass-through; `cloudflare.tf` carries only the owner-only Access gate. `/contact` is similarly open (spam-relays straight to the owner's Telegram). Phase 9.4 was a single milestone-table row ("prod cutover + hardening"); this drill-in decomposes it.

**9.4 decomposition.** 9.4 splits into **9.4a — abuse-hardening** (this section, buildable + dev-verifiable now) and **9.4b — prod cutover/content** (persona de-genericization, real `candidate_profile` + the live `/api/profile` projection, server-side resume PDF, the §24.65 /kit-surface D3 gate — owner-content-gated, its own drill-in). 9.4a ships the full layered defense in one pass (owner call, 2026-06-14: "everything now") so the prod cutover is a near-pure apply and the public surface is protected from its first publish — the 9.1 gate-before-route discipline applied to abuse.

**Verification reality.** Prod doesn't exist yet (only the Access-gated dev surface is deployed). The **application layer** (Turnstile, Workers RL, the DO caps) is built AND box-verified on dev now via Cloudflare's always-pass Turnstile test keys (D5). The **edge/origin layer** (Terraform WAF/Bot-Fight, real Turnstile challenges, origin-JWT-vs-forged-assertion) gets its live verification at the 9.4b prod apply — recorded, not skipped.

**Decision register (owner, 2026-06-14 plan-mode conversation).**

- **D1 — Verification lives at the Worker edge (supersedes CLOUDFLARE_PATTERNS §2's "backend Express verifies").** Under the §24.39 D12 reversal the browser talks ONLY to the Worker; all `/api/*` (JSON + SSE) proxies through `$.ts` to the tunnel. The Worker is therefore the only place that sees a raw visitor request before it crosses the tunnel, so **Turnstile siteverify + Workers Rate Limiting + the Durable-Object caps all live in `$.ts`** — which grows from a pure pass-through into a "guard the two public mutations, blind-forward everything else" proxy. `contact-relay.ts`'s header already states this intent. Backend `checkSimulatorAllowed()` stays as the `simulator_enabled` kill switch + the in-SDK `simulator_max_budget_usd` output cap — defense-in-depth behind the edge, not the perimeter.
- **D2 — AOP/mTLS is inapplicable to the tunnel; origin Access-JWT is the real Layer-3 (corrects CLOUDFLARE_PATTERNS §5 Layer 3).** `infra/tunnel.tf` fronts the backend with a cloudflared tunnel: the VM has no inbound ports, the API binds `127.0.0.1` only, the daemon dials outbound. There is **no public origin to pull from**, so Authenticated Origin Pulls is moot — the tunnel + loopback-bind already are the "only Cloudflare reaches the origin" guarantee. The applicable Layer-3 defense-in-depth is **validating `Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion` in Express** against the team JWKS (`aud` = the api Access app) — nothing validates it today; the api Access app's deny-by-default + the worker service-token are the only gate. Env-gated (on only when `CF_ACCESS_TEAM` + `CF_ACCESS_AUD` are set).
- **D3 — The daily caps enforce in the BACKEND (SQLite), not a Worker Durable Object — a deviation from the original plan, decided 2026-06-14 after build-time research.** The TanStack Start server-entry (`@tanstack/react-start/server-entry`) cannot cleanly export a DO class: it's **undocumented**, with several open framework/plugin issues (verified against the primary TanStack hosting doc + workers-sdk #11100 / TanStack #6487). Rather than build the must-be-invincible budget cap on a fragile, undocumented framework hack, the per-IP daily cap + global $-budget live in `checkSimulatorAllowed()` — the seam §24.19 explicitly staged for exactly this. This is *better*, not merely easier: the backend already holds the real per-run cost ledger (`simulator_runs.total_cost_cents`), so the budget caps against **actual spend**, not an edge pre-charge estimate; SQLite is strongly consistent; and the caps already exist in `defaults.json` (`sandbox_daily_global_budget_usd`, `sandbox_per_ip_daily_run_cap`). Concurrent-start overshoot is bounded by also counting **in-flight** (not-yet-persisted) runs toward both caps. The Worker forwards the CF-verified visitor IP as `x-cp-client-ip` (overwriting any client value, so a spoofed/rotated header can't evade the per-IP cap); the backend keys the count on it. Enforcing one hop past the edge is fine for a *daily* backstop — the edge (Turnstile + Workers-RL burst) already sheds floods/bots in real time; the daily cap is slow correctness, not latency. (Owner-weighed alternative: KV-at-the-edge — rejected as eventually-consistent + estimate-based + a second spend ledger that drifts from the real one.)
- **D4 — Config tier by enforcement layer.** Edge-tier (Worker, no DB): the Workers-RL burst limits (`wrangler.jsonc` `unsafe.bindings`) + the Turnstile secret/hostname (env.dev's always-pass test secret committed; prod injected post-deploy). Host-tier (`defaults.json`/`getConfig`, where the backend enforces the daily caps): `sandbox_daily_global_budget_usd`, `sandbox_per_ip_daily_run_cap`, `simulator_max_budget_usd` (the in-flight estimate *and* the in-SDK per-run cap) — all pre-existing — plus the one addition `origin_jwt_validation_enabled`. (The earlier plan's `SIM_*` edge vars are dropped with the DO per D3; the daily caps live host-side, where they're enforced.) Zero-magic-numbers holds across both tiers.
- **D5 — Dev uses Cloudflare always-pass Turnstile test keys** (site `1x00000000000000000000AA`, secret `1x0000000000000000000000000000000AA`): the widget renders (visual + flow parity) but never challenges the owner, and dev is Access-gated regardless. Prod uses an owner-created widget (`VITE_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY` build var + `TURNSTILE_SECRET` Worker secret). Env-parameterized via the `[env.dev]` wrangler block.
- **D6 — Terraform WAF posture is host-scoped (free-zone-correct; reconciles CLOUDFLARE_PATTERNS §6).** Two build-surfaced facts: the free CF managed ruleset is **auto-on, not Terraform-deployable** (deployable managed rulesets need Pro+ — nothing to declare; it just runs), and free **Bot Fight Mode is a zone-wide toggle** — enabling it would also evaluate `api.<host>` and could challenge the *automated* Worker→tunnel fetch (so §6's "ON apex / OFF api" isn't achievable for a zone-wide free toggle when both hosts share the zone). We therefore do **not** enable zone-wide Bot Fight Mode: the api host is already deny-by-default behind Access (no token, no entry), and the public host's edge protection is two **host-scoped** `cloudflare_ruleset` rules (`infra/waf.tf`, pinned to `local.frontend_host` + the two mutation paths so the api path is untouched, per-env): a rate-limit rule (10 s window, block) in front of the Worker's own burst limiter + a custom managed-challenge on elevated `cf.threat_score`. `terraform validate` confirms the v4 syntax; `apply` is owner-gated (needs the CF token + touches the live zone).

**Layered defense, cheapest-first (the order in `$.ts` for the two guarded mutations).**

| # | Layer | Where | Catches |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Free managed ruleset (auto) + host-scoped WAF rules | CF edge (`waf.tf`, D6) | known-bad + floods + high-threat, before the Worker runs |
| 1 | Workers Rate Limiting burst | `$.ts` (`SANDBOX_BURST` 5/60 s, `CONTACT_BURST` 3/60 s) | 60 s scripted floods |
| 2 | Turnstile siteverify | `$.ts` (human gate, D1/D5) | non-human callers — rejected before any budget is touched |
| 3 | Global $-budget (real spend) | Express `checkSimulatorAllowed` / SQLite (D3) | total daily spend ($5 ceiling) |
| 4 | Per-IP daily cap | Express `checkSimulatorAllowed` / SQLite (D3) | single-IP grinding (`sandbox_per_ip_daily_run_cap`, 5/day) |
| 5 | Backend output cap | container (`simulator_max_budget_usd` 0.10) | per-run blast radius even on a passed run |
| 6 | Origin Access-JWT | Express (D2) | a request that somehow reached the loopback port |

(The simulator runs all six; `/contact` runs 0–2 only — it spends no money, just relays.)

**Guard contract (`$.ts`).** `guardPublicMutation(request, env)` runs only for `POST /api/contact` and `POST /api/simulator`; returns a 429 (`{ error, retryAt }`) on a rate/cap miss or a 403 (`turnstile_failed`) on a bad token, else `null` → the existing `proxy()` runs unchanged. Client IP from `cf-connecting-ip` (read before `proxy()` strips it). Every other path stays byte-stable pass-through. Turnstile actions: `contact_submit`, `simulator_run`; hostname asserted against `TURNSTILE_HOSTNAME` (env).

**Config (the D4 split).** Edge (`wrangler.jsonc`, per env): `TURNSTILE_SECRET` + `TURNSTILE_HOSTNAME` (vars) + the RL burst limits (`unsafe.bindings`). Host (`defaults.json`/`getConfig`): the daily caps the backend enforces — `sandbox_daily_global_budget_usd`, `sandbox_per_ip_daily_run_cap`, `simulator_max_budget_usd` (all pre-existing) — plus the NEW `origin_jwt_validation_enabled` (false locally; true on deployed stacks). Migration 132 adds `simulator_runs.client_ip` for the per-IP count.

**Definition of done.**
1. `$.ts` Turnstile-verifies + rate-limits `POST /api/{contact,simulator}` and DO-caps the simulator (global $ + per-IP), returning 429/403 honestly; all other `/api/*` (incl. SSE) stay byte-stable pass-through; the contact + simulator forms render the Turnstile widget and gate submit on a token.
2. The backend `checkSimulatorAllowed(ip)` enforces the per-IP daily count + the global $-budget (real persisted cost + an in-flight estimate) against the `getConfig` caps, scoped to the UTC day; the Worker forwards the CF-verified `x-cp-client-ip` (overwriting any client value); a tripped cap returns HTTP 429 (D3).
3. `infra/waf.tf` adds two host-scoped `cloudflare_ruleset` rules (rate-limit + a `cf.threat_score` managed-challenge) on the public host's mutation paths, per-env via `local.frontend_host`; `terraform validate` passes (apply owner-gated). No zone-wide Bot Fight Mode (D6).
4. Express validates `Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion` when `origin_jwt_validation_enabled` + `CF_ACCESS_TEAM`/`CF_ACCESS_AUD` are set; local/test paths skip it.
5. Edge tunables are wrangler config; the daily caps are host-tier `getConfig` (D4); no magic numbers; both suites + 3 tscs + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend) green.
6. **Dev box (application layer):** with test keys auto-passing, hammering the dev `/simulator` trips the per-IP 429 after `sandbox_per_ip_daily_run_cap` (backend) and the global-budget 429 at the daily ceiling, plus the Workers-RL 429 on rapid repeats; `wrangler tail` shows the edge-guard decisions; non-gated panels stay live. `terraform apply -var environment=dev` activates the managed ruleset + custom rule; origin-JWT rejects a forged assertion.
7. **Prod-gated (recorded for 9.4b):** a real Turnstile challenge from a real browser, live Bot Fight Mode, and a public abuse simulation — verified when the public prod surface exists.

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.70 (9.4 decomposition + D1–D6 + the layered table + guard/config contracts + DoD); the §24.38 Phase-9 table's 9.4 row repointed here (9.4a hardening / 9.4b cutover); PORTAL §5.7 (the /contact Turnstile build note updated to the Worker-edge reality) + §5.3 (the simulator abuse note); CLOUDFLARE_PATTERNS §1/§2/§5/§8/§9 reconciled to D12 + the tunnel topology (Worker verifies; AOP dropped → origin-JWT). Memory: [[status_current]].

---

#### 24.71 Agent-composed work profile — the auto-built `/work` page (Phase 9.4b, 2026-06-14)

**Problem / framing.** "De-Jane-Doe" (the §24.70 9.4b content half) is the project's most visible win: the portal still renders a hardcoded `Jane Doe` object (`frontend/src/lib/work-profile.ts`) because the `GET /api/profile` projection over the private `candidate_profile` was deferred (PORTAL §5.6 build note). The naïve close is "owner hand-fills 11 profile fields." The owner's call (2026-06-14 conversation) elevates it: instead of a fill-in form, the **agent composes a presentable `/work` page from light onboarding basics** — *"give me the basics of who you are and I'll build you a nice professional page."* This is simultaneously better owner UX **and** a second, very legible AI showcase sitting on the single most recruiter-visited page (it feeds the owner's TODO #2 "AI-clarity flavor" + TODO #5 "how-it-works"). This drill-in specs the **profile/content** slice of 9.4b; the deploy mechanics of the prod cutover + the §24.65 /kit D3 gate remain the other 9.4b work.

**Decision register (owner, 2026-06-14 plan-mode conversation).**

- **D1 — Compose at write-time, render at read-time (the load-bearing rule).** The agent generates the structured page artifact during an onboarding/update **turn** (it is already a live agent at that moment); the portal projects that persisted artifact **deterministically**. No LLM in the SSR hot path — that would add per-visitor latency, reword the page every refresh, and reintroduce a per-visitor spend/abuse vector on a public route (exactly what §24.70 just hardened against). Generation is an explicit, persisted, owner-approvable step; the public page changes only when the owner asks.
- **D2 — The `WorkProfile` TS interface is the agent's OUTPUT contract, not a constraint on the owner's input.** The section menu the agent composes *into* — `title`, `bio[]`, `lookingFor[]`, `experience[]`, `projects[]`, `writing[]`, `skills[]`, `education[]`, `links` — is the existing `frontend/src/lib/work-profile.ts` shape (already the `/work` render contract). So the owner writes a **natural, comprehensive master resume**; the agent maps + curates it into that shape. The "fixed-section contract" lives on the agent's output side, freeing the owner from hand-formatting — they optimize for completeness + accuracy of raw material, nothing else.
- **D3 — Persist the composed page as a JSON projection on `candidate_profile`, not as new per-section columns.** Add `work_profile_json` (the composed `WorkProfile`) + provenance columns `work_profile_generated_at` + `work_profile_source` (`agent` | `owner` | `seed`). The portal shape is a *view* the agent regenerates — the authoritative source stays `master_resume` + the basics fields, so a wide structured-column migration would duplicate truth and drift. One JSON blob matches how the render hook + `update_profile_field` already treat JSON-valued fields (`target_roles`, `skills`, `location_pref`). Single ALTER, additive.
- **D4 — No fabrication: compose, don't invent (the honesty guardrail).** Generation = curate / structure / word the owner's **real** material. The agent must not invent a company, date, title, or project absent from the source. The showcase line is *"auto-composed by the agent from my master resume,"* never *"the agent wrote my career."* Encoded in the composer skill + a self-check pass (output facts must trace to source); the on-page provenance marker makes the claim legible rather than a hidden trick.
- **D5 — Minimum bar + graceful degradation reuse PORTAL §12's placeholder strategy.** The composer decides per-section "enough material → include; else omit"; the render already skips null sections, and §12 already specs the not-yet-enough UX (muted/italic in public mode, bright "fill via Telegram" in owner mode). A small readiness minimum (name + bio + (≥1 experience **or** a master resume) + ≥1 link) gates the "looks finished" claim — below it the page honestly reads WIP, which §12 notes recruiters charitably read as "launching in public."
- **D6 — Onboarding simplifies to "basics + master resume → compose → preview."** The render-hook onboarding sentinel (`full_name → target_roles → comp_floor → master_resume → bio → search_goals`) shifts emphasis to collecting the **basics** (name, contact/links, target roles, comp) + the **master resume** blob, after which the agent runs the compose step and shows the owner a preview to approve. The owner then refines in natural language ("tighten the bio," "drop that project," "lead with the agent platform") → recompose. This *is* the "give me the basics and I'll build your page" loop.

**Persisted artifact + flow.**

```
Telegram onboarding ──basics + master_resume──▶ candidate_profile (authoritative source)
                                                      │
                          agent compose turn (write-time, D1/D2/D4)
                                                      ▼
                                   candidate_profile.work_profile_json  ← owner preview/approve
                                                      │
                              GET /api/profile (deterministic projection, read-time)
                                                      ▼
                                       SSR /work + landing hero  +  provenance marker
```

The agent writes the artifact via a new MCP tool (`set_work_profile`, sibling to `update_profile_field`) → host action `career_pilot.set_work_profile` (validates the JSON against the `WorkProfile` shape, stamps `work_profile_generated_at` + `work_profile_source='agent'`). `/api/profile` returns `work_profile_json` when present, else the typed placeholder (so the page is never blank).

**Staging (the portal half is identical either way, so it decomposes cleanly).**

- **9.4b-1 — Portal projection (the deterministic, instant de-Jane-Doe win).** `GET /api/profile` reads `work_profile_json` (placeholder fallback) and SSR `/work` + the landing hero render it, **replacing the hardcoded `workProfile`**; §12 placeholder strategy for unset bits. Works whether the artifact was agent-composed or owner-seeded — so real content ships the moment the artifact exists (it can be seeded once by hand from the owner's resume to land the visible win before 9.4b-2). The server-side resume **PDF** (PORTAL §5.6) is its own increment on top of the structured artifact — until it lands the Download-PDF button stays omitted, not dead.
- **9.4b-2 — Agent composition (the showcase).** The write-time composer: `master_resume` + basics → `WorkProfile` artifact; the `set_work_profile` MCP tool + host action; the composer skill (section menu from D2 + the D5 minimum bar + the D4 no-fabrication guardrail); the on-page **provenance marker** ("composed by the agent on \<date\> from the master resume"); owner preview/approve + recompose-on-update.
- **9.4b-3 — Candidate identity SSR + deploy-URL config.** Two cleanups surfaced by owner review (the broken `janedoe`/`example.com` links across the site). (a) **Per-candidate contact/social identity** (email, GitHub, LinkedIn, X, website) becomes a canonical `identity` projection on `/api/profile`, read from `candidate_profile` *columns* — NOT the composed blob, so it's available before the page is composed — and SSR'd into `/contact`, the landing teaser, and (via a `profile.links` override, so the two never disagree) `/work`. Every link optional, **omitted when null** (no broken placeholder links). New `public_email` column (+ `update_profile_field` field); the dev/prod public-email split falls out of the separate per-env DBs — no env-specific code. Telegram contact **dropped** (owner-only admin channel, locked strict; the form is the path — owner call 2026-06-14). (b) **Per-DEPLOYMENT constants** — the fork's repo URL (`view source` links), the public origin (absolute og: URLs, must resolve at build), and the **header brand name** — move to build-time env (`VITE_REPO_URL` / `VITE_SITE_URL` / `VITE_PERSON_NAME`), set as GH Environment vars so real identifiers never enter the repo; the committed defaults stay the generic `janedoe` / `example.com` / `Jane Doe` placeholders. The split is the rule: per-candidate facts → DB+SSR; per-deployment identity → build-time env.

**Definition of done.**
1. `GET /api/profile` projects `candidate_profile.work_profile_json` (typed placeholder fallback) and SSR `/work` + the landing hero render it; the hardcoded `Jane Doe` `workProfile` is no longer the live source on a populated profile; unset sections degrade per §12 (no invented data, no blank page).
2. A migration adds `work_profile_json` + `work_profile_generated_at` + `work_profile_source` to `candidate_profile` (single additive ALTER set); the render-persona read path tolerates the new nullable columns.
3. The `set_work_profile` MCP tool + `career_pilot.set_work_profile` host action validate agent output against the `WorkProfile` shape, reject malformed JSON (`isError`, never throw), and stamp provenance; the composer never emits a fact absent from the source (D4 — spot-checked against a seed resume).
4. The composer applies the D5 minimum bar (omit under-sourced sections; gate the "looks finished" claim) and `/work` shows the provenance marker when `work_profile_source='agent'`.
5. The onboarding flow collects basics + master resume then composes + offers an owner preview (D6); a natural-language refinement recomposes and re-stamps `work_profile_generated_at`.
6. No magic numbers (the readiness minimum + any caps via `getConfig`); host + frontend suites + 3 tscs + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend) green.

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.71 (the agent-composed model + D1–D6 + the artifact/flow + the 9.4b-1/9.4b-2 staging + DoD); the §24.38 Phase-9 9.4 row repointed (9.4b content half → here); PORTAL §5.6 (the `/work` build note: agent-composed at write-time, `WorkProfile` as the output contract, provenance marker) + §12 (the owner-populates-variables note: basics-in → agent-composes-page). Memory: [[status_current]].

---

#### 24.72 Server-rendered résumé PDF — master download + tailored-via-simulator (Phase 9.4b, 2026-06-14)

`/work` has always promised a server-rendered Download-PDF (§5.6) — "generated from the structured content, NOT a static PDF" (the "I version-control my résumé" signal). With the real `WorkProfile` now composed (§24.71), it's unblocked. This drill-in specs the PDF *infrastructure* and extends it into the strongest conversion artifact on the site: a résumé **tailored live to the recruiter's own role** via the simulator.

**Two tiers (the second reuses the first's engine).**
- **Tier 1 — master résumé PDF on `/work`.** Server-rendered from the composed `WorkProfile`. Table-stakes (a recruiter wants *a* PDF to forward) and the rendering foundation Tier 2 reuses.
- **Tier 2 — tailored résumé via the simulator.** The simulator already runs `tailor-resume`; its output extends to a full résumé the visitor downloads, aimed at the company/role they typed — plus a cross-sell on `/work` ("Want one aimed at your role? Run the 2-minute simulator →"). Turns "neat demo" into "I walked away with something useful for *my* pipeline" — the forward-up conversion path (§1).

**Decisions.**
- **D1 — `@react-pdf/renderer`, not headless Chromium.** We have *structured data* (`WorkProfile`), not markdown. The two real options are (a) headless Chromium rendering an HTML/CSS template (what `md-to-pdf`/Puppeteer do; available via Cloudflare Browser Rendering or backend Playwright) and (b) react-pdf building the PDF from React components, no browser. We pick **(b)**: deterministic layout from known sections means the document fits *by construction* — the human "render → eyeball → adjust" loop collapses to a one-time template design. No Chromium binary to operate; faster than spinning a browser per download; and the **same renderer serves Tier 2** (a tailored résumé is just a different `WorkProfile` cut). Trade-off accepted: react-pdf is its own flex-like layout engine (not full CSS), which doesn't bite for a conventional 1–2-page résumé; determinism is worth far more than exotic layout. (Verify react-pdf's font-registration + pagination API against its docs at build — no bluffing specifics.)
- **D2 — Renders on the backend, served via the BFF.** The host owns the `WorkProfile` (`candidate_profile`) and runs Node (react-pdf's home). `GET /api/resume.pdf` reads the profile, renders `<ResumeDocument profile={…} />`, and streams `application/pdf` with `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<Name>.pdf"`. The Worker BFF (§24.39 D12) already proxies `/api/*`; confirm binary pass-through is byte-clean. Single source of truth with `/work` (same `WorkProfile` → page and PDF can't drift).
- **D3 — "Fit to 1–2 pages" is a template property, not a per-render check.** Density rules live in the template; react-pdf's page-flow handles the rare long résumé. The owner approves the look *once* (at template design), not per download. A length-guard heuristic is a later refinement if content volume varies wildly.
- **D4 — AI-provenance footer = transparency *and* a traveling conversion vector.** Every generated PDF carries a footer, consistent with the `/work` provenance marker. On the **tailored** PDF it *is* the payload: when the recruiter forwards the file to their hiring manager, the footer says the candidate's *own* agent system auto-tailored it for that exact role — the strongest endorsement, carrying the site URL back. It states the honesty guardrail in-line so "tailored" can't read as "fabricated":
  - *Master:* `◇ Composed by my AI agent system · hire.example.com`
  - *Tailored:* `◇ Auto-tailored for the <Role> role at <Company> by my own AI agent system — the same one running my live job search at hire.example.com. Generated <date>; all content reflects real experience.`
  (`<Company>`/`<Role>` are the visitor's own simulator input — their data, not obfuscated — so naming them is fine.)
- **D5 — Tier-2 honesty guardrail: tailoring = re-emphasis, never fabrication.** The tailored résumé may reorder, select, and re-weight *real* `WorkProfile` content toward the role; it may not invent roles, dates, employers, or claims. Carries `tailor-resume`'s existing constraint up to the full-document level (higher stakes than a few bullets) — enforced in the subagent prompt + a projection that can only draw from the master `WorkProfile`.
- **D6 — Tier-2 cost/abuse envelope re-sized (§24.70).** A full tailored résumé per visitor is more LLM spend than the current bullets. The sandbox per-run pre-charge estimate + daily budget caps (§24.70 D3) are re-sized so the heavier run can't drain budget; values via `getConfig`/wrangler vars (no magic numbers). Tier 2 does not ship until this envelope is set.

**Staging.**
- **9.4b-r1 (Tier 1, this kick-off):** the `ResumeDocument` react-pdf engine + `GET /api/resume.pdf` + the `/work` Download-PDF button (top + bottom) + the master footer (D4). Binary BFF pass-through verified.
- **9.4b-r2 (Tier 2, spec'd when built):** extend `tailor-resume` to a full structured résumé under D5; the simulator-results "Download tailored résumé" + the `/work` cross-sell; the tailored footer (D4); the §24.70 envelope (D6). A short spec precedes the build.

**Definition of done (Tier 1 / 9.4b-r1).** `GET /api/resume.pdf` streams a valid, well-laid-out 1–2-page PDF from the composed `WorkProfile`, fonts embedded, master footer present; the `/work` Download-PDF button (top + bottom) downloads it through the Worker BFF byte-clean; a placeholder profile yields a sensible PDF (no committed personal identifiers — the §24.71 identity split holds); host suite + tsc + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend) green; the renderer is a pure `WorkProfile → Document` component (testable without a network).

**9.4b-r2 build plan (Option A — the full tailored résumé).**
- **Structured capture via a marked output block (built — chosen over an MCP tool).** Today a run accumulates the sandbox's *chat prose* into `simulator_runs.tailored_resume` — the PDF engine needs a structured `WorkProfile`. The sandbox emits one as a fenced ` ```tailored-resume-json ` block at the end of its output (instructed by `buildSimulatorPrompt`); the host extracts + guardrail-validates it in `persistRun`, persisting to `tailored_resume_json` and stripping the fence from the human-facing share text. **Chosen over a container MCP tool** (`emit_tailored_resume`) to avoid the host↔container RPC + a sandbox tool-palette change — the honesty guardrail runs host-side either way, so transport is the only difference, and a malformed/absent block degrades gracefully (no tailored PDF, never a broken run). Upgrade to a structured tool only if emission proves unreliable in practice.
- **Mechanical honesty guardrail (D5, enforced host-side).** The tool validates the emitted profile against the candidate's MASTER `WorkProfile` and **hard-rejects** (returns `isError` → the agent retries within bounds) anything that doesn't trace to it: identity (name/title/links) is taken from the master, not the agent; every `experience` entry must match a master entry by normalized company (+ role) with the master's `period`; `education`/`skills`/`projects` must be subsets of the master's. The agent MAY select, reorder, and re-bullet (rewrite bullet/summary prose toward the role); it may NOT invent an employer, title, degree, skill, or project. The structural skeleton is code-locked; the residual (rephrased prose can still subtly overstate) stays on the persona guardrail — defense-in-depth, with the structural anchor sharply bounding drift. The validator is pure + tested.
- **Persistence:** a migration adds `simulator_runs.tailored_resume_json` (TEXT, nullable); `persistRun` writes the accumulator's validated tailored profile alongside the existing prose blob. Ephemeral — same 30-day TTL as the run.
- **Render endpoint:** `GET /api/simulator/results/<id>/resume.pdf` → load the run's `tailored_resume_json`, render via the Tier-1 `renderResumePdf(tailored, identity, tailoredFooter(company, role, date))`. 404 when the run has no tailored profile (pre-r2 rows, or generation failed). Streamed through the BFF (byte-clean — proven in r1).
- **Tailored footer (D4):** `◇ Auto-tailored for the <role> role at <company> by my own AI agent system — the same one running my live job search at <host>. Generated <date>; all content reflects real experience.`
- **UX:** the simulator results page gains a "Download tailored résumé (PDF)" action; `/work`'s Download-PDF area gains the cross-sell ("Want one aimed at your role? Run the 2-minute simulator →" → `/simulator`). PORTAL §5.3 + §5.6 take these notes at build.
- **Cost envelope (D6) — re-checked, holds with no change.** The structured emit adds ~1–2k output tokens to a run. The existing per-run hard cap (`simulator_max_budget_usd`, $0.10) already bounds it, and the daily/per-IP gate (`checkSimulatorAllowed`) sums REAL `total_cost_cents`, so the heavier run can't drain budget. No magic-number change needed; the caps stay `getConfig` tunables if the cost profile shifts.
- **DoD (9.4b-r2).** A sandbox run emits a tailored `WorkProfile` that (a) passes the guardrail, (b) renders a valid tailored PDF carrying the company/role footer, (c) a fabricated employer is rejected by the tool (tested); the results download + `/work` cross-sell are wired; the guardrail validator is pure + tested; host suite + 3 tsc + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend) green.

**Résumé-quality rework (owner review of the first PDFs, 2026-06-15).** The first downloads exposed real flaws; fixed test-first — the rendered PDF is now INSPECTED with pdfjs (one-page fit, Link annotations, the text layer + glyph, text positions), so "looks right" is a green assertion (`resume-pdf.render.test.ts`), not a vibe. (1) **Brand font:** Inter registered from committed `assets/fonts/*.woff` (resolved via `process.cwd()`) — kills the built-in-Helvetica `◇→Ç` mojibake and matches the site. (2) **One-page density** + clickable contact/project **Link** annotations + fixed header spacing + `wrap=false` rows (no orphaned tags / near-empty page 2). (3) **Grouped skills:** an additive `WorkProfile.skillGroups` (`{category, items}[]`); the PDF + `/work` render groups when present; the flat `skills` stays the guardrail's canonical set (the projector derives it from the groups so they can't drift). (4) **The honesty guardrail now SNAPS bullets (D5 extended).** The first tailored résumé fabricated tech ("PostgreSQL", "60% latency") because bullet PROSE was free-form. Now each tailored bullet snaps to the master bullet it most resembles (≥ half its tokens shared), substituting the master's VERBATIM text; an unmatched (genericized or invented) bullet is dropped; a role emptied by dropping falls back to the master's bullets. Tailoring is now SELECTION + ordering + the summary, never bullet-rewriting — fabrication in bullets is structurally impossible. The simulator prompt is updated to match (copy real bullets, keep metrics; emit grouped skills). (5) The D4 footer drops `◇` and reads cleanly with or without a configured host (`PORTAL_PUBLIC_URL`; omit-when-unset). Deferred: set `PORTAL_PUBLIC_URL` on the box so the footer carries the URL.

**"Watch me apply to your role" reframe + the two-gift results + the layered honesty model (owner-driven, 2026-06-15/16).** Owner testing of the first tailored flow drove a sequence of fixes that reshaped the simulator into the site's gift-first conversion centerpiece. **(1) Reliability (in code, not prompt).** Two bugs made the tailored download flaky: the host extract/strip is now robust to every way the sandbox fences the block (a tagged ` ```tailored-resume-json `, the tag on a ` ```json ` info or label line, or a bare WorkProfile-shaped ` ```json `), and `validateTailoredResume` injects the master identity BEFORE projecting (`projectWorkProfile` requires a name; the instructed emit shape omits identity, so faithful emits were silently rejected and the gift vanished). Emitting the block became a first-class persona deliverable (the agent had been dropping it). **(2) Rebrand.** The `/simulator` route is unchanged (share links persist), but everything a recruiter reads is reframed to "**Watch me apply to your role**" (the balloon-animal arc: name your role → watch it run → keep the gift). The nav splits into a SYSTEM-SHOWCASE group (`Live · Job Pipeline · Architecture · Watch it work`) and a PERSONAL tail (`Experience` — renamed from "Work" — `· Contact`) divided by a subtle rule; the subline is explicit that nothing is submitted. **(3) Two-gift results (one shared `SimResult`; live done-state ≡ share page, by construction).** A run ends in two equal gifts: the tailored résumé (downloadable PDF + preview) and a personalized cold-outreach email (subject + 2-line sneak-peek, expand/collapse). The résumé PROSE is dropped — the PDF is the artifact. The agent activity collapses below ("See how my agents worked"), now flagging the data dependency: consumer subagents dispatched after `research-company` carry a "⤷ research" badge (honest by construction, like "∥ parallel"). PDF preview is an inline `<dialog>` modal on desktop and a direct new-tab open on mobile (mobile browsers won't render PDF-in-iframe); download shows an accurate "Preparing…" state (a JS fetch — the PDF is render-per-request by design, `Cache-Control: no-store`). The live SSE output is stripped of the JSON block client-side (the persisted/shared copy is stripped server-side). **(4) Quality floor — the gift is never a worse subset of the master.** The agent emits skeletons (empty summary, no projects, two skills); the old filter-to-master faithfully rendered that as a downgrade. Now skills/skill-groups are ALWAYS the master's full set, the bio falls back to the master summary when empty/stub, projects fall back when dropped, lookingFor when empty — tailoring ENHANCES (a role-specific summary + bullet selection/ordering), never strips. **(5) Layered honesty (mechanical where possible, honestly framed where not).** Bullets snap to the master verbatim (above). The BIO is mechanically backstopped: it falls back to the master summary if empty/stub OR if it cites a digit absent everywhere in the master résumé (catching the recurring fabricated "60%"). An "Approved figures" allow-list — the real résumé's distinct numbers — is injected host-side into the sandbox candidate fragment (`renderSandboxCandidate`) and bound by the persona + `draft-outreach` (cite only these, in the summary AND the email), curbing fabrication at the source — but a prompt lever, NOT a guarantee (a real dev run still fabricated; note dev pins the model tier to Haiku via the inspector, so the test rig overstates the risk — prod's `opus→sonnet` tier + this lever lower the odds). The cold-outreach EMAIL is free prose with no master to anchor or fall back to, so it is framed honestly as a "**sample draft**" — accurate (the live flow writes a Gmail draft for human review before anything sends) and the right expectation for an illustrative artifact. Net posture: **the résumé is the mechanically-guaranteed document; the email is an honest best-effort sample.** All structural logic is pure + unit-tested (`tailored-resume`, `parse-outreach`, `render-persona`); the two-gift shape + the data-flow badge are e2e-asserted.

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.72 (the two-tier model + D1–D6 + the r2 build plan + the résumé-quality rework + the "Watch me apply" reframe / two-gift results / layered honesty model + staging + DoD); PORTAL §5.6 (the `/work` Download-PDF build note → the react-pdf infra + the Tier-2 cross-sell); PORTAL §5.3 (the simulator reframed to "Watch me apply to your role" + the two-gift results). Memory: [[status_current]].

---

#### 24.73 AI-authorship design language — the ✦ provenance marker + the cast registry (Phase 9.4b, 2026-06-16)

**Problem / framing.** The showcase displays a great deal of agent-authored content — the tailored résumé, the cold-outreach "sample draft", the composed `/work` page, the win-confidence rationale, the interview-kit dossier, the published funnel notes — but marked AI authorship in **five different phrasings with no shared primitive** ("Composed by my agent", "Composed by my AI agent system", "my agent writes these…", "Built by the build-interview-kit subagent…"), one literal `✦` glyph used in exactly one place, and **several surfaces with no marker at all**. This is the owner's TODO #2 ("AI-flavor"): a modern, *consistent* way to signal AI authorship across the whole site. The named subagents are the showcase's unique asset, so the marker should make the **cast** legible rather than reading a generic "AI did this".

- **D1 — Reuse the site's own register, don't import a foreign badge.** The site already has a glyph + mono-caption vocabulary (`◆ public`, `▤ kit`, `⊘ sealed`, `⤷ research`, `∥ parallel`). The AI marker is a first-class member of that family — standardize on the `✦` glyph — **not** a colored sparkle pill from a UI kit, which would introduce a competing visual language (the opposite of consistency).
- **D2 — A dedicated AI color.** New semantic token `--ai` / `--color-ai` (iris/violet `hsl(255 92% 76%)`, AA-safe on the near-black bg), deliberately distinct from `primary` (green) and `accent-cool` (cyan/links). Carries the `✦` glyph, the `AgentRef` names, and AI-scored data viz (the win-confidence bar + `~%`, the `▤` kit cue) — so "an AI did this" actually reads. Body prose stays neutral; the violet marks, it doesn't flood.
- **D3 — Registry of AI *actors*, not just subagents (honesty-load-bearing).** `frontend/src/lib/ai-actors.ts` is the single source of truth: `kind: 'subagent' | 'host' | 'system'`. The six dispatched subagents are `subagent`; the **win-confidence scorer is `host`** — a host-side LLM call (`win-confidence.ts`) that runs *outside* the orchestrator loop, so attributing it to a subagent would be a fabrication; `system` ("my agent system") is the orchestrator, the honest fallback for whole-system output (`/work`, the master résumé). Two corrections fell out of verifying producers: (a) the win-confidence score + rationale is host-side, **not** `pipeline-scribe` (which authors the *published notes*); (b) the **public-view sanitizer is deliberately NOT a registered actor** — it's currently deterministic (regex + name replacement; the Pass 3 LLM review is designed but deferred per §24.10/§24.12), so marking it AI would be the exact dishonesty this guards against. The read-only actors' access badges say "reads, changes nothing" (not "reads only") so they don't read as contradicting a blurb that describes producing output.
- **D4 — Two primitives on one shared mechanism.** `AgentRef` renders an actor's handle as an explainable term (AI-violet, dotted underline) with a tap/click popover (role + blurb + honest access badge); `AgentMark` is the `✦` marker built on `AgentRef`, with inline (footers/cards/ticker) and block-header (kit dossier, rationale) scales. Both share `DisclosureTip` — the portal/dismiss mechanism factored out of `InfoTip` so the ⓘ tip and the cast chip use **one** interaction contract. `AgentRef` is a `<button>`, so it is never nested in another button (the funnel card gets the AI **color** only; the explainable chip lives in the non-button detail panel).
- **D5 — Mark authored *content*, explain pure *transforms*.** `✦` stamps content at its point of display (the résumé, the email, the rationale, the kit). Pure transforms like sanitization are *explained* (the registry + the architecture node) but don't stamp every sanitized field — everything public is sanitized, so that would be noise. Unknown wire names fall back to plain text — never a false chip. Non-interactive surfaces (the `@react-pdf` footer; Inter ships no `✦` dingbat) use the registry's `actorPlainText` ("the tailor-resume agent") — same signal, wording-only, no glyph.

**Definition of done.** New `--ai` token; `ai-actors.ts` registry (9 actors) + `AgentRef` + `AgentMark` + `DisclosureTip` (InfoTip refactored onto it, DOM-stable); every existing marker rewired and every unmarked AI surface marked (`/work`, simulator résumé + email gifts + trace labels, `/kit` masthead + footer, win-confidence rationale → host actor, published note → `pipeline-scribe`, the architecture "cast" roster, win/kit data viz recolored); `tailoredFooter` names the agent; unit tests for the registry + both primitives + the refactored InfoTip; the touched E2E copy/testid assertions reconciled; host + frontend tsc + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend) green; `@visual` baselines regenerated on Linux.

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.73 (the design-language model + D1–D5 + DoD); PORTAL §5.6/§5.3/§5.4/§5.9 + §8 (the marker as a cross-cutting component + the cast registry as source of truth). Feeds TODO #5 (how-it-works / `/about`). Memory: [[status_current]].

#### 24.74 Visitor attribution + the owner-private prod admin (Phase 9.5, 2026-06-16)

**Problem / framing.** Post-launch the owner needs two things the public showcase can't give: (1) **who is visiting and from where** — specifically, did a link the agent put *into* an outbound artifact (a cold-outreach email; a résumé that gets forwarded) get clicked, and does that click attribute to the target company — and (2) a **prod-safe, owner-only place to see it**. This is the owner's TODO #3. The success metric is recruiter→offer ([[project_goal]]), so "the recruiter I emailed at the company I'm targeting clicked through to my live search" is the single highest-value signal the system can surface. Three findings shaped the design: the marquee link lands in a real inbox, so **referrers are useless** (email/PDF clients strip them — only an explicit URL marker survives); the résumé PDF is **host-rendered** (`resume-pdf.ts` + `api.ts`), so minting is a host concern, not a `tailor-resume` tool (that subagent emits bullets, never a link); and the existing `dev-inspector` is the **wrong base** for a prod admin (it's hard-gated to *not exist* in prod via `isDevEnv()` and exists to drive the recruiter-sim + expose unredacted PII — exactly the dev-only surface to exclude).

- **D1 — Per-link opaque tokens, not UTM or referrers.** A short opaque code at `/r/<code>` that resolves server-side to a rich campaign row (company, role, artifact type, `application_id`, created-at) and **302s to `/`** (the landing hero — the owner's call; no per-token landing personalization). Chosen over UTM (leaks the target in the recruiter's URL bar, carries only flat metadata, lost on forward) and referrers (stripped from the highest-value links). Tokens are opaque, metadata-rich, and revocable/expirable. **Referrer is captured passively as a free supplement** (good only for organic web links — a LinkedIn post), never the primary.
- **D2 — Minting is automatic + host-side at outbound-artifact composition — never a subagent tool.** Option (b): mint at the **outreach-email footer** (the orchestrator's `create_gmail_draft` step already substitutes `<portal_url>` in the attribution footer → swap to `<portal_url>/r/<token>`, keyed to the recipient's company the orchestrator already knows — the highest-signal case) and the **master résumé PDF footer** (`api.ts` master render — the "traveling proof" case when the PDF is forwarded). The **simulator tailored PDFs are excluded** (their "company" is visitor-typed → noise, not a real target). No subagent change, no manual minting, no mint form — the token slots into a URL the host is *already writing*.
- **D3 — Capture is backend-side first-party telemetry; the Worker enriches `/r/*`.** A new **private** `visit_telemetry` table mirroring `request_telemetry` (one row per resolved click; never a public surface — `public_audit_trail` stays the curated showcase). Because the DB lives on the backend and the Worker has no DB at the edge (§24.70 D4), the Worker **proxies `/r/*` to the backend** and forwards CF-derived signals (the salted IP hash, `cf-ipcountry`, `referer`) as headers — the same enrichment pattern as the existing `x-cp-client-ip` forwarding in `$.ts`. The backend resolves the token, writes the visit, and returns the 302 to `/` (Worker passes it through).
- **D4 — Privacy: first-party, minimized, disclosed.** Store a **salted hash of the IP** (gives repeat-visit/unique detection without retaining raw IPs), **coarse** geo (country/region from CF, never precise), a UA *class* not the full string; **no third-party cookies, no cross-site tracking**. The **Cloudflare Web Analytics beacon (CLOUDFLARE_PATTERNS §7) is declined** — it's aggregate-only, so it can't join a visit to a token (can't do attribution at all), and it adds a third-party script against the all-first-party posture. A bounded **retention window + auto-prune** (mirrors the telemetry tables), and one honest line on `/about` disclosing the first-party visit log — consistent with the site's whole transparency ethos (feeds TODO #5).
- **D5 — The prod `/admin` is a new Access-gated surface that delivers §17.2 with reconciled auth — NOT a `dev-inspector` extension.** A path (`/admin`) on the main host, gated by **Cloudflare Access + origin-JWT** (reuse the §24.70 D2 `access-jwt` validator, fail-closed) — the **same mechanism that already gates the dev surface**, which supersedes §17.2's original "signed cookie issued via Telegram" auth (that predated the D12 Worker-only + CF Access architecture). It's a **read-only browser**: the attribution feed (per token — company, clicks, coarse geo, repeat visits) joined to `visit_telemetry`, plus §17.2's prod-safe subset (cost/`request_telemetry` rollups, the funnel as the owner sees it, `pnpm health`, contact submissions). **Explicitly excludes** the recruiter-sim, the dev pacing knobs, and any destructive op — those stay on CI/Telegram per the standing "destructive ops off web buttons" lean.
- **D6 — Config tiers (zero magic numbers).** Host-tier (`defaults.json`/`getConfig`): `visit_telemetry_retention_days`, the attribution-link table defaults. Secret-tier (env): the IP-hash salt (`VISIT_IP_HASH_SALT` — never committed, regenerated by bootstrap-vm.sh). Edge-tier (`wrangler.jsonc`, per §24.70 D4): the `/r/*` proxy is structural in `$.ts`, no new var. The token + visit tables are backend (central DB, new migration).

**Decomposition (DD cadence — spec separate; build commits may split/merge as they land).** Commit 1 — spec (this §, no deploy trigger). Commit 2 — the token table + automatic mint at the two D2 injection points + the `/r/*` backend resolver/recorder + the Worker `/r/*` enrichment proxy + `visit_telemetry`. Commit 3 — the `/admin` page (Access + origin-JWT gate, the attribution browser, the §17.2 prod-safe panels) + the `/about` disclosure line.

**Build status (as shipped, 2026-06-16).** Commit 1 spec (`ce5fb98`); Commit 2 mint + `/r` capture (`a037494`); Commit 2b deploy-wiring (`0b5e82d`) — the box never set `portal_public_url` (so minting was dormant) and the host never loads `.env` into `process.env` (so the IP salt had to move to `readEnvFile`): `provision-backend.ts` now seeds `preferences.portal_public_url` from the `PORTAL_PUBLIC_URL` GH env var, the salt is the `VISIT_IP_HASH_SALT` GH secret written to the box `.env`; Commit 3 the `/admin` attribution browser (`0051e72`). **Gating realization:** `adminEnabled()` is OPEN on the dev stack (the surface is already owner-Access-gated) and FAIL-CLOSED → 404 elsewhere until `admin_api_enabled` (a host kill-switch, default off) AND origin-JWT are set behind the prod `/admin*` + `/api/admin/*` Cloudflare Access app (the PRIMARY edge gate, a prod-cutover step). Commit 3 shipped the attribution browser only; the broader §17.2 panels + the `/about` line are a follow-up (below).

**Definition of done.** A minted `/r/<code>` 302s to `/` and writes one `visit_telemetry` row (salted-hash IP, coarse geo, referrer, token); an outreach draft's footer and the master PDF footer carry `/r/<token>` keyed to the company; the simulator tailored PDF does **not**; `/admin` is reachable only behind Access + a valid origin-JWT (a forged/absent assertion fails closed) and shows the attribution feed + the prod-safe panels with **no** sim/dev/destructive surface; retention prune runs; the `/about` disclosure line is live; host + frontend tsc + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend) green; dev-verified on the Access-gated dev surface (owner clicks a minted link, watches the row land + the feed update), prod-valued at cutover.

**Out of scope (recorded).** A first-party **all-visits page-view beacon** (the self-hosted, joinable equivalent of CF analytics, for organic/direct traffic beyond token clicks) — clean fast-follow once the token path proves out. **Manual ad-hoc link minting** (a one-off LinkedIn share link via the admin) — deferred; automatic minting covers the real outbound. **Bot/crawler filtering** beyond a UA-class tag — deferred until the data shows it matters. **The `/admin` §17.2 prod-safe panels** (cost/`request_telemetry` rollups, `pnpm health`, contact submissions, the funnel-as-owner view) + **the `/about` first-party-log disclosure line (PORTAL §5.8)** + **an `/admin` page E2E** — a follow-up after the attribution browser proves out; the prod `/admin*` Cloudflare Access app (Terraform) lands at the prod cutover.

**Deferred admin work — resume plan (Phase 9.5 tail / prod cutover). → RESUMED as §24.138 (Phase A1).** The build of the REST of the `/admin` surface is now spec'd in §24.138 (the tabbed control-center + the include-by-default knob audit); the prod Access gate stays Phase B. The plan below is the original decomposition, kept for its dependency notes (items 1–4) which §24.138 builds on. The attribution browser shipped (`adminEnabled()` gate + `buildAttributionReport()` + `GET /api/admin/attribution` + the `/admin` page). The REST of the `/admin` surface was deferred by deliberate decision (2026-06-16: lower-leverage than the recruiter-facing `/about`; its prod value is cutover-gated anyway; the dev inspector already covers owner-visibility on dev). When resuming, the pieces + their dependency:

1. **Prod gate — DO AT THE PROD CUTOVER (untestable before; prod doesn't exist yet).** A Cloudflare Access application (Terraform, alongside the §24.70 edge posture) scoped to BOTH `/admin*` (the page) AND `/api/admin/*` (the data) on the prod host, owner-email policy — the PRIMARY gate. Then flip `preferences.admin_api_enabled = true`. The backend `adminEnabled()` already fails closed (`isDevEnv() || (admin_api_enabled && originJwtEnabled())`) until both are in place, so prod is safe meanwhile; dev needs none of this (the whole dev surface is owner-Access-gated). Verify at cutover: a non-owner hitting `/admin` or `/api/admin/*` on prod gets the Access challenge; the owner gets through.
2. **§17.2 prod-safe panels — ANYTIME, but most useful at cutover.** Read-only, promoting a subset of the dev inspector to the admin gate, REUSING existing builders: **Health** (`runHealthChecks`, `health.ts` — already on dev via `/api/dev/health`; add `/api/admin/health`); **Cost/telemetry rollup** (`getTelemetry`, `portkey-analytics.ts` + `request_telemetry` aggregates by provider/surface/traffic_class — 24h spend, error rate); **Pause/halt state** (read-only `getPauseState`/`system_modes`); **Contact submissions** — VERIFY a durable store exists first (`contact-relay.ts` may only relay to Telegram, not persist; if not, a tiny `contact_submissions` table is a prerequisite). **EXCLUDE** (heavier / off the prod-safe lean): the unsanitized agent-trace stream, the sanitization raw-vs-sanitized spot-check, the §24.11 audit-trail re-sanitize maintenance, and every writer / dev-knob / destructive op.
3. **`/admin` page E2E** — seed `attribution_link` + `visit_telemetry` in the portal-e2e-server; assert the table renders + the "unavailable" degradation when the endpoint 404s.
4. **All-visits page-view beacon (separate fast-follow, not strictly admin).** `navigator.sendBeacon('/api/_v', …)` from the root layout → a `visit_telemetry` row with `link_code` NULL (organic/direct beyond token clicks) — the self-hosted, joinable equivalent of the declined CF beacon. The `/admin` recent-visits feed already renders null-link rows, so it surfaces for free once written.

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.74 (D1–D6 + decomposition + DoD); §17.2 (the `/admin` auth pattern reconciled to CF Access + origin-JWT superseding the Telegram-cookie issuance, plus the attribution browser added to its panels); PORTAL §5.8 (the `/about` first-party-log disclosure); CLOUDFLARE_PATTERNS §7 (the beacon marked *declined for attribution* — link tracking is first-party); config/defaults.json (`visit_telemetry_retention_days`/`_prune_interval_sec`). Memory: [[status_current]], [[project_goal]]. Builds on §24.70 (edge layer) + §24.72/§24.73 (the artifacts that carry the links).

#### 24.75 The value pitch — the home narrative beat + the story-first `/about` (Phase 9.6, 2026-06-16)

**Problem / framing.** This is the owner's TODO #5. The showcase is **show-rich and tell-poor**: every surface *proves* the system (the live funnel, the trace ticker, the architecture map, the simulator) but nowhere does the site stop and *explain, in plain English, what is actually happening and why it's valuable*. A visitor has to reverse-engineer the gestalt from the evidence — the hero gives a one-line hook (*"I built an AI agent system that runs my job search — and this entire page is it, working live"*) and then drops straight into raw live data. The win condition is recruiter→offer ([[project_goal]]), and a visitor who can't quickly form the mental model bounces before the proof lands. The missing piece is the **pitch** — the value narrative in the candidate's own voice — and the question is *where it lives*. The owner's instinct (recorded across the design discussion) is that this pitch and the long-deferred `/about` page are the same idea wearing two hats; this drill-in confirms that and places both.

The three explainer surfaces, disambiguated: the **pitch** (this §, plain-English value, *everyone*, zero clicks) · `/architecture` (PORTAL §5.5, the live technical *proof*, the skeptic engineer, one click) · `/about` (PORTAL §5.8, the methodology/credibility *depth*, the convinced-but-curious, one click). **Pitch / prove / substantiate.** The gap is the pitch.

- **D1 — The pitch belongs on `/`, zero clicks — not a page.** "What is this?" must be answered on the surface the visitor lands on, in the first scroll, with no click — a dedicated page only reaches the minority who click it, and the confused-on-arrival visitor (the one the pitch is *for*) never gets there. So the load-bearing fix is a new **narrative beat on the home** (PORTAL §5.1 Viewport 1.5), inserted between the hero *hook* (Viewport 1) and the live *proof* (Viewport 2, the funnel strip): a compact, value-first explanation in the candidate's voice — the job-search loop (finds roles → tailors materials → drafts outreach → builds interview prep), framed as *why this is a smart way to run a real search*, ending with *"…and you can watch it happen, or run it on your own open role right now."* An **enhancement** to the home (the five viewports stay; this slots as 1.5), not a rework. Static prose, candidate-POV, no per-visitor data — it never dead-ends the less-interested, who scroll past into the proof.
- **D2 — The long-form story merges with `/about` — one deep page, not two hidden ones.** The pitch beat ends with one quiet, ignorable deepener — **"Read the full story →"** — into a single page, and that page *is* `/about`, restructured **story-first**. Rationale: the "story" and `/about` are the same *kind* of thing — optional depth, one click off the main path, for the more-interested visitor — and they're complementary halves of one read (the **value story** flows naturally into the **substance** a skeptic reads next; story → substance is a normal long-form arc). Merging resolves the owner's standing intuition that these belonged together, and avoids building/maintaining two near-hidden pages. The page opens with the value narrative (the long version of the §5.1 beat) and flows: *the story → how-it-works-in-words (links out to `/architecture`, never re-draws it) → meet-the-cast (the §24.73 `ai-actors` registry) → the trust depth (two-tier vault · anonymization `#anonymization` · visitor privacy · modes · tech choices · fork · honest limitations) → FAQ.*
- **D3 — IA: never the header; two framed doorways.** `/about` is **not** a top-nav item (the PORTAL §8.1 rule: header = the journey, footer/background = depth). It is reached exactly two ways, framed for two motivations: the home beat's **"Read the full story →"** (for the freshly-hooked visitor who wants the narrative) and the footer's **"About"** (PORTAL §8.2, the conventional background slot). A visitor who doesn't care never touches it; a hooked one has a clear deep read waiting. The route **stays `/about`** — the `#anonymization` deep-link (from `/work` + the funnel obfuscation note) and the existing footer/home references already point there, and the URL sits behind framed link text anyway, so a prettier slug would only cost rewiring with no visitor-facing gain.
- **D4 — Cost reuses the existing public telemetry; no new surface, no public-$ decision.** The §5.8 cost section renders from the **already-public** `GET /api/telemetry` — `turn_cost_cents_total` + `sim_cost_cents_total` (the combined headline) and `cache_hit_rate` — the *same* data `/live`'s "Cost & cache" panel already shows. So there is no new backend surface and no "should real spend be public" call to make: the number is *already* on `/live` for anyone to see. The earlier §5.8 wording — "live numbers, **not** estimates" — is **reconciled** to the honest reality: the Agent SDK resolves **estimates** only (exact per-call figures need Portkey's Enterprise admin key, §24.47), so the figures render **labeled `est`**, exactly as on `/live`. "Live estimates, honestly labeled" — the honesty rule wins over the aspiration.
- **D5 — Reuse, don't invent; honesty by construction.** "Meet the cast" is the §24.73 `lib/ai-actors.ts` registry + `AgentRef`/`AgentMark` — no new content model. The beat + page are marketing register (calm, `max-w-prose`, opening warm and deepening into precise). The persona stays generic in the public repo ([[project_generic_persona]]): the beat/page copy is generic framing; the candidate's name/voice are the DB-driven identity already SSR'd into the hero ([[identity_ssr_principle]]). The connective rail's existing `/about` row (PORTAL §8.4) applies. **This drill-in satisfies the §24.74 deferred "`/about` first-party-log disclosure line"** — it lands as the §5.8 "Visitor privacy" section when the page is built.

**Decomposition (DD cadence — spec separate; build commits may split/merge as they land).** Commit 1 — spec (this §, no deploy trigger). Commit 2 — the home pitch beat (PORTAL §5.1 Viewport 1.5) + its "Read the full story →" link. Commit 3 — the story-first `/about` route + content (the narrative, how-it-works-in-words, meet-the-cast via the registry, the trust depth reusing the §5.8 subsections, the cost section over `/api/telemetry`), the footer "About" doorway, the connective rail, E2E + `@visual` baselines.

**Definition of done.** The home carries a plain-English value beat between the hero and the funnel strip (candidate-POV, static prose, nothing faked) ending in "Read the full story →" → `/about`; `/about` exists **story-first** (story → how-it-works-in-words → meet-the-cast → trust depth → FAQ), reached from the beat **and** the footer, **never** the header; `#anonymization` still lands; "meet the cast" reads from the `ai-actors` registry (no bare strings); the cost section renders live `est`-labeled spend + cache rate from the existing `/api/telemetry` (no new endpoint); the §24.74 visitor-privacy disclosure line is live as the §5.8 "Visitor privacy" section; host + frontend tsc + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend) green; E2E covers the beat's deep-link, the `/about` sections, and the footer doorway; `@visual` baselines regenerated for the home (new beat) + `/about`.

**Build status (as shipped, 2026-06-16).** Commit 1 spec (`3793cb5`). Commit 2 the home pitch beat + a minimal-real `/about` (the value narrative + how-it-works-in-words) so the type-safe `Link to="/about"` lands without a 404 (`022203a`). Commit 3 the rest of the `/about` arc — meet-the-cast (the `ai-actors` registry), `#anonymization` + the trust depth (vault, visitor-privacy [the §24.74 disclosure], modes, tech, fork, honest limits), the cost section over `/api/telemetry`, the FAQ — plus the connective-rail `/about` row + a functional/a11y E2E (`about.spec.ts`). An `a11y` find fixed in the build: in-prose links must be underlined at rest (`link-in-text-block`, WCAG 1.4.1), not color-only. **Two pieces deferred (deliberate, recorded):** (1) the **sitewide §8.2 footer** (the second "About" doorway) — a cross-cutting component (socials, a `/privacy` dependency) that changes *every* page's baseline, so it's its own slice (**now built as §24.76**); `/about` was reachable via the beat + its own rail meanwhile; (2) the **`@visual` re-bless** (the home gains the beat → `home.png` changes; new `about.png`) — folded into the pending final `/` style/polish pass (owner-flagged), since the home changes again there and re-blessing now would be throwaway.

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.75 (the gap framing + D1–D5 + decomposition + DoD); PORTAL §5.1 (the Viewport 1.5 pitch beat + the "Read the full story →" deep-link); PORTAL §5.8 (restructured **story-first**, the IA — beat + footer doorways, never the header — and the cost-wording reconciliation to "live estimates, honestly labeled"); PORTAL §8.1/§8.2 reaffirmed (the header/footer IA rule). Satisfies the §24.74 deferred `/about` disclosure line. Memory: [[status_current]], [[project_goal]], [[project_generic_persona]], [[identity_ssr_principle]]. Builds on §24.73 (the cast registry the "meet the cast" section reuses) + the existing `/api/telemetry` (the cost data).

#### 24.76 The sitewide footer — the social/legal strip (Phase 9.6 tail, 2026-06-16)

**Problem / framing.** §24.75 shipped the home pitch beat + the story-first `/about` but **deferred the sitewide footer** — `/about`'s second framed doorway (PORTAL §8.2) — as its own slice: it's cross-cutting (it renders on every page, so it re-blesses every `@visual` baseline) and it carried two open dependencies (a `/privacy` target, and a brand-icon approach). Both are now resolved. This is a small, self-contained slice — one shared `SiteFooter`, a lightweight identity loader at the two register layouts, themed brand icons, and the `/about#privacy` anchor.

- **D1 — One slim social/legal strip, below the rail, on every page.** PORTAL §8.2 is reconciled to what actually ships: the original live `SYSTEM STATUS / deploy-SHA / cache% / $-per-day` metadata footer **stays retired** (redundant with `/live` + the §8.3 indicator — the §24.35 Pass A call); the footer is the **slim, muted social/legal strip** the §8.2 build note already planned, now un-deferred because its blockers cleared. It renders on BOTH register layouts (`(marketing)` / `(ops)`), below the §8.4 connective rail, so `/contact` and unmapped routes (which render no rail) still get a footer. The footer self-determines the rail's register from the path (matching the rail's max-width above it) so it never looks misaligned.
- **D2 — Socials are SSR'd identity, omit-when-null; themed brand icons.** The strip's GitHub/LinkedIn/X/website links come from the SSR'd `Identity` (the §24.71 9.4b-3 identity SSR principle — DB-sourced, each rendered only when non-null; the owner has no X account → no X link, no special-casing). Because the footer lives in the *layout* (which has no loader today), a lightweight `getIdentity` server fn — hitting the same `/api/profile` the §24.71 `getWorkProfile` uses — is added to both layout loaders; the footer reads it. (On `/` and `/work`, which already fetch `getWorkProfile`, that's one extra cheap backend GET during SSR; on ops pages it's the only profile call and serializes a small identity-only payload.) The persona wordmark + the "built with" credit are build-time/static. Icons are **inline simple-icons SVG paths (CC0)**, drawn `fill-current` so they inherit the color tokens — **not** lucide (it dropped brand marks; its generic globe is fine for the website link). Email stays off the footer (scraping; `/contact` owns it).
- **D3 — `/privacy` → `/about#privacy`, no standalone page.** The §8.2 strip's "Privacy" link resolves to the existing `/about` "What this site logs about your visit" disclosure (§5.8, the §24.74 first-party-log line) via a new `#privacy` anchor — honoring the spec's "never link to a 404" deferral condition without building/maintaining a near-empty `/privacy` route or its own baseline. A real standalone privacy page can supersede the anchor later if legal posture warrants.

**Decomposition (DD cadence).** Commit 1 — spec (this §, PORTAL §8.2, no deploy trigger). Commit 2 — the `SiteFooter` + brand icons + the `getIdentity` loader at both layouts + the `/about#privacy` anchor + unit/E2E. Commit 3 — `@visual` re-bless (every desktop + mobile baseline gains the footer).

**Definition of done.** Every page renders the slim footer below the rail (including `/contact`); socials come from SSR'd identity, omit-when-null, as themed `fill-current` brand icons; "About" → `/about`, "Privacy" → `/about#privacy` (the `#privacy` anchor lands); no metadata/status/SHA block; host + frontend tsc + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend) green; E2E asserts the footer + its doorways on a marketing page and an ops page; `@visual` baselines regenerated.

**Spec deltas.** This §24.76; PORTAL §8.2 (reconciled to the shipped strip + the `/privacy`→`/about#privacy` resolution + the retired-metadata note); PORTAL §5.8 (a `#privacy` anchor id on the visitor-privacy section). Memory: [[status_current]]. Builds on §24.75 (`/about`) + §24.71 (the identity SSR the footer reuses).

#### 24.77 The naming/rebrand pass — route renames, nav, funnel→pipeline (Phase 9.6 tail, 2026-06-17)

**Problem / framing.** A coordinated cleanup of accumulated naming drift (owner's post-footer TODO, Group 2). Bundled into ONE slice because the pieces churn the same files, redirects, selectors, and `@visual` baselines — splitting them re-blesses the same surfaces repeatedly. Route renames need redirects (the §24.59 `/momentum`→`/pipeline` precedent: a thin `beforeLoad: throw redirect({to, search, replace:true})` stub) so shared links never 404. `routeTree.gen.ts` is plugin-generated + committed → every route add/rename regenerates it via `vite build`.
