- **D1 — Route renames, each with an old-path redirect stub (carry `search`).** `/(ops)/live`→`/(ops)/dashboard` (label "Live"→"Dashboard"); `/(marketing)/work`→`/(marketing)/experience`; `/(marketing)/simulator.index`→`/(marketing)/watch.index` + `/(marketing)/simulator.results.$id`→`/(marketing)/watch.results.$id`. The renamed file is the real page; a same-named stub at the OLD path redirects to the new (carrying `?app=` / the `$id`). Internal `<Link to=…>` + `Route.useNavigate` targets + `seo({path})` + e2e `goto`s repoint to the new paths. `/pipeline`, `/architecture`, `/kit`, `/about`, `/contact`, `/dev`, `/admin` keep their paths.
- **D2 — Header nav rename + grouping (owner's layout).** `My Job Pipeline` · `Watch it work` | `Dashboard` · `Architecture` | `Experience` `Contact` — the owner's separators (· within a group, | between groups). "Job Pipeline"→"My Job Pipeline" everywhere user-facing (the `/pipeline` H1 + nav + any teaser). "Live"→"Dashboard" wherever the page is named; reword copy that calls it "the live page".
- **D3 — `funnel`→`pipeline`, alias dropped (scope-bounded).** **In:** all user-facing strings; the `public_audit_trail.category` value `'funnel'`→`'pipeline'` (the mirror write in `public-audit.ts` + a one-time migration of existing rows) and the historical-name display aliases (`funnel-curator`→`pipeline-scribe`, `prep-interview`→`build-interview-kit`) — migrate the stored `agent_name`s so the data is natively new and the FE alias-mapping layer is deleted; frontend identifiers (`FunnelBoard`→`PipelineBoard`, `useFunnel`→`usePipeline`, `FunnelCompact`, etc.); the "Simulator result…" share tab title. **Out (internal plumbing, never user-visible; renaming = heavy migration / deployed-state breakage / e2e+baseline churn for zero visitor benefit):** the `funnel_events` / `funnel_curator_output` **table names**; the **`funnel-curator` series id** (the §24.59 precedent — renaming orphans the live recurring series on deployed boxes); the internal `/api/funnel` fetch URL; the **`data-testid="funnel-*"` selectors** (a component↔test contract spanning the Playwright specs + the named `funnel*.png` visual baselines — same churn-for-no-visitor-benefit logic as the URL); the dev-only internal keys (`funnel-data` reset scope, `funnel_curator_cron` knob, the `maybeAdvanceFunnel` / `seedDeterministicFunnel` fixture helpers, the `public_funnel_view` read-model). A comment at each retained-`funnel` boundary states the in/out line so the split is legible, not accidental.
- **D4 — Sitewide wording audit.** Connective-rail copy, each page's header/caption + bottom methodology text, and any stray "simulator"/"live" drift, all reconciled to the new names. Remove "simulator" from user-visible surfaces entirely (the route rename removes the last URL occurrence).

**Decomposition (DD cadence; build commits may split/merge as they land).** Commit 1 — spec (this §; PORTAL route-name + nav references; no deploy trigger). Commit 2 — route renames + redirect stubs + internal Link/nav/seo repoint + `routeTree.gen.ts` regen. Commit 3 — `funnel`→`pipeline` (mirror-write + migration + FE identifiers + drop the alias). Commit 4 — nav grouping + "My Job Pipeline" + the sitewide wording audit. Commit 5 — e2e repoint (paths, nav labels, redirect round-trips) + `@visual` re-bless.

**Definition of done.** Old paths (`/live`, `/work`, `/simulator`, `/simulator/results/<id>`) 301-style redirect to the new ones carrying their params (e2e round-trips each); the nav reads the owner's grouped labels; no user-visible "funnel"/"simulator"/"Job Pipeline"(bare)/"live page" remains (a repo grep over `frontend/src` + the persona/marketing copy is clean except the deliberately-retained internal `funnel` boundaries, each commented); the `public_audit_trail` category/agent_name migration runs forward + the FE alias code is gone (unit-tested: a raw `pipeline` category renders without a mapping layer); host + frontend tsc + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend) green; both unit suites + e2e green; `@visual` baselines regenerated. Box: the migration applies on deploy; the renamed routes resolve and the old ones redirect.

**Spec deltas.** This §24.77; §24.59 (the funnel/pipeline rename — extended: the display alias is now retired, not just display-mapped; the in/out boundary recorded); PORTAL route-name references (`/live`→`/dashboard`, `/work`→`/experience`, `/simulator`→`/watch`) + §8.1 nav grouping. Memory: [[todo_backlog]], [[status_current]].

#### 24.78 Deterministic owner-path subagent traces — the public stream stops depending on the model calling `record_progress` (B1 root-cause fix, 2026-06-17)

**Problem / framing.** The `/dashboard` public activity stream's `subagent_progress` rows depend on the owner-path subagent **voluntarily** calling `record_progress` — a best-effort tool call the model frequently skips. The host already has a *deterministic* trace emitter, `sdkMessageToTraceEvents` (`container/agent-runner/src/providers/claude.ts`), that reliably catches every `Agent`/`Task` dispatch with its `subagent_type` — but it is gated on `emitTrace`, which is `true` only for the sandbox group (`src/container-config.ts:100`: `emitTrace: group.folder === 'career-pilot-sandbox'`) and **off for the owner** by design: the raw SDK stream carries PII (real company names in tool inputs / `input_summary`) that cannot be mirrored to the public portal, so the owner path's *only* sanitized trace channel is the model calling `record_progress` (→ `handleRecordProgress` → 3-pass `sanitizeForPublic` → `insertProgressRow`). Net: when the model skips that call, the public stream goes silent even though the subagent ran.

**This is the durable bug behind B1 (owner's backlog), confirmed by box investigation 2026-06-17:** `job_leads` (scrape-jobs) and `funnel_curator_output` (pipeline-scribe) landed durable rows **every day** including Jun 16–17, while `public_audit_trail.subagent_progress` was **zero** those days — the subagents ran and wrote, only the trace was absent. The model tier was a **red herring**: `agent-turn` telemetry was Haiku on Jun 16 and Sonnet on Jun 17, **both** zero traces, and Jun 11–15 *had* traces also under Haiku. Even "good" days skipped it (Jun 12 / Jun 15 wrote leads with no scrape-jobs trace). §24.44 already observed that "Haiku records progress less eagerly" but only adjusted **frontend rendering** of the sparse stream; §24.68's `cascade-silent` finding correctly **detects** the silence but the emission gap itself was never closed. This § closes it.

- **D1 — A deterministic, PII-safe subagent-lifecycle emit on the owner path.** Extend the always-on (emitTrace-independent) §24.34 loop in `claude.ts` to emit a lifecycle signal on each `Agent`/`Task` `tool_use` dispatch. The signal carries **only** `subagent_type` (already the public-safe label the FE renders — `pipeline-scribe`, `scrape-jobs`, etc.) plus a fixed stage from a **closed host vocab** (`dispatched`; optionally a later `completed` bookend). It **never** reads the tool input / prompt / description — that is where the PII lives. Because the payload is a closed, host-controlled vocab with no free text, it **bypasses `sanitizeForPublic`** (PII-free by construction) and writes synchronously via the same `insertProgressRow` the `record_progress` path uses — `category='subagent_progress'`, `agent_name=<subagent_type>`, no `application_ref` (lifecycle markers carry no attribution; the richer `record_progress` rows still carry it when present).
- **D2 — The host emit is the floor, not a replacement for `record_progress`.** When the subagent *does* call `record_progress`, those richer sanitized stage strings still land and render alongside the lifecycle row. The deterministic emit only **guarantees a non-empty trace whenever a subagent runs**; `record_progress` enriches it when the model bothers. (The owner-path "stream byte-identical / emits nothing" property from §24.20/§24.34 is *deliberately relaxed* here — but only for the closed-vocab lifecycle rows, which are PII-safe; the raw tool stream stays unmirrored.)
- **D3 — Container→host bridge piggybacks the existing turn-telemetry action.** The provider collects the turn's dispatched `subagent_type`s in the always-on §24.34 loop and carries them on `TurnTelemetry.subagent_dispatches`; the poll-loop already fires `career_pilot.record_turn_telemetry` (owner-only by perimeter) on EVERY turn (§24.55), so the host handler `handleRecordTurnTelemetry` emits one `subagent_progress` row per name via `insertProgressRow` inside its existing `trafficClass !== 'sandbox'` gate — written *before* the turn row so the lifecycle lines precede the turn's batch seal (§24.35). No new action, no new outbound kind, no new transport.
- **D4 — Config: a single reversible toggle, no magic numbers.** Gate the owner-path emit behind a `getConfig` flag (`owner_subagent_trace_emit_enabled`, default `true`) so the prior byte-identical-owner behavior is one flip away without a deploy. The stage vocab is a `const`; no other tunables.
- **D5 — Retire the §24.68 model-tier red herrings.** The **ops haiku-floor** in `applyOpsSpawnEnv` (§24.68) was added on the now-disproven premise that Haiku caused the silence; remove it so the ops cascade runs at the configured `dev_model_tier` again (the owner reverted to Haiku to cut cost, and the floor currently silently overrides that revert — keeping ops on Sonnet). **Keep** the `cascade-silent` health finding (it correctly detects the silence) but correct its `detail`/`next_step` copy that blames the "weak-orchestrator-model signature" → point at this emission gap + §24.78.
- **D6 — Canonical subagent name at the single write point (a §24.77 tail closed here).** The subagent *definitions* still carry their original ids (`funnel-curator`, `prep-interview`); §24.77 migrated the historical rows + dropped the FE display alias, but the live write path still emitted the old names — so a *new* `record_progress` row would have rendered the old label the rename eliminated. `insertProgressRow` now applies `publicSubagentName()` (mirroring migration 137's map) so BOTH the model's `record_progress` rows and the §24.78 lifecycle rows store the canonical name — every new row natively new, the dropped FE alias stays unnecessary.

**Decomposition (DD cadence).** Commit 1 — spec (this §; the §24.34/§24.68/§24.44 reconciliation notes; PORTAL §5.2 trace-reliability note; no deploy-relevant code). Commit 2 — build: the provider lifecycle emit (PII-safe) + container→host bridge + host insert (no sanitization) + the `owner_subagent_trace_emit_enabled` flag; remove the ops haiku-floor; correct the `cascade-silent` copy. Tests: provider unit (owner path emits a `subagent_progress` row on an `Agent` dispatch with **no** `record_progress` in the stream; sandbox path unchanged; the emitted payload contains no free text / tool input), host insert-without-sanitization unit, `applyOpsSpawnEnv` test updated (floor gone), health-copy assertion. Host + container `tsc` + prettier.

**Definition of done.**
1. On the owner path, dispatching a subagent (`Agent`/`Task`) writes a `subagent_progress` row to `public_audit_trail` (`agent_name=<subagent_type>`, fixed stage) **deterministically** — unit-tested against a mock SDK stream that never calls `record_progress`.
2. The emitted payload contains only the closed stage vocab + `subagent_type` — asserted; no tool input / prompt can leak (the PII-safety guarantee).
3. The model's `record_progress` rows still land and render alongside (regression test); the lifecycle row + the stage rows coexist.
4. The ops haiku-floor is gone — `applyOpsSpawnEnv` no longer overrides `dev_model_tier`; ops runs at the configured tier (test updated).
5. `cascade-silent` copy no longer blames the model; it points at the emission path + §24.78.
6. Host + container `tsc` + prettier (`--no-semi` frontend untouched) green; both unit suites green.
7. **Box (post-deploy, time-gated):** the next ops cascade (job-scrape ~11:00, pipeline-scribe ~13:30 UTC) produces `subagent_progress` rows for `scrape-jobs` / `pipeline-scribe` in `public_audit_trail` **even on a Haiku run**; the `/dashboard` stream shows the ops subagent lines again.

**Spec deltas.** This §24.78; §24.34 (emission-gate reconciliation — the owner path now has a deterministic floor, not `record_progress`-only); §24.68 (`cascade-silent` copy corrected + ops haiku-floor removed: the model was a red herring); §24.44 (the Haiku-eagerness observation is now root-fixed, not just FE-rendered); PORTAL §5.2 (the trace stream's reliability note). Memory: [[b1-june16-cascade-diagnosis]], [[status_current]], [[todo_backlog]] (B1/B5).

---

#### 24.79 `/pipeline` finishing pass (backlog T1): stat-tile InfoTip trim, context-aware stage names, taller desktop lanes

**Problem / framing.** The post-§24.76 backlog's first finishing touch (T1) is a polish pass on the `/pipeline` board, surfaced by the owner watching it live: (1) three of the four stat tiles carry InfoTips that re-state what their label already says; (2) the five stage names are a single fixed string per component, so the same medium-length names (`Applied`/`Screening`/`Tech`/`Final`/`Offer`) appear both on the destination board (where there's room for more descriptive names) and on the cramped compact strips that link to it (where short codes read better); (3) the desktop board lanes are a fixed `16rem` tall and only fit ~2 cards in view, wasting the vertical room a desktop has. This § specs the polish. It is presentation-only — no endpoint, no read-model, no `data-testid`/visual-baseline-name change (the §24.77 retained-internal "funnel" selector boundary is untouched; this pass moves display strings + heights, not selectors).

- **D1 — Drop the self-evident stat-tile InfoTips; keep only the heuristic one.** `Applications YTD`, `Interviews this month`, and `Offers` are clear from their labels (owner's call) — their InfoTips are removed. `Avg days active` **keeps** its InfoTip: it's a labeled heuristic whose honest caveat (active-only averaging, closed applications excluded, "a heuristic not a benchmark") is load-bearing and *not* derivable from the label — exactly the §24.60 "explain the non-obvious derivation" case. `deriveStatTiles` makes `tip` nullable (`tip: string | null`, `null` for the three); `StatTiles` renders the `<InfoTip>` only when `tip != null`. The derivation copy still lives next to the math (the §24.60 invariant) for the surviving tile.
- **D2 — One stage-label source with short + long variants, chosen by context.** A new `frontend/src/lib/pipeline-stages.ts` exports the canonical left→right `PIPELINE_STAGES: { stage; short; long }[]`, replacing the two drifted per-component literals (`PipelineBoard`'s `COLUMNS`, `PipelineCompact`'s `STAGES`). **`long`** = `Applied` / `Screening` / `Tech interview` / `Final interview` / `Offer` — rendered on the destination `/pipeline` board, which has the column width; the headers' existing `uppercase` class shows them in caps (`TECH INTERVIEW`), while the stored natural-case string keeps the `<section aria-label>` screen-reader name sensible. **`short`** = `APP` / `SCREEN` / `TECH` / `FINAL` / `OFFER` — rendered on the compact strips that *link to* `/pipeline` (the `/dashboard` right-rail panel + the marketing-home strip), where the cells are narrow. The board↔compact split mirrors the owner's "short for the widgets, long when actually on the page" framing.
- **D3 — Taller desktop swimlanes that scale with viewport; tablet + mobile unchanged.** The board lane height moves from a fixed `sm:h-[16rem]` to a viewport-relative clamp at the `lg` breakpoint: `lg:h-[clamp(20rem,calc(100vh-22rem),46rem)]` — floor `20rem` (never below today's usefulness), the `calc` subtracts the page chrome above the board so the lane fills the remaining viewport, cap `46rem` (a very tall monitor doesn't turn one lane into the whole page). The `sm` (tablet) tier keeps `16rem`; mobile stacks at content height (unchanged). The board **and** its loading skeleton read the same shared lane-height class constant, so loading→loaded never resizes (§24.36 Tier-2 / §24.62). The route's page-level loading-skeleton uses the same lane height; the **empty/error** state placeholders deliberately keep their compact `min-h-[16rem]` reserved band — a centered "offline" note floated in a 46rem void reads worse than a tight band, and Tier-2 stability that matters is skeleton↔loaded-board (both tall), not the edge states. The clamp is deterministic at the Playwright visual viewport (fixed height → fixed computed lane height), so the re-blessed baselines stay stable.
- **D4 — Stage icons and dashboard taller-lane scaling are explicitly OUT (owner decided 2026-06-17).** *Stage icons:* skipped — the card glyph vocabulary (`◆ public`, `▤ kit`, the AI win-confidence bar) already carries the per-card semantic load, header icons compete with it, and the owner's "3 interview stages share one icon" idea would actively *blur* `SCREEN`/`TECH`/`FINAL` on the compact strip, where they most need distinguishing; the rail is already ordinal (left→right = progression). *Dashboard scaling:* not adopted — the `/dashboard` trace stream already sits taller (`max-h-[22rem]`) and shares its row with a two-panel right rail, so uniform viewport-scaling there risks an unbalanced grid + a gap beside a stretched trace. Both are recorded here as reversible non-decisions, not gaps.

**Decomposition (DD cadence).** Commit 1 — spec (this §; the PORTAL §5.4 build note; no deploy-relevant code). Commit 2 — build: `pipeline-stages.ts` source + `StatTiles`/`deriveStatTiles` tip-trim + `PipelineBoard`/`PipelineCompact` wiring + the lane-height constant; unit-test updates (`board.test` region names = the long labels + InfoTip count now 1; `panels.test` compact short labels); e2e updates (`funnel.spec` region-name list = long labels; repoint the InfoTip-open assertion from `Applications YTD`→`Avg days active`/heuristic copy); `@visual` re-bless of every page whose pixels move (`/pipeline` board height + the `/dashboard` + home compact strips' short labels). fe-`tsc` + prettier `--no-semi`.

**Definition of done.**
1. `StatTiles` renders exactly **one** `info-tip-trigger` (the `Avg days active` tile); the other three tiles render their label + hint with no tip — unit-asserted.
2. A single `PIPELINE_STAGES` source drives both surfaces: the `/pipeline` board headers + `aria-label`s use the **long** names (`Tech interview`/`Final interview`), the compact strips use the **short** codes (`APP`/`SCREEN`/`TECH`/`FINAL`/`OFFER`) — asserted in the board + panels unit tests.
3. Desktop board lanes are taller than `16rem` and scale with viewport (the `lg` clamp); the loading skeleton matches the same height (no loading→loaded resize); tablet (`16rem`) and mobile (stacked) unchanged.
4. Stage icons and dashboard lane-scaling are absent (recorded out, D4).
5. fe-`tsc` + the three FE unit suites (`board`, `panels`, `use-pipeline`) + prettier green; `funnel.spec` region/InfoTip assertions updated and passing; every touched `@visual` baseline re-blessed; no `data-testid`/baseline-filename churn.

**Spec deltas.** This §24.79; PORTAL §5.4 (T1 build note — the InfoTip trim, the short/long stage-name model, the taller desktop lanes). Memory: [[todo_backlog]] (T1), [[status_current]].

---

#### 24.80 `/architecture` node-health promotions (backlog T2): Web sandbox + Cron sweep get real probes; Anthropic stays structural; Idle clarified

**Problem / framing.** The `/architecture` diagram's honesty core (§24.24 / §24.28) paints a node's health badge **only** from a real probe; a node we don't probe is `structural` and carries *no* claim. The owner asked to promote three `structural` nodes we *can* honestly probe — `trig-web` (Web sandbox), `trig-cron` (Cron sweep), and "maybe" `cont-anthropic` (Anthropic API) — and to audit the `idle` status ("not sure what the intention was there"). The signals already exist host-side: the §24.69 `request_telemetry` 24 h aggregates (incl. the `sandbox` traffic class), the `simulator_enabled` kill switch + `sandbox_daily_global_budget_usd` cap, and the 60 s host sweep loop. The constraint is the honesty core: promote only where there's a *real* signal, never a color we don't probe.

- **D1 — Web sandbox → a `sandbox` probe.** Folds three signals the host owns: the `simulator_enabled` kill switch, the 24 h sandbox spend (`request_telemetry` `traffic_class='sandbox'`, already aggregated by observability), and the `sandbox_daily_global_budget_usd` cap. Status: `down` when disabled (kill switch); `degraded` when 24 h sandbox spend ≥ the daily cap (the owner's "reached our daily spend limit"); `idle` when enabled but **no** sandbox spend in the window (nobody ran it — honest rest); else `healthy`.
- **D2 — Cron sweep → a `sweep` probe (freshness).** The host sweep loop (`src/host-sweep.ts`, 60 s) records an **in-process** last-run timestamp (module-level — the architecture handler runs in the same process; no per-tick DB write); the architecture endpoint exposes its age. Status: `healthy` when the last sweep is within `arch_sweep_stale_sec` (a small multiple of the interval); `down` when the loop has gone silent past it (stalled). **By-design skips stay healthy (owner call):** quiet-hours-deferred work is not a fault — the node modal says the sweep is alive and that some work is intentionally deferred. Deep "missed a job" detection stays the `pnpm health` queue-starvation finding (the runbook), linked from the modal, **not** a public badge color — the badge claims only what it probes (is the loop alive), not what it can't cleanly distinguish (intentional vs. dropped).
- **D3 — Anthropic API stays `structural` (owner call).** Every model call is telemetered as the `portkey` gateway slug — there is **no** Anthropic-specific probe. A badge derived from Portkey's rows would merely mirror the Portkey node and could misattribute a gateway failure to Anthropic, violating the honesty core. The Portkey gateway node already carries the real LLM-path health; Anthropic keeps its no-claim `structural` badge (its modal already frames it "external — every reasoning turn is a call here").
- **D4 — `idle` kept, clarified (owner call).** `idle` stays the honest word for a node with nothing running *right now* (the ops container spins on-demand → `running=0` is the normal resting state, not a fault) and for a provider/sandbox with no rows in the window. **No new status.** The audit's finding: every current `idle` use is honest (cold-load transient, on-demand rest, or a quiet provider) — the gap was *copy*, not semantics. `NodePanel` gains a one-line clarification that idle = at rest, not broken.

**Backend.** Extend `ArchitectureData` (`/api/architecture` → `handleArchitecture`) with `sandbox: { enabled, spend_24h_usd, daily_budget_usd }` (spend reuses the cached observability `sandbox` aggregate) and `sweep: { last_run_age_sec }` (reads the host-sweep last-run stamp; `null` before the first tick). New config `arch_sweep_stale_sec` (default 180 = 3 sweep intervals). Zero magic numbers.

**Frontend.** Two new `ProbeKind`s (`sandbox`, `sweep`) + their `deriveNodeStatus` cases; `trig-web`/`trig-cron` flip from `structural`; `NodePanel` idle-clarifying copy + the cron by-design note. No node geometry change (the diagram viewBox/coordinates are untouched → only badge pixels move).

**Decomposition (DD cadence).** Commit 1 — spec (this § + PORTAL §5.5 build note; no deploy-relevant code). Commit 2 — build: host-sweep last-run stamp + the architecture-endpoint fields + the `arch_sweep_stale_sec` config + the two FE probes + the NodePanel copy; tests (backend `handleArchitecture` shape + the sandbox/sweep derivations; FE `nodes`/`diagram` status cases); architecture `@visual` re-bless. Host + frontend `tsc` + prettier.

**Definition of done.**
1. Web sandbox badge: `down` when `simulator_enabled=false`; `degraded` when 24 h sandbox spend ≥ the cap; `idle` when enabled with no spend; `healthy` otherwise — unit-tested against the derivation.
2. Cron sweep badge: `healthy` when the sweep ran within `arch_sweep_stale_sec`; `down` when stale past it; a by-design quiet-hours skip does **not** degrade it — unit-tested.
3. Anthropic API stays `structural` (no badge claim) — asserted.
4. No new status value; the `idle` copy clarifies the resting state; the audit's "all current idle uses are honest" conclusion recorded.
5. Host + frontend `tsc` + prettier green; backend + FE unit suites green; the architecture `@visual` baselines re-blessed (no node-geometry / baseline-filename churn).

**Spec deltas.** This §24.80; PORTAL §5.5 (T2 build note — the three promotions + the idle-copy clarification). Memory: [[todo_backlog]] (T2), [[status_current]].

#### 24.81 Résumé-download UX parity (backlog T3): one shared, progressively-enhanced download control

**Problem / framing.** Two surfaces hand a visitor a résumé PDF: the `/watch` results page's "Your tailored résumé" gift (`SimResult`'s `ResumeGift`) — a *polished* control that fetches the PDF in JS, shows a "Preparing…" beat for the real server-render delay, honors the server's `Content-Disposition` filename, and falls back to a plain open — and the `/experience` page, whose button is a bare `<a download>` with none of that. The owner asked for three fixes: (1) the Experience button should "work like the fancy one"; (2) neither should resize as its label flips; (3) the results page's download+preview buttons sit awkwardly on desktop (two content-width buttons with dead space to the right).

- **D1 — One shared `ResumeDownload` component** (`frontend/src/components/ResumeDownload.tsx`), the single source of truth for the download interaction (JS fetch → "Preparing…" → filename → `window.open` fallback), used by BOTH the results gift and the Experience page. `ResumeGift` becomes a thin wrapper; the Experience page drops its bare `<a>`.
- **D2 — Progressive enhancement.** The control is a real `<a href={pdfUrl} download>` so it works JS-disabled (the SSR Experience page requires that — PORTAL §10); with JS, the click is hijacked (`preventDefault`) for the Preparing state + server filename. No-JS → native anchor download. (The results page is JS-only anyway, but the one component serves both honestly.)
- **D3 — No resize on state change.** Both labels (`Download résumé (PDF) ↓` / `Preparing…`) stack in one CSS-grid cell (`col-start-1 row-start-1`, the idle one toggled `invisible`), so the control's width is the wider idle label's and never jumps when the state flips. This also fixes the results button, which previously shrank to the shorter "Preparing…".
- **D4 — Results-page desktop layout (owner call).** Download is the primary action and fills the row (`sm:flex-1`); Preview stays compact (`sm:w-auto`) beside it — together they fill the card with a clear primary/secondary hierarchy, no dead space. Mobile stays stacked full-width.
- **D5 — Experience-page parity scope (owner call).** The upgraded download *behavior* only (loading state, filename, fallback, no resize) — **no** preview modal: the button appears twice on the page and the page already *is* the résumé. It keeps its quiet `outline`/`sm` register (so the at-rest `/experience` render is visually unchanged).

**Decomposition (DD cadence).** Commit 1 — spec (this § + the PORTAL §5.3/§5.6 build notes). Commit 2 — build: the shared component + both call sites + a unit test; re-bless the `simulator-results` `@visual` baseline (the desktop layout moved) and `work` only if its at-rest render shifts. fe `tsc` + prettier `--no-semi`.

**Definition of done.**
1. Both surfaces render the download through the shared `ResumeDownload`; the results page additionally shows Preview (desktop modal / mobile new tab), the Experience page does not.
2. The control is an `<a href download>` (no-JS download resolves); with JS the click is hijacked → Preparing state + server `Content-Disposition` filename + `window.open` fallback — unit-tested.
3. The control does not resize toggling idle↔Preparing (the grid-stack) — asserted structurally.
4. Results desktop: Download fills (`sm:flex-1`) + Preview compact; mobile stacked. Experience: download-only, `outline`/`sm`.
5. fe `tsc` + the new unit suite + prettier green; `simulator-results` (+ `work` if it moved) re-blessed; the share-page axe a11y check stays clean.

**Spec deltas.** This §24.81; PORTAL §5.3 (the results gift's shared download control + the desktop fill layout) + §5.6 (the Experience download button gains the shared behavior). Memory: [[todo_backlog]] (T3), [[status_current]].

#### 24.82 Site-header spacing fix (owner nitpick): the wordmark stops crowding the nav

**Problem.** The site header (`SiteHeader`) put the persona wordmark left and the six-link grouped nav right in a `max-w-3xl` box via `justify-between`. The dense nav (≈560 px) nearly filled the box, so the short placeholder name left only ~105 px of slack — but a real, longer `VITE_PERSON_NAME` (the deployed wordmark) ate almost all of it and pressed up against the first link.

- **Fix.** Widen the nav row `max-w-3xl` → `max-w-4xl` and add a guaranteed `gap-6`, with `shrink-0` on both the wordmark and the link cluster so neither compresses the other — restoring ~100 px of breathing room for a real name (and the placeholder reads as comfortably spaced). Bundled a grouping-legibility tweak (owner invited a header look): each cluster is its own `gap-4` flex with the wider gap reserved for the dividers, so the three groups read as groups, not one long row. Mobile (the hamburger disclosure) is unaffected.
- **Active-page indicator (added on owner go-ahead).** The current page's link now brightens to foreground AND carries an `accent-cool` underline (`text-decoration`, so inactive links reserve no space and nothing shifts when the active link changes; `underline-offset-[6px]` reads as a deliberate indicator, not a hyperlink). "You are here" now lands at a glance across the six items. Shared `linkClass` → applies on both the desktop row and the mobile menu.
- **Out (offered, owner's call):** more wordmark identity (a logo mark / accent on the name) — a discretionary visual-identity choice, not pursued.

**DoD.** Header renders with comfortable wordmark↔nav spacing at the real name length (reasoned from the placeholder slack + the +~100 px real-name width); the active link is unmistakable (foreground + accent underline) with zero layout shift; fe `tsc` + prettier green; the header-bearing desktop `@visual` baselines re-blessed across both passes (mobile header = wordmark + hamburger, unaffected; the open mobile menu re-blessed for the active underline). **Spec deltas:** this §24.82; PORTAL §8.1 build note. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

#### 24.83 Long-form reading scaffold (the `/kit` model, shared) + footer-dedup & email-leak hardening (owner T4)

**Problem.** `/kit` earned a real long-form reading model — a document masthead, a sticky scroll-spy TOC (desktop left rail + mobile chip strip pinned under the header), and a mono section-heading register — all locked inside `KitDossier`. `/about` (≈12 sections) and `/experience` (the résumé sections) are the site's other "walls of text" and have **no** navigation aid. Separately, now that the sitewide footer (§24.76) carries the candidate's socials on every page, `/experience` ("Elsewhere") and `/contact` ("Or reach me directly") still hand-roll their own GitHub/LinkedIn lists — duplication — and `/contact` *and* the home "Talk to me" block render the candidate's **email in plain text + `mailto:`**, contradicting the footer's deliberate email exclusion (a `mailto:` invites scraping; the contact form is the relay).

- **D1 — One scaffold, three consumers.** Extract the generic long-form machinery out of `KitDossier` into a shared `LongformDoc` (`frontend/src/components/longform/LongformDoc.tsx`): the desktop sticky rail + mobile chip strip, the IntersectionObserver scroll-spy, the tap-owned-highlight + Chromium-scroll-kill jump workaround, and the optional prev/next stepper — all the §24.65-hardened behavior moves verbatim (the kit unit tests + visual baselines are the regression guard; kit's appearance/behavior do **not** change). The scaffold takes `sections: { id; title; sealed? }[]`, an `idPrefix` (test-id namespace), `navLabel`, and `stepper?`; section *content* stays owned by each consumer and is passed as children — each section element marked `data-longform-section={id}` + `id` + the shared scroll-margin (the scaffold observes by that attribute inside its content ref). `KitDossier` becomes a consumer (`idPrefix="kit"`, `stepper`, `sealed = kind==='withheld'`), keeping its part-headers / redaction / pocket-card rendering as the children.
- **D2 — `/about` adopts it (owner: full reuse).** `/about` gains a document masthead (title + an "ABOUT · long" register tag) and feeds its ~12 prose sections to `LongformDoc` (`idPrefix="about"`). Section model: a typed `{ id, nav, heading }[]` (short `nav` label for the TOC, full `heading` for the section h2) so the TOC reads cleanly while headings stay sentence-length. The existing `#anonymization` + `#privacy` deep-link ids are preserved as section ids. The live `CostSection` telemetry + the registry-backed `CastRoster` ride along unchanged as section content.
- **D3 — `/experience` adopts it (owner: "same exact treatment").** `WorkSections` already renders a variable set of sections (omit-when-empty); it now also computes the ordered list of *present* sections and renders each marked for the scaffold. `/experience` wraps them in `LongformDoc` (`idPrefix="experience"`) below the existing masthead (name + title + provenance + download). The TOC reflects only present sections (a partial profile → a shorter rail, no empty entries).
- **D2/D3 stepper note (owner follow-up):** the mobile ‹ › prev/next steppers that flank the chip strip are part of the kit treatment, so `/about` + `/experience` keep them too (`stepper` on for all three consumers — full reuse). With no sealed sections on the prose docs the steppers simply walk section-to-section (in kit they skip the sealed runs); they're a one-tap "next section" mobile affordance. Desktop is unaffected — the steppers live only in the `lg:hidden` chip strip.
- **D4 — De-dup the socials (owner).** Remove `/experience`'s "Elsewhere" section and `/contact`'s "Or reach me directly" section: the footer (§24.76) is the single sitewide socials strip, so repeating GitHub/LinkedIn per-page is redundant. `/contact` keeps the form (the relay) + the footer; `/experience` keeps the résumé + the footer.
- **D5 — Stop leaking the email (owner; honors the §24.76 footer decision).** The candidate's email must not appear as plain text / `mailto:` on any public surface — the footer already excludes it (scraping), and the contact form is the relay. Removing the `/contact` direct section (D4) drops it there; **also** remove the same `mailto:` email line from the home "Talk to me" block (`index.tsx`) — found during this pass, same leak, same fix (keep the "Contact form →" link). No public surface renders the raw address; email reaches the candidate only through the relay form.

**Test/baseline deltas.** Kit: unchanged (guards the faithful extraction). `/about`: `about.spec.ts` keeps its anchors (heading levels, `#anonymization`, cast, rail) + gains a TOC presence assertion; `about` visual re-blessed (masthead + rail). `/experience`: `work.spec.ts` + `sections.test.tsx` drop the "Elsewhere"/GitHub-link assertions (the link moved to the footer, already asserted by the footer E2E) and gain the TOC; `work` visual re-blessed. `/contact` + home: no baseline change (identity is un-backed in the harness, so the direct section / email never render in those baselines) — the removals are verified by reading + the unit/e2e suites staying green.

**DoD.** One `LongformDoc` is the only implementation of the scroll-spy TOC; `/kit`, `/about`, `/experience` all consume it; kit's unit tests + visual baselines pass unchanged. `/about` + `/experience` render the masthead + a working sticky TOC (desktop rail + mobile chips) with active-section tracking; `#anonymization`/`#privacy` still resolve. No "Elsewhere"/"Or reach me directly" sections remain; no raw email renders on `/contact` or the home page (grep-clean of `mailto:` on public marketing surfaces). fe `tsc` + FE unit + prettier `--no-semi` + the touched e2e specs green; the `about` + `work` `@visual` baselines re-blessed (kit/contact/home unchanged). **Spec deltas:** this §24.83; PORTAL §5.6 / §5.8 / §5.9 + §8.2 build notes. Memory: [[todo_backlog]] (T4), [[status_current]].

#### 24.84 `/dashboard` LLM-spend panel: two equal amounts — 24h spend (left) + cache (right) (owner T5)

**Problem.** The `LlmSpendPanel` (§24.69) leads with the 24h-spend total as a big-number `Metric`, but the cache-hit rate — which lives in this box deliberately, because it's a *cost lever*, not a perf metric — was a small inline `cache NN%` span squeezed to the right of the total. It read as an afterthought next to the headline, when it's the thing that explains *why* the spend is low.

- **Fix.** Promote the cache to a second, equally-styled `Metric` so the box leads with **two big numbers, bookended**: 24h spend on the left (label "24h · est"), cache on the right (label "cache"), each with its own explain-on-tap InfoTip underneath. Same `text-2xl` big-number register for both; the cache `Metric` is right-aligned (`align="right"`) so the pair reads as deliberate left/right bookends. The two sit on one row beside each other (`justify-between`), so the tile adds **no height** — it still fits the four-box stat-row's 196 px floor (the chart + per-class legend below are unchanged). Cache still renders only when `cacheHitRate != null` (a name-only/no-turn state shows just the spend).
- **Mechanism.** Add an optional `align?: 'left' | 'right'` to the shared `Metric` helper (default `left` — every existing call is byte-identical); `right` adds `items-end text-right`. New value testid `llm-cache-rate` mirrors `llm-spend-total`. The InfoTip aria-label follows the visible label ("cache") — the unit assertion updates `About: cache rate` → `About: cache`.

**DoD.** `/dashboard`'s LLM-spend box shows two equal `text-2xl` amounts (spend left, cache right), each with a label + InfoTip beneath; the cache amount matches the spend's size; the panel height is unchanged (stat-row stays uniform). fe `tsc` + FE unit (`spend-by-class.test.tsx` asserts both amounts are `text-2xl` + the renamed InfoTip) + prettier green; the `live` + `mobile-live` `@visual` baselines re-blessed. **Spec deltas:** this §24.84; PORTAL §5.2 build note. Memory: [[todo_backlog]] (T5).

#### 24.85 InfoTip ⓘ glyph centering — a component-wide fix (owner T6)

**Problem.** The `InfoTip` trigger is a 14 px bordered circle whose content is a lowercase text **"i"** (`font-sans text-[9px] leading-none`). A text glyph centered by flexbox is centered on its *advance box*, not its ink: the sans "i" has asymmetric side-bearings (more space on the right), so the ink sits **left of center**, and with `leading-none` the x-height-plus-dot ink isn't vertically centered on the baseline either. Net: the "i" looks off-center (owner saw it most on `/dashboard`'s active-sessions + the mode/agent chips on `/dashboard` + `/architecture`), and the offset varies by surface because it's font-metric-dependent. The InfoTip is the single shared affordance, so this is **one fix, everywhere**.

- **Fix.** Replace the text "i" with a tiny **inline SVG** glyph (a dot + a rounded stem) drawn centered in a symmetric `0 0 16 16` viewBox, `fill="currentColor"` so it still inherits the muted→foreground hover colors. An SVG has no side-bearings or baseline — flex-centering the symmetric viewBox is pixel-perfect and identical on every surface. The bordered-circle look, sizing (`h-3.5 w-3.5`), colors, focus ring, and the whole `DisclosureTip` interaction contract are unchanged; only the glyph rendering changes. Drop the now-unused text classes (`font-sans text-[9px] leading-none`). `align-middle` stays (inline alignment where the tip sits next to text, e.g. the `Metric` label).
- **Scope.** `InfoTip.tsx` only — every consumer (the §24.84 spend/cache amounts, the `Metric` labels, the `ModeBanner` Mode/Agents chips, the turn seal, board/kit tips) inherits the centered glyph. The `AgentRef` cast chip is a different `DisclosureTip` skin (no "i") and is untouched. No testid/behavior change (tests find the trigger by `info-tip-trigger` / `About: «label»`, never by its "i" text).

**DoD.** The ⓘ glyph is optically centered in its circle on every InfoTip, identically across surfaces; the circle/size/colors/interaction are unchanged. fe `tsc` + FE unit (`InfoTip.test.tsx` green unchanged — testid/aria contract intact) + prettier green; the InfoTip-bearing `@visual` baselines re-blessed (the glyph pixels changed). **Spec deltas:** this §24.85; PORTAL §5.2 build note. Memory: [[todo_backlog]] (T6).

---

#### 24.86 InfoTip ⓘ vertical alignment vs adjacent text (owner T6, part 2)

**Problem.** §24.85 centered the glyph *inside* the circle; the owner then flagged the other half of the original nitpick — the ⓘ *circle* sits slightly low relative to the text it follows, and the offset varies by surface ("not always aligned"). Cause: every InfoTip sits next to **uppercase mono** text in a `flex`/`inline-flex items-center` wrapper. `items-center` centers the 14 px circle on the text's **line box**, but uppercase ink occupies the *upper* part of that box (the descender space below the baseline is empty for all-caps), so the circle's center lands ~0.5–1 px below the caps' optical center. The magnitude scales with each context's font-size / line-height (10 px labels, 12 px headers, the 14 px chip value), which is why it reads as inconsistent.

- **Fix.** Nudge the InfoTip trigger up a hair (`-translate-y-px`) so the circle optically centers on the uppercase caps rather than the line box. It's a transform (no layout reflow, tap target moves with the icon), applied once at the shared component → uniform across every surface (all of which are uppercase contexts: the `Metric` labels, the `ModeBanner` Mode/Agents chips, the trace-stream header + turn seal, the `StatTiles`, the kit drawer). The circle, size, colors, and interaction are otherwise unchanged.
- **Note.** A fixed 1 px is the pragmatic single-value correction (the contexts cluster at 10–12 px). It is intentionally simple, not per-context-perfect; verified against the real render so it reads centered, not over-corrected.

**DoD.** The ⓘ circle reads vertically centered with its adjacent uppercase label across `/dashboard` + `/architecture` (chips, stat tiles, headers, seal); fe `tsc` + FE unit + prettier green; the InfoTip-bearing `@visual` baselines re-blessed. **Spec deltas:** this §24.86; folded into the PORTAL §5.2 §24.85 note. Memory: [[todo_backlog]] (T6).

#### 24.87 Home pipeline strip: long stage names on desktop (owner)

**Problem.** §24.79 D2 gave the compact pipeline strip the **short** stage codes (APP/SCREEN/TECH/FINAL/OFFER) because it's a small widget that *links to* the full board (which carries the long names). The owner noted the marketing-home strip (`/`) is wide enough on desktop to carry the **long** names (Applied/Screening/Tech interview/Final interview/Offer) and they read better there.

- **Fix.** Add an opt-in `expandLabels` prop to `PipelineCompact` (default off). When on, the strip renders the **short** code below `lg` and the **long** name at `lg+` (`whitespace-nowrap`, the same caps-via-CSS treatment) — responsive, so a phone still gets the compact code. **Only the home strip opts in**; the `/dashboard` rail (narrow side-panel column) keeps the short codes (the long names don't fit its width). Both still source from the one `~/lib/pipeline-stages`.
- **Scope.** `PipelineCompact.tsx` + the home `/` call site. The `/dashboard` call site and `panels.test` (which renders the default → short) are untouched.

**DoD.** On desktop `/`, the strip shows the long stage names on one line each (no wrap/overflow); on mobile `/` and on `/dashboard` it stays the short codes. fe `tsc` + FE unit (`panels.test` short-label assertions still green — default unchanged) + prettier green; the `home` `@visual` baseline re-blessed (mobile-home unchanged — still short). **Spec deltas:** this §24.87; PORTAL §5.1 build note. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

#### 24.88 Finishing touches batch 2: stale "live"-page wording + Experience polish (owner)

A small grab-bag of owner-flagged polish after the §24.77 rename + recent reworks. Audited the site for stale *page* references and rebranding drift, and two Experience-page UX nits.

- **Wording audit (owner items "live refs" + "rail/caption audit").** The §24.77 pass was thorough — the only **user-facing** stragglers referencing the old `/live` page are two strings: the `/pipeline` connective-rail label **"Watch it live" → "See it run"** (reword + match the other →`/dashboard` deepen labels: "See it work"/"See the system"/"See it run"; "Watch it live" both name-dropped the dead page and risked confusion with the `/watch` "Watch it work" route), and the sanitizer-demo caption **"Sanitized · what /live shows" → "…what the dashboard shows"**. Everything else flagged is internal and intentionally retained per the §24.77 boundary: code comments, the `~/components/live/` directory, and `funnel-*` test-ids / `aria` ids. The **shared-run tab title** ("Simulator result …") is **already** "Run result — «name»" in code (fixed in the §24.77 pass, `watch.results`); what the owner saw is a **stale dev deploy / browser cache**, not live code — no change, just redeploy/hard-refresh. Page headers/captions + bottom methodology text scanned clean of stale terms (no "simulator"/"funnel"/"live-page" in visitor copy).
- **Experience — second download button (owner: "awkward… change or removed?").** The page had a bare duplicate "Download résumé (PDF)" button at the foot. Reframed as a **deliberate end-of-page affordance**: a `border-t` separator above it so it reads as an intentional closing action after the résumé, not a stray repeat. (Kept rather than removed — a download at the foot of a long résumé is a real convenience once the masthead button has scrolled away; the framing was the fix.)
- **Experience — mobile cross-sell wrap (owner).** "Want one aimed at your role? Watch me apply to it →" wrapped with the **arrow orphaned** onto its own line on mobile. Wrap the CTA half in `whitespace-nowrap` ("Watch me apply to it →" stays one unit) so the line breaks **between the question and the CTA** — two clean lines on mobile, one on desktop, never a lone arrow.

**DoD.** No user-facing "Watch it live"/"what /live shows" remains; the Experience foot download reads as intentional (border-t); the cross-sell never orphans its arrow on mobile. fe `tsc` + FE unit + prettier green; the affected `@visual` baselines re-blessed (`funnel` for the rail label, `architecture-sanitizer-modal` for the caption, `work` + `mobile-*` for the Experience tweaks as they move). **Spec deltas:** this §24.88; PORTAL §5.6/§8.4 build notes. Memory: [[todo_backlog]]. **Out of this slice:** the 2 buggy `/dashboard` "pipeline" traces (ops + audit-mirror investigation — handled separately).

#### 24.89 Public trace readability: mirror the agent's summary, not the raw payload (owner)

**Problem.** The owner caught 2 `/dashboard` traces reading `pipeline ◆ [ai-a] status_change APPLIED→REJECTED {"summary":"…","source":"gmail_signal","confidence":95}` — a raw JSON blob, not a sentence. Diagnosed on the dev box: the source `funnel_events` are correct (a status-change with a structured `payload` carrying the agent's human one-liner in `payload.summary`), but `mirrorFunnelEvent` built the public text as **`${kind} ${from}→${to} ${payload}`** — concatenating the *raw* payload JSON. So **every** `status_change` mirror row was unreadable (these 2 were just the first rejections the recruiter-sim produced). A host bug, not bad agent output — the agent's `summary` field was right there.

- **Fix.** A `funnelEventPublicText(row)` helper: when the payload parses as JSON with a non-empty string `summary`, mirror **that** verbatim (it already describes the transition in prose); otherwise fall back to the literal `kind from→to {payload}` form (plain-text / summary-less payloads — unchanged, the existing `{note:…}` tests prove it). Either branch is still sanitized downstream (this only picks the *source* text, never the trust boundary), so the company name in the summary is still obfuscated.
- **Actor is NOT a bug (owner's other guess).** The rows show "pipeline" (not a subagent name) **by design** — `EventSourceLabel` (§24.73) renders the board/category label `pipeline` in the plain register, reserving the explainable chip for named cast members; a pipeline status-change is a board event, not a subagent's authored output. The readability fix alone resolves the "buggy" feel. (The `pipeline-scribe` chip on the good rows comes from the §24.78 subagent-lifecycle path, which sets `agent_name`.)
- **The 2 existing rows.** Deleted on the dev box (owner-authorized) rather than re-mirrored: `resanitizeApplicationAuditTrail` re-stamps `ts=now`, which would resurface 2 old simulated rejections at the top of the live trace as if they just happened — worse than removing the buggy lines. The deployed host fix makes all *future* status-changes readable at their real time; the `funnel_events` truth is untouched (a future policy-triggered re-mirror would regenerate them correctly).

**DoD.** A `status_change` funnel event mirrors its `payload.summary` (sanitized) — no `status_change …{json}` ever reaches the public trace; `{note:…}` / summary-less payloads keep the literal form (existing tests green + a new `public-audit.integration.test` case for the summary path). host `tsc` + host unit + prettier green. **Box:** the 2 buggy rows deleted; the next sim-driven status-change renders as a sentence (time-gated, post-deploy). **Spec deltas:** this §24.89. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

---

#### 24.90 B5: restart-storm investigation (not a runaway) + the health.test FS-race fix

The B1 deep dive flagged "~115 restarts / every 10–40 min" as possible runaway fragility; resolved as two findings.

- **B5a — restart storm is EXPECTED deploy churn, not a runaway (investigation, no code).** Ground truth from the dev box + GitHub: `deploy-backend.yml` is the only restarter, triggered solely by a push to `dev` touching backend paths (`update-tokens`/`bump-version` are upstream-`if`-gated, inert on the fork). GitHub shows ~8 deploys/day; systemd shows **`NRestarts=0`** (no crash-loop / `Restart=always` never fired), no `WatchdogSec`, no `RuntimeMaxSec`, no timer/cron. So restarts ≈ deploys; the "115 / every 10–40 min" was active-push clustering over a **multi-week unrotated** `nanoclaw.log`, not a watcher. The ops session already resumes cleanly across restarts (B1 confirmed the daily cascade fires). **The one real fragility (recommended follow-up, own slice):** the unit is `KillMode=process` and the host `shutdown()` tears down channels/polls but **not its docker containers**, so each stop logs "Unit process (docker) remains running after unit stopped" — the warm ops container orphans until the ceiling-reaper, wasteful churn that also bites a prod deploy. A clean fix is a registered shutdown callback that `docker stop`s the host's running containers before exit — but it touches NanoClaw's container lifecycle (warm-container reconnect semantics), so it wants `NANOCLAW_INTERNALS` review + its own slice, not a bundled change. Deferred, recorded.
- **B5b — health.test parallel-isolation flake FIXED.** `health.test.ts` intermittently failed in the **parallel** full host suite with "Cannot open database because the directory does not exist" (`insertInboundRow → openInboundDb`). Root: every test file's session paths resolve to the **cwd-relative `DATA_DIR/v2-sessions`** (captured once at config load), so vitest's parallel **fork** workers (health + `ops-session` + `dev-inspector`) all contend on that one on-disk base; under Windows FS timing the just-mkdir'd dir intermittently isn't openable. Fix (the backlog's "temp base dir per file"): a **prod-inert env seam** — `sessionsBaseDir()` honors `process.env.NANOCLAW_SESSIONS_DIR` (unset in prod) — and `health.test` points it at a unique `os.tmpdir()/nanoclaw-health-test-<pid>` in `beforeAll` (cleaned in `afterAll`). The shared resource is removed entirely, so the race cannot occur.

**DoD.** B5a: documented as expected churn (not a runaway) with the container-orphan follow-up recorded. B5b: `sessionsBaseDir()` honors the test env seam (prod path unchanged); `health.test` isolates to a per-process temp base; host `tsc` + the **full parallel host suite** green (the actual flake scenario — 1220/1220) + prettier. **Spec deltas:** this §24.90. Memory: [[todo_backlog]], [[b1-june16-cascade-diagnosis]].

---

#### 24.91 Container-orphan-on-restart: stop this install's containers on graceful shutdown (B5a follow-up)

**Problem.** §24.90 B5a surfaced the one real fragility behind the restart churn: the systemd unit is `KillMode=process` and the host `shutdown()` tears down channels/polls/CLI/portal but **not its docker containers**. Containers are owned by dockerd (a separate cgroup), so neither `KillMode=process` *nor* `control-group` reaps them — they outlive the host process that supervises them. On every deploy (~8/day) the warm ops container (and any mid-turn session container) orphans: it keeps polling its session DB and **spending LLM tokens unsupervised** until the *next* boot's `cleanupOrphans()` reaps it (with only `docker stop -t 1` — a near-hard kill of whatever it was mid-flight on).

**NANOCLAW_INTERNALS review (the gate §24.90 deferred — resolved).** The held-back concern was "warm-container reconnect semantics." Confirmed there are none to preserve:

- Resume is via the **persisted SDK continuation** (transcript replay from the container-owned `session_state`, NANOCLAW_INTERNALS "Resume + continuation"), not a kept-warm container — a stopped container's next wake reloads full prior context.
- The "already running → no-op" wake guard is the **in-memory `activeContainers` Map** (`container-runner.ts`), cleared on process start — **not** the DB `container_status`. So a restarted host wakes a fresh container fine; a stale `container_status='running'` never blocks a wake (it already can't today — `cleanupOrphans` kills without touching status, and that's been benign).
- ∴ stopping containers on shutdown is **safe and lossless**: the next inbound re-spawns and continues from the continuation. Graceful shutdown becomes the *symmetric twin* of start-time `cleanupOrphans()`.

- **Fix (stop on shutdown).** Extract the install-scoped stop loop in `container-runtime.ts` into a shared private primitive that both `cleanupOrphans()` (start path — behavior/log unchanged) and a new exported `stopInstallContainers(reason)` (shutdown path) delegate to (the `docker ps --filter label=<install> → docker stop` body, returning the stopped names). Call `stopInstallContainers('host-shutdown')` from `src/index.ts shutdown()` after the registered shutdown callbacks and before exit — stopping the money-spending, DB-mutating containers as the first concrete teardown. Bound each `docker stop` with a timeout (`STOP_TIMEOUT_MS`) so a wedged daemon can't push total shutdown past systemd's `TimeoutStopSec` (default 90s) into a SIGKILL that would re-orphan. Label-scoped (not `activeContainers`-scoped) so it reaps even a container the in-memory map lost track of.
- **Fix (phantom `container_status` — owner-spotted symptom).** The `/dashboard` showed **ACTIVE SESSIONS "3 running"** (rows where `sessions.container_status IN ('running','idle')`) while **CONTAINER POOL "0 / 4"** (`countRunningContainers()` = live `docker ps`) — i.e. 3 phantom rows, 0 real containers. Same root cause: `container_status` is a *cache* flipped to `running` on spawn and back to `stopped` only by the container's `close` handler, which lives **in the host process**. A restart that kills the host mid-turn never fires `close`, so the cache freezes at `running` forever (next-boot `cleanupOrphans` kills the real container but never touches the row). It hasn't "blown up" because the **wake path checks the in-memory map, not this cache**, and delivery's **sweep poll keys on `status='active'`** (the fast active poll keys on `getRunningSessions()`/`container_status`, but the ~60s sweep backstops it on `status`) — so it's a misleading number + wasted active-poll iterations, not dropped work. **Reconcile on startup:** right after `cleanupOrphans()` (which guarantees zero live containers + an empty in-memory map), reset every session's `container_status` to `stopped` (`resetAllContainerStatusesToStopped()` in `db/sessions.ts`). Safe by construction (nothing is truly running at that instant); the DB is honest from boot and both panels agree (`0/0` at rest, matching counts under load). Natural in-process exits still self-update via `close` → `markContainerStopped`; this only catches the host-died-without-close case.
- **Division of labor (kept).** Graceful SIGTERM (deploys, `systemctl restart`) → shutdown-stop reaps containers cleanly *now*; startup reconcile makes the cache honest. Ungraceful exit (SIGKILL/OOM/power loss) → start-time `cleanupOrphans()` + the same startup reconcile are the backstop. `KillMode=process` stays (intentional — it lets `shutdown()` run to completion; `control-group` wouldn't reach the dockerd-owned containers anyway, so it's not the lever).

**DoD.** On graceful shutdown the host stops its install-labeled containers (no "Unit process (docker) remains running after unit stopped" orphan after a deploy); `cleanupOrphans()` behavior/log unchanged (delegates to the shared primitive); each `docker stop` is timeout-bounded. On startup, `container_status` is reconciled to `stopped` for all sessions, so `/dashboard` ACTIVE-SESSIONS-running and CONTAINER-POOL agree (no phantom "running" rows after a restart). host `tsc` + host unit (`container-runtime.test` for `stopInstallContainers`; a `sessions` test for the reconcile) + prettier green. **Box:** post-deploy the stop log shows the ops container stopped on the way out; `/dashboard` reads `0/0` at rest; the next inbound resumes the ops session from its continuation (time-gated — next deploy + any owner turn). **Spec deltas:** this §24.91; the host-shutdown container-stop + startup status reconcile are a tracked **addition** over upstream NanoClaw's shutdown (upstream relies solely on next-start `cleanupOrphans`) — flag for a manual upstream sync. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

---

#### 24.92 Enforce the container concurrency cap (the "/4" the dashboard already shows)

**Problem.** §24.91's owner review surfaced that `container_max_concurrent` (the "/4" in CONTAINER POOL) is **display-only** — read once at `api.ts` for the panel and nowhere else. `getActiveContainerCount()` exists but no spawn path consults it. Every container spawn funnels through `wakeContainer()` (owner inbound via `router.ts`; the public **sandbox/simulator** via the portal adapter → `routeInbound` → `wakeContainer`; plus approvals / agent-to-agent / scheduling / self-mod / container-restart), and none gate on capacity. So under real concurrency — N sim visitors + the cron cascade + owner turns waking at once — the host spawns one container per session with due work, **unbounded**; at 512 MB each on a 4 GB e2-medium, ~6-7 concurrent containers OOMs the box (the documented reason e2-small was rejected). It can't surface at single-user pace — it's the prod-under-load failure the owner intuited.

**Fix.** A capacity gate in `wakeContainer()`, the single chokepoint, placed after the pause gate and after the already-running / already-mid-spawn early-returns (those sessions already hold a slot, so they're never blocked):

- **Count = distinct sessions holding or acquiring a slot** = union of `activeContainers` (running) + `wakePromises` (mid-spawn), deduping the brief handoff window. This — not `activeContainers.size` alone — is what makes the gate **atomic under the single-threaded event loop**: each `wakeContainer` synchronously commits to `wakePromises` before yielding, so N concurrent distinct-session wakes serialize (wake #2 sees #1's reservation) and can't overshoot — the exact sim-visitor-stampede shape.
- When count ≥ cap (`getConfig('container_max_concurrent')`, default 4; **cap ≤ 0 ⇒ unlimited** so a misconfig can't brick spawning), **refuse: log + return `false`** — the identical "transient failure" contract the pause gate uses. Every caller already tolerates it: the inbound row stays pending and host-sweep's next tick re-wakes when a slot frees (≤ `SWEEP_INTERVAL_MS`). Backpressure, not loss.
- Pure predicate `atContainerCapacity(committed, cap)` so the boundary is unit-tested without spawning; cap + count read fresh each wake (operator preference change applies live).

**Why this also makes the dashboard honest.** The "/4 · running/max" gauge now reflects an *enforced* ceiling instead of implying one. No panel change needed (§24.91's reconcile already fixed the data; this makes the cap real).

**Scope / non-goals.** The cap is the **OOM backstop** — a soft ceiling, not abuse defense (that's 9.4a's per-IP + budget caps, which shed sandbox load *before* it reaches this gate) and not a fairness queue (deferred wakes retry via host-sweep, no priority). Sustained cap-hits are a scale-the-box signal, not a retry-latency tuning target. A "deferred-at-capacity" counter on /dashboard is a possible future enhancement; logged-only for now.

**DoD.** `wakeContainer` refuses a NEW spawn (returns false, logs `committed`+`cap`) when the committed count ≥ `container_max_concurrent`, while already-running / mid-spawn / under-capacity wakes pass through; the count is the dedup union of running + in-flight; `cap ≤ 0` disables the limit. host `tsc` + host unit (`atContainerCapacity` truth table + a `wakeContainer` at-capacity-refuses test via a count test-seam) + prettier green. **Box (time-gated):** under the realistic-pace sim, `docker ps` never exceeds the cap; a deferred wake resumes within a sweep tick. **Spec deltas:** this §24.92. Memory: [[todo_backlog]]. **Out of scope:** abuse/budget caps (9.4a), priority queue, retry-latency tuning, the dashboard "deferred" counter.

---

#### 24.93 "Watch it work" cold-start affordance — honest expectation-setting for the ~15s container boot

**Problem.** A "watch it work" run flips to the live 2-pane view the instant `POST /api/simulator` returns an id, but the first `research-company` trace doesn't arrive until the sandbox container has cold-started (~15s: docker spawn → agent-runner boot → SDK init → first dispatch). During that window the Activity pane shows a single static line "starting sandbox session…" and the status dot reads **"running"** — so the showcase's grippiest page opens on ~15s of an unchanging, slightly-jargon line that can read as *stuck*, and the dot claims "running" when nothing is yet. (The same `running && trace=[]` state also covers the rare §24.92 queued-5th-visitor wait, just longer — so fixing it covers both.)

- **Fix.** While `running && trace.length === 0` ("warming"): the status label reads **"starting"** (honest — the container is booting), and the empty-pane line sets the expectation instead of naming an internal artifact ("Spinning up a fresh sandbox — the first step appears shortly…"). The pulsing dot + live elapsed ticker already convey aliveness and stay. Once the first trace lands, the label flips to "running" as before. No fabricated progress steps — we observe none between POST and first trace (the honesty rule); just honest framing + the ticker.

**DoD.** During the cold-start/queue window the Activity status reads "starting" and the pane sets an honest expectation (no "running" claim, no "sandbox session" jargon); the label flips to "running" once a trace arrives; the elapsed ticker + pulse persist. fe `tsc` + FE unit (`SimActivity.test` updated) + prettier `--no-semi` + any affected `@visual` baseline re-blessed. **Spec deltas:** this §24.93; PORTAL §5.3 build note. Memory: [[todo_backlog]]. **Separate (owner-gated, own slice):** abandonment teardown — a closed tab today only drops the SSE subscriber; the sandbox container runs to completion (full spend + a held §24.92 slot) since the fetch-stream transport never reconnects.

---

#### 24.94 "Watch it work" abandonment teardown — stop the run when the visitor leaves

**Problem.** When a visitor closes the tab (or drops) mid-run, the host only removes the SSE subscriber (`removeSimulatorClient` from `req.on('close')`); the fetch-stream transport never reconnects, so the visitor is gone — yet the sandbox container keeps running the turn to completion or the 5-min hard wall, spending the full per-run cost AND holding a §24.92 container slot for a result nobody will see. Safe (budget-bounded; hard-wall + the §24.69 reaper guarantee eventual teardown) but **wasteful — abandoned runs squat the very capacity §24.92 caps.**

- **Fix.** Detect the last-viewer-leaves transition and tear the run down after a short grace. The broadcaster gains a viewer-change callback (`setSimulatorViewerHandler`, mirroring `setSimulatorOutputSink`'s decoupling) fired from `addSimulatorClient`/`removeSimulatorClient` with the run's live viewer count. The simulator registers a handler: a viewer connecting marks the run `hadViewer` and cancels any pending teardown; the last viewer leaving — only for a run that HAD a viewer, so the POST→first-connect gap never trips it — schedules a grace timer (`simulator_abandon_grace_ms`, default 5s). On expiry, if the run is still in-flight, `finalizeSimulatorRun(runId, 'abandoned')`.
- **Discard, don't persist (owner decision).** The `'abandoned'` finalize branch skips `persistRun` entirely — an incomplete run the visitor abandoned would only litter the recent-runs feed, and the transport doesn't reconnect so nobody returns for it. It closes the (empty) stream topic and calls `teardownSimulatorSession`, which `killContainer`s the sandbox (stops spend, frees the slot) and closes the session row. The `complete`/`hard-wall` paths are unchanged (still persist + share).
- **Why a grace at all (no reconnect).** The FE never reconnects to a runId (a refresh starts a NEW run), so abandonment is effectively permanent — the grace only absorbs a transient close+reopen flap or the Worker-proxy hop double-firing, hence 5 s, not minutes. (If FE reconnect is ever added — e.g. mobile backgrounding — the grace becomes the reconnect window and would be retuned.)

**DoD.** A run whose last viewer leaves is finalized `'abandoned'` after the grace: the sandbox session is closed (container killed → slot freed, spend stopped) and **no `simulator_runs` row is written** (discarded). A run that never had a viewer is not torn down (the start→connect gap is safe); a viewer reconnecting within the grace cancels the teardown; `complete`/`hard-wall` runs still persist + share. host `tsc` + host unit (`simulator.test` abandonment cases, fake-timed) + prettier green; `simulator_abandon_grace_ms` in `defaults.json`. **Spec deltas:** this §24.94; PORTAL §5.3 build note. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

---

#### 24.95 "Container pool" explain-on-tap — the on-demand model + the enforced cap

**Problem.** The §24.62 explain-on-tap treatment reached every `/dashboard` stat tile EXCEPT the `Container pool` "running / max" gauge — the one panel making a claim ("0 / 4") with no InfoTip. A visitor (or the owner) reads "0 running" as possibly-broken (the exact confusion that kicked off the §24.91–§24.94 container work), and post-§24.92 the "/ max" is now a real *enforced* ceiling that deserves a word. The sibling `Active sessions` panel already explains the ephemeral model.

- **Fix.** Add the `Metric info=` InfoTip to `ContainerPoolPanel`, copy built from the live `capacity_max` + `memory_mb_each`: "Agent containers spin up on demand and stop when idle — 0 running at rest is normal. Capped at N concurrent (×512 MB) to protect the host; extra runs queue briefly until a slot frees." Falls back to generic wording when the runtime is down/unknown (cap null). One honest tip lands all three ideas: the ephemeral model (0 = healthy), the §24.92 OOM cap, and the graceful queue (§24.92's deferred-wake behavior).

**DoD.** The `Container pool` "running / max" metric carries an explain-on-tap InfoTip stating the on-demand model + the enforced cap; copy uses the live cap/memory when present, generic when down. fe `tsc` + FE unit (`panels.test` asserts the tip) + prettier `--no-semi` + the dashboard `@visual` baseline re-blessed if the glyph shifts layout. **Spec deltas:** this §24.95; PORTAL §10 build note. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

---

#### 24.96 Make the idle-container ceiling configurable (wire the unwired `container_idle_timeout_sec`)

**Problem.** The host sweep reaps a warm-but-idle container when its heartbeat is stale past `ABSOLUTE_CEILING_MS` — a **hardcoded 30 min** in `host-sweep.ts`, and the only thing that ends an idle "hey"/"hey" container (the agent-runner poll-loop runs `while(true)` and never self-exits; idle polling doesn't touch the heartbeat — only per-event turn activity does). Meanwhile `config/defaults.json` already carries `container_idle_timeout_sec: 1800` (= exactly 30 min) that is **defined but never read** — a knob specced and forgotten. The ceiling should be tunable (via `/dev` now, the future `/admin`) so the owner can trade warm-reuse latency against held RAM / §24.92 slots.

- **Fix.** Wire the existing key: `decideStuckAction` gains an `absoluteCeilingMs` arg (defaulting to the `ABSOLUTE_CEILING_MS` constant, so the pure decision stays testable + unchanged when omitted); `enforceRunningContainerSla` reads `getConfig('container_idle_timeout_sec') * 1000` and passes it. The Bash-extension (`max(ceiling, declaredBashMs)`) is preserved, so lowering the ceiling never kills an active long turn (heartbeat is fresh during a turn; the declared-timeout floor still protects a long Bash run). Surface it in the dev inspector's `KNOB_SPECS` (sessions group) so `/dev` — and the future `/admin`, which edits the same `preferences` tier — can set it live.
- **Default stays 30 min.** Kept the proven value: warm reuse spares the ~15 s cold-start on a follow-up and costs no tokens (idle = local DB polling only). 15 min was considered — frees the now-finite slots/RAM ~2× sooner and still covers most bursty owner chats — and is the natural tighten if slot pressure ever shows; now a one-knob change, not a code edit.

**DoD.** The idle ceiling reads `container_idle_timeout_sec` (default 1800 s) instead of a constant; a custom value is honored by `decideStuckAction`; `/dev` lists it as an editable `sessions`-group knob. Applies live (read each sweep tick). host `tsc` + host unit (`host-sweep.test` custom-ceiling case + `dev-inspector` knob membership) + prettier green. **Spec deltas:** this §24.96. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

#### 24.97 Home hero stat line — two count bugs ("3 active" vs "2 APPLIED"; "N actions in 24h · last activity 2d ago")

**Problem.** The `/` hero stat line (PORTAL §5.1 Viewport 1) shows three honest live numbers. Two counted the wrong populations — confirmed against live dev data (stage distribution `rejected=5, applied=2, bookmarked=1`; 24h audit categories `turn=8` only; last non-turn event `~2d ago`):

- **A — "N active job applications" counts pre-application `bookmarked` leads.** `activeApplicationCount` excluded only the *closed* stages (`rejected`/`withdrawn`), so a `bookmarked` lead (a role the agent found but hasn't applied to — a "pre-stage", surfaced off-board per §24.79) counted as an "active job application." But the pipeline strip directly below renders only the five board stages (applied/screening/tech/final/offer), so the bookmarked row is invisible there. Result: headline "3 active" over a strip whose columns sum to 2 → reads as a contradiction, and is dishonest (a bookmark isn't an application).
- **B — "N agent actions in 24h" counts per-turn cost seals, but its line-neighbors don't.** `activity_events_24h` is `COUNT(*)` over *all* `public_audit_trail` rows in 24h, **including `turn` rows**. The same line's "last activity X ago" (`MAX(ts) WHERE category != 'turn'`) and the ticker below both *exclude* turns by design (the per-turn cost seals are the dashboard's story, not the home narrative — §24.71 hero audit). So a day of only owner-chat turns yields "8 agent actions in 24h · last activity 2d ago" — structurally impossible unless the two halves count different populations.

**Fix.**
- **A.** Redefine "active job application" as *in one of the five board stages* — `activeApplicationCount` filters on the canonical `PIPELINE_STAGE_SET` (the single stage source, §24.79) instead of a private negative `CLOSED_STAGES` list. This excludes both closed *and* pre-application `bookmarked`, and makes the headline identically equal to the sum of the strip's five columns, by construction (good coupling: the headline and the strip must agree).
- **B.** Add a non-turn 24h count to the telemetry payload — `agent_actions_24h` = `COUNT(*) … WHERE category != 'turn' AND ts >= cutoff` — and point the hero (live + SSR seed) at it. `activity_events_24h` stays the all-category count for the dashboard's raw `events / 24h` telemetry row (where "events incl. turns" is the honest meaning). Now "agent actions in 24h" and "last activity" describe the same non-turn population: with no non-turn activity in 24h the count is 0 → the segment is omitted (the existing `>0` guard) → the line reads "2 active job applications · last activity 2d ago", coherent.

**DoD.** Headline "active job applications" equals the strip's column sum (bookmarked excluded); "agent actions in 24h" counts only non-turn audit rows and never disagrees with "last activity". `agent_actions_24h` present in `/api/telemetry` (+ the unconfigured fallback) and typed on the FE. host `tsc` + FE `tsc` + host unit (telemetry shape) + FE unit (`hero-stats` bookmarked-excluded case) + prettier green. Box-verifiable on next deploy (the dev DB already carries the bookmarked lead + a turn-only 24h). **Spec deltas:** this §24.97 + PORTAL §5.1 (corrected stat definitions). Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

#### 24.98 Experience page — drop the awkward end-of-page résumé download (reverses §24.88)

**Problem.** `/experience` rendered the résumé-download control twice: once in the masthead (with the `/watch` cross-sell) and again at the very bottom inside a `border-t … pt-8` block. §24.88 framed that second copy as "a deliberate end-of-page closing affordance." In practice it reads as a bare duplicate under a horizontal rule that mimics the per-section `border-t` dividers (`WorkSections`' `Section` wrapper) — so it looks like the start of another, empty section rather than an intentional CTA.

- **Fix.** Remove the bottom block. The masthead download stays — it's above the fold, the first control after the name/title (where a recruiter looks for the PDF), and the page body *is* the résumé, so a second download adds little.
- **Deferred option (recorded, not built).** If post-scroll download reachability proves valuable, the clean home for it is a persistent control anchored to the bottom of the desktop `LongformDoc` rail (an optional `railFooter` slot — additive; `/kit` + `/about` unaffected), NOT a bottom-of-page button. Build on evidence, not speculation.

**DoD.** `/experience` shows exactly one résumé-download affordance (the masthead). FE `tsc` + `work.spec` (unchanged — never asserted the second button) + prettier `--no-semi` green. No `@visual` re-bless (download buttons are absent in the no-profile harness render → `canDownload=false`). **Spec deltas:** this §24.98 (reverses the §24.88 end-of-page-affordance decision). Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

#### 24.99 LongformDoc desktop rail — make the active-section highlight legible

**Problem.** The shared `LongformDoc` TOC (`/experience`, `/kit`, `/about`) marks the current section on its desktop `rail` variant with `text-foreground` (active) vs `text-muted-foreground` (inactive) — a brightness-only delta that's hard to read at the rail's small `font-mono text-[11px]`. The mobile `chip` variant at least carries a colored border (`border-primary/50`); the desktop rail has no hue or positional cue, so "which section am I on" is ambiguous.

- **Fix.** Add an unambiguous "you are here" to the active rail entry: keep `text-foreground`, add `font-medium` (weight contrast), and render a 2px `bg-primary` accent bar pinned to the rail's left edge for the active entry's height (an absolutely-positioned child `<span>`, not a `before:` pseudo — avoids the Tailwind `content-['']` quoting and stays readable). The bar overlays the container's grey `border-l`, the classic docs scroll-spy indicator. Geometry is overlay-only (`relative` button + `absolute` bar) so no layout shift and inactive entries are untouched. The mobile `chip` active style is unchanged.
- **Scope.** `rail` variant only; the bar renders solely for `variant === 'rail' && active`. `/kit` + `/about` inherit it (same component) — desirable, the inconsistency was the bug.

**DoD.** The active desktop-rail entry is obvious at a glance (accent bar + weight + full-strength text) against muted inactive entries; mobile chips unchanged. FE `tsc` + `LongformDoc`/kit unit (testid + active-data contract intact) + `work`/`about`/kit e2e + prettier `--no-semi` green; desktop longform `@visual` baselines re-blessed if the active entry is captured (experience/kit/about). **Spec deltas:** this §24.99. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

#### 24.100 Honor the location preference (schema pin + strong off-location demotion)

**Problem (item #4, confirmed on the box).** The candidate's `location_pref` is honored nowhere it matters. Two causes:

- **Schema drift.** The persona row stores `{"type":["remote","hybrid"],"preferred_cities":["Denver"]}`, but every reader — the lead scorer (`profileFromRow`) and BOTH persona renderers (`renderPersona` + `renderSandboxCandidate`) — reads `{remote, hybrid_cities}`. Neither key exists in the data, so `acceptable_cities` is always `[]` (Denver never credited) and the persona's `## Location` section renders as a bare header with no content (the agent never learns it wants remote/Denver). The field is free-form JSON with no pinned schema, so what the agent wrote diverged from what the code reads.
- **Location is a soft nudge, not a gate.** Even with the schema fixed, location contributes only 0–15 of 100; a non-remote California role from a target company (the ATS scrape pulls *all* of a company's openings) still outranks weaker on-location roles on keyword + recency, and the orchestrator reads the top-20 by `rules_score DESC` (no surfacing floor).

**Decisions (owner, 2026-06-17).** (1) **Strong demotion, keep unknowns.** (2) **Canonical schema = `{type: ('remote'|'hybrid'|'onsite')[], preferred_cities: string[]}`** — the data's shape (more expressive; the agent already writes it). No back-compat (dev-only, no prod): readers drop the legacy `{remote, hybrid_cities}` shape entirely.

**Fix.**
- **Scorer (`lead-rules-score.ts`).** `profileFromRow` reads `type` (→ `remote_ok = type.includes('remote')`) + `preferred_cities` (→ `acceptable_cities`), and sets `location_specified = type.length>0 || preferred_cities.length>0`.
