Pull upstream's `scripts/upgrade-state.ts` + the boot guard in `src/index.ts`; stamp via `pnpm exec tsx scripts/upgrade-state.ts set` on the box before restart. **The single most likely way to leave the box unbootable if forgotten** — call it out in RECOVERY.md.
- **Stage B — OneCLI SDK `^0.5.0` → `2.2.1` + gateway binary.** npm confirms the methods we call still exist (`ensureAgent`, `applyContainerConfig`, `configureManualApproval`, `getContainerConfig`) plus new `OneCLIError`/`OneCLIRequestError` exports — so the break is signature/behavior + the `/v1` API, not removals. Call sites to re-validate: `src/container-runner.ts` (construction + `ensureAgent` + `applyContainerConfig`), `src/modules/approvals/onecli-approvals.ts` (`configureManualApproval`, `ApprovalRequest`/`ManualApprovalHandle` types). Also upgrade the **gateway binary** on the box (the SDK talks `/v1` to a running gateway — likely an `/init-onecli` re-run) and confirm the **two-vault topology survives** (Portkey holds the Anthropic key; OneCLI holds the Portkey key — the container sees neither).
- **Stage C — claude-agent-sdk `^0.2.128` → `^0.3.170` + MCP `^1.29.0`; re-validate §24.115.** Our consumers in `container/agent-runner/src/providers/claude.ts` are **defensive untyped parsers** (`accumulateTurnSignals`, `subagentDispatchesFromMessage`, `sdkResultToTurnTelemetry`, `sdkMessageToTraceEvents`) — they won't *throw* on shape changes but can **silently return wrong data** if the §24.115 "one assistant message per content block under a shared `message.id`" streaming semantics, the `Agent`/`Task` tool-naming, or the `modelUsage`/`cacheReadInputTokens` field names changed at 0.3.x. Also re-check the typed imports (`query`, `HookCallback`, `PreCompactHookInput`) and the `query({ options })` shape (`systemPrompt` preset, `effort`, `permissionMode: 'bypassPermissions'`, the hooks map). Mitigant: `AGENT_SDK_PATTERNS.md` was already authored against 0.3.150, so the 0.3.x surface is partly pre-researched.
- **Stage D — Dockerfile / claude-code reconciliation.** Upstream moved the claude-code/agent-browser/vercel pins out of Dockerfile ARGs into a `cli-tools.json`. Our Dockerfile is heavily customized (OneCLI cache-mounts, pnpm-version lockstep comment). Open decision: adopt `cli-tools.json` (track upstream's structure, easier future bumps) **or** keep our ARG approach and just bump claude-code to the v2.1.17 pin (minimal churn). Lean: keep ARGs for this pass, note the divergence for a future manual upstream sync.
- **Stage E — spec realignment.** `NANOCLAW_INTERNALS.md` §11 deltas and `AGENT_SDK_PATTERNS.md` §0/§1 pin-rationale are written against `^0.2.128`; update them to the 0.3.x reality once Stage C is box-green. Also revisit the locked decision-memory pin note (decision_architecture).

**Decision D4 — 0.3.x is an opportunity, not just a tax (harvest, don't just survive the bump).** The 0.2→0.3 delta (verified against the SDK changelog) fixes several things we've fought with on `^0.2.128`. Stage C should *adopt* these, not merely keep the lights on:
- **`subagent_type` + `task_description` now surface directly on messages (0.3.142); refusals carry `stop_reason:"refusal"` + `stop_details` (0.3.162).** Item-#1 Q2 (render deterministic dispatches as *system lifecycle markers*, not agent speech) was deferred precisely because the discriminator lived in undelivered `details_json` — 0.3.x delivers it on the wire, likely *unblocking* that follow-up (§24.116).
- **Richer task lifecycle: `task_started` / `task_notification` (now with `tool_use_id`) / `task_progress` (cumulative usage).** Item-#1 Q3 wanted a deterministic "completed" beat instead of the flaky prompt-nudged `record_progress` — these are that beat.
- **Fast embedded find/grep in Bash (0.3.162)** — native builds stop registering dedicated Grep/Glob tools by default → a smaller tool schema in the preamble (feeds §24.125 WS2).
- **`startup()` pre-warm (~20× faster first query, 0.2.89)** + long-session memory-growth and MCP child-process cleanup fixes (0.2.51 / 0.2.94) — directly relevant to our cold-spawn + container-lifecycle pain (§24.112 / §24.114).
- **`getContextUsage()` (0.2.86)** — a context-window breakdown by category; the measurement tool for §24.125 WS2.
- **Watch-items (validate, don't blind-adopt):** the `TodoWrite` → `TaskCreate/Update/Get/List` migration (0.3.142) and **non-blocking MCP connections by default** (0.3.142 — slow servers report `status:"pending"`; our career-pilot MCP tools must be present at turn-1, so set `MCP_CONNECTION_NONBLOCKING=0` or mark servers `alwaysLoad:true` if a cold turn races the connect). Re-confirm the `Agent`/`Task` delegation-tool naming our `subagentDispatchesFromMessage` keys on against 0.3.x.

**Open design decisions (resolve at build time, not now):** (1) Stage D's cli-tools.json adopt-vs-keep-ARG; (2) whether `@anthropic-ai/sdk ^0.100.0` is actually needed (we route host LLM through Portkey via `callPortkeyChat`, so likely not — add only if a forced upstream code path references it); (3) exact gateway-upgrade procedure on the box (verify against OneCLI docs at Stage B).

**DoD (the whole-upgrade gate).** Host `pnpm build` + full `vitest` suite green on `^2.2.1` OneCLI types; container `tsc` green on `^0.3.170` SDK types; `./container/build.sh` clean on the bumped claude-code pin; `data/upgrade-state.json` stamped and the box boots past the tripwire; **box-verified** post-deploy — a real owner turn still emits the §24.115 deterministic dispatch trace row AND `/api/telemetry` `cache_hit_rate` stays non-zero (proves the 0.3.x message-shape parsers still read true); a real approval-gated action (e.g. a `create_gmail_draft` dry-run) still round-trips through the upgraded OneCLI `configureManualApproval`. RECOVERY.md gains the tripwire + gateway-upgrade runbook. **Spec deltas:** this §24.124; Stages C/E amend `AGENT_SDK_PATTERNS.md` + `NANOCLAW_INTERNALS.md` when they land. Memory: [[todo_backlog]], [[decision_architecture]].

---

#### 24.125 Fork hygiene + runtime-preamble audit — what the surgical upgrade unlocks (item #9 rider)

**Why now.** D1 commits us to *not* merging upstream wholesale — which removes the only reason to keep upstream artifacts career-pilot never uses (they existed to minimize future merge conflicts). The surgical approach makes deleting them free: nothing re-pulls them. The owner asked to fold this cleanup into the upgrade. Two workstreams with very different risk profiles — keep them separate, and don't oversell a bloat problem that's largely already solved.

**Workstream 1 — repo hygiene (dev-facing, ZERO runtime effect, low risk).** The repo-root `.claude/skills/` carries ~50 skills, most upstream NanoClaw channel/admin skills career-pilot will never run (`add-slack`/`discord`/`whatsapp`/`signal`/`matrix`/`teams`/`webex`/`wechat`/`imessage`/`deltachat`, `add-emacs`, `add-macos-statusbar`, `convert-to-apple-container`, `add-karpathy-llm-wiki`, `add-mnemon`, `add-ollama-*`, `x-integration`, `migrate-from-v1`, `migrate-from-openclaw`, `get-qodo-rules`, `qodo-pr-resolver`…). These load into the **host dev/operator** Claude session (and clutter its `/`-skill list) — **NOT** the container agent — so pruning is pure declutter, reversible via git history. **Keep** the career-pilot-relevant ops skills (`setup`, `init-onecli`, `manage-mounts`, `manage-channels`, `add-gmail-tool`, `add-gcal-tool`, `add-github`, `update-nanoclaw`, `migrate-nanoclaw`, `debug`, `customize`, `claw`, `init-first-agent`, `update-skills`). Decision needed: confirm the prune-list. Also sweep `src/channels/` + `src/providers/` (already minimal — telegram/cli/portal; claude) and any orphaned upstream docs/scripts.

**WS1 done — aggressive clean (2026-06-18, owner-greenlit).** Pruned 32 `.claude/skills/` (kept 17 — the audit added `add-telegram` + the `add-ollama-provider`/`-tool` GLM-test pair to the keep set). Owner chose the aggressive variant, so also removed the unused-channel **setup twins**: `setup/channels/{discord,imessage,signal,slack,teams,whatsapp}.ts`, the six `setup/add-<channel>.sh`, the channel wiring in `setup/auto.ts` (imports, `ChannelChoice` type, dispatch chain, `channelDmLabel`, `askChannelChoice` menu), and the dead `setup/lib/claude-assist.ts` hint entries. **Correction to the "pure declutter / zero coupling" premise above:** the channel skills are NOT fully decoupled — the deploy entry `setup/index.ts` (run by `bootstrap-vm.sh`) and `setup/lib/claude-assist.ts` reference channel machinery, and the channel auth-helpers (`signal-auth.ts`, `whatsapp-auth.ts`, `lib/teams-manifest.ts`) + the `install-*.sh` pullers are coupled into that deploy path. Those deeper helpers were deliberately **left** — removing them is disproportionate risk because `setup/` has **no CI typecheck** (the only net is a throwaway `tsc -p` run, used here; it confirmed **zero dangling `./channels/*` imports**; the 15 remaining `setup/` tsc errors are pre-existing latent issues in never-type-checked code). Verified: `pnpm build` (src) green. Touching `setup/**` fires `deploy-backend` (one provision run, owner-accepted).

**Workstream 2 — runtime-preamble audit (touches the LIVE agent; measure-driven; do AFTER 0.3.x lands).** Honest framing first: the preamble is **already fairly lean** — the §24.49 series trimmed −13.6% warm body, `container/skills/` ships **no** `instructions.md` fragments (zero skill-fragment bloat), the §24.49e gating already drops dead module fragments (`agents`/`self-mod`) from the owner palette, and the persona-vs-skill refactor was deliberately **declined** (the trade inverted — [[decision_persona_skill_refactor]]). So this is not a bloat rescue; it is harvesting the few NEW levers the 0.3.x upgrade exposes:
- adopt 0.3.x fast-embedded-search → stop registering `Grep`/`Glob` as tools (tool-schema shrink, from D4);
- audit `settingSources: ['project','user','local']` → drop `user`/`local` if they pull nothing wanted into the container preamble (the cribsheet documents only `['project']` — the `user`/`local` additions are unexplained drift worth confirming);
- re-baseline with `getContextUsage()` before/after so any change is **measured, not asserted**.

**Sequencing.** Workstream 2 runs *after* §24.124 Stage C is box-green — 0.3.x itself moves the preamble (fast-search + settingSources behavior), so we measure against one stable baseline, not two moving parts. Workstream 1 (repo hygiene) is independent of the dep bump and can land anytime (a natural first, low-risk commit of the whole item-#9 effort).

**DoD.** WS1 — the pruned skill set equals the confirmed career-pilot-relevant set; host `pnpm build` + full suite green (nothing referenced a deleted skill); a `git grep` sweep confirms no dangling references. WS2 — `getContextUsage()` shows the tool-schema / settingSources reduction with **no agent-behavior regression** (a real box turn still dispatches subagents and emits the §24.115 trace), plus a documented before/after token delta. **Spec deltas:** this §24.125; if WS2 changes `settingSources` or tool registration, amend `AGENT_SDK_PATTERNS.md` §gotchas (it currently documents `['project']` only). Memory: [[todo_backlog]], [[decision_persona_skill_refactor]].

---

#### 24.126 Stage A — port the upgrade-marker tripwire (§24.124 Stage A, the build)

**The forced change (nanoclaw 2.1.0 `[BREAKING]`).** Upstream's host refuses to boot unless `data/upgrade-state.json` records that the install reached the running code version through a sanctioned path — guarding against a raw `git pull` + restart landing 2.1.x-expecting code on an un-upgraded environment. We're not wholesale-pulling upstream's `src/index.ts` (ours is heavily customized), so we **port the mechanism** into the fork rather than inherit it.

**Upstream mechanism (read at the `v2.1.17` tag).** `src/upgrade-state.ts` exports `UpgradeState {version, updatedAt, via}`, `markerPath()` (→ `data/upgrade-state.json`), `getCodeVersion()` (package.json), `read/writeUpgradeState()`, and `enforceUpgradeTripwire()` — a boot gate that `process.exit(1)`s when the marker is missing/corrupt **or** `marker.version !== code version`. `src/index.ts` calls it at "step 0.5", before DB init. `scripts/upgrade-state.ts` is the `get`/`set` CLI; the sanctioned callers (setup / a manual stamp) run `set` on success.

**Decisions (the port + our adaptations).**
- **D1 — port faithfully, three artifacts:** `src/upgrade-state.ts` (adapted to our `DATA_DIR`/`log`; package.json resolved via `path.resolve(DATA_DIR,'..','package.json')` so it's cwd-independent), `scripts/upgrade-state.ts` (CLI), and the `enforceUpgradeTripwire()` call as the first line of `main()` in `src/index.ts` (before DB init), matching upstream's step-0.5 placement.
- **D2 — `set` always stamps the CODE version (divergence from upstream's `set [version]`):** the marker version must equal the running code, always — letting a caller pass an arbitrary version is a foot-gun (could stamp a mismatch that then trips or, worse, falsely passes). Our `set [via]` takes only an optional `via` label; version is non-overridable.
- **D3 — box-safety is the ordering (the brick-the-box control):** the box has **no marker today**, so the guarded process would `exit(1)` on first restart unless stamped first. `scripts/bootstrap-vm.sh` stamps the marker (`scripts/upgrade-state.ts set bootstrap`) in a new **step 5.5, immediately before `--step service`** — which is where dist is rebuilt + the service restarts. So the guarded process always boots against a fresh, matching marker. Idempotent (re-stamps every deploy). A pre-stamp bootstrap failure leaves the *old* process running (the service isn't restarted until step 6), so a failed deploy can't brick.
- **D4 — test/dev escape hatch:** `enforceUpgradeTripwire()` no-ops under `VITEST` / `NODE_ENV=test` / `CP_SKIP_UPGRADE_TRIPWIRE=1` so the suite and local `pnpm dev` don't trip (the tripwire protects the box/production boot, not the harness). The pure decision (`evaluateTripwire(state, codeVersion)`) is the unit-test seam.
- **D5 — version stays `2.0.70` for now; the tripwire becomes load-bearing when we bump it.** Stamping the current version trivially passes today; the guard earns its keep at the later stage that bumps `package.json` (the dep-upgrade marker), where any non-bootstrap restart then correctly trips until re-stamped.

**DoD.** `evaluateTripwire` unit test (missing → trip, version-mismatch → trip, match → ok); host `pnpm build` + full suite green; `scripts/upgrade-state.ts get/set` round-trips locally; `bootstrap-vm.sh` stamps at step 5.5 before `--step service`; RECOVERY.md gains an "Upgrade tripwire" runbook. **Box-gated (the deploy is the proof):** the post-push `deploy-backend` provisions, stamps, restarts, and the portal smoke check passes (a brick would fail it). **Spec deltas:** this §24.126; §24.124 Stage A marked built. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

---

#### 24.127 Stage B — OneCLI gateway pre-v1 → v1 migration (codified; mechanism confirmed)

**Problem.** Stage B's `@onecli-sh/sdk` 0.5→2.2.1 bump requires the gateway's `/v1` API — the SDK hardcodes `POST ${ONECLI_URL}/v1/agents` (read the 2.2.1 source; no base-path override). The dev box's gateway, **upgraded in-place** 1.23.0→1.36.0 (a bare `docker compose` image-tag bump per upstream `docs/onecli-upgrades.md`), still serves the **legacy `/agents` API and 404s every `/v1`** — so `ensureAgent` 404'd and all container spawns failed (the 2026-06-18 incident; SDK rolled back to `^0.5.0`, box healthy on 0.5.0↔1.36.0, gateway + vault intact). The documented standard upgrade did NOT activate `/v1`.

**Mechanism — CONFIRMED via local Docker (both gates resolved; isolated from the box).** Two throwaway 1.36.0 gateways settled it:
1. **A clean 1.36.0 serves `/v1`:** `/v1/health→200`, `/v1/agents→401` (auth-gated, exists), `/agents→307` (legacy redirected away), 64 migrations. ⇒ the blocker is **in-place state, not the version**.
2. **The dev-box vault `pg_dump` restored into a clean 1.36.0 → `/v1` works AND the vault is intact:** `/v1/health→200`, `secrets=2`, `app_connections=3`, `migrations=64`. ⇒ **the pgdata/vault is correct; the differentiator is the stale `onecli_app-data` volume** (pre-v1 gateway runtime state), and **NO OAuth re-auth is needed** (the 3 Google connections live in pgdata and survive).

**The migration (codified → `scripts/migrate-onecli-v1.sh`; idempotent, backup-gated).** On the box:
1. **Backup:** `pg_dump` the vault + `cp -a ~/.onecli` → `~/.onecli-backups/` (the incident pattern, already proven).
2. **Reset the gateway runtime state, KEEP the vault:** `docker compose -p onecli stop onecli` → `docker volume rm onecli_app-data` (keep `onecli_pgdata`) → recreate (`docker compose -p onecli up -d`). Fresh app-data ⇒ 1.36.0 serves `/v1`; pgdata (vault) untouched. *(Fallback if state proves entangled: export → clean install → restore the dump — exactly what local test #2 validated end-to-end.)*
3. **Redistribute the regenerated CA:** the app-data reset regenerates the gateway MITM CA (`/app/data/gateway/ca.*`), so refresh `~/.onecli/ca-bundle.pem` + the per-group container mount and re-apply the durable bind-host config (re-run the bootstrap OneCLI durable-config step). **This is the only real-box risk** — a transient credential-injection break until the new CA is redistributed; the backup + verify gate it.
4. **Verify:** `curl 127.0.0.1:10254/v1/health → ok`; vault counts unchanged (2 secrets / 3 connections); a real container spawn + Gmail/Portkey injection 200.
5. **Then re-land the held SDK bump** (`package.json` `@onecli-sh/sdk` → `2.2.1`, already tsc+test-verified) + redeploy → `ensureAgent` hits `/v1/agents` → success.

**DoD.** `scripts/migrate-onecli-v1.sh` codifies steps 1–4 (backup-first, idempotent, verifies `/v1` + vault before declaring success); RECOVERY.md gains the "OneCLI v1 gateway migration" runbook; box-verified — gateway serves `/v1`, SDK 2.2.1 spawns + injects. **Spec deltas:** this §24.127; the §24.124 Stage B SDK 2.2.1 bump re-lands after a green migration. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

##### 24.127.1 EXECUTION POST-MORTEM (2026-06-18) — two premises were WRONG; re-auth WAS required

The migration ran and **succeeded** (gateway → 1.36.0, `/v1/health` 404→200, serving both `/v1` and legacy `/api/*`; SDK 2.2.1 re-landed green). But two load-bearing claims above were false, and the box took a recovered incident. Recording the truth so the prod cutover and any reuse of the script don't repeat it:

1. **The box was on 1.23.0, not 1.36.0.** A bare `docker compose` tag bump never happened — `bootstrap-vm.sh` defaulted `CP_ONECLI_VERSION=1.23.0` and re-pinned the durable `~/.onecli/.env` to it on every deploy (the install-time `setup/onecli.ts` 1.36.0 only applies on a *fresh* install; bootstrap *reuses* the existing gateway). So the Stage-B `/v1` 404 was the **pre-v1 1.23.0 binary**, not stale runtime state. Fixed: `CP_ONECLI_VERSION` default → 1.36.0.

2. **"The stale `onecli_app-data` volume is the sole blocker; NO OAuth re-auth needed" was WRONG.** OneCLI stores the **secret-encryption master key in `onecli_app-data`** (not pgdata; not in env or `~/.onecli`). `docker volume rm onecli_app-data` generated a *new* master key, orphaning every `secrets` row (Portkey, SerpApi) **and** every `app_connections` OAuth token (gmail/calendar/drive) — they survived as rows (UI still shows "connected") but the gateway logs `decrypt failed (wrong key or format mismatch)` and forwards no credential → 401. **The validation gap:** local test #2 counted vault *rows* (2 secrets / 3 connections) but never *decrypted* them. "Connected" in the UI is stored row-state, not token validity (the §24.66 trap). **Recovery required re-registering Portkey/SerpApi + owner OAuth re-auth for all three Google apps.**

**Corrected mechanism (TO VALIDATE before prod cutover / reusing the script):** the destructive `volume rm` was almost certainly **unnecessary**. `/v1` is a property of the **binary** — a plain image-tag bump 1.23→1.36 (steps 1–2 + 4, **skipping** the step-3 `volume rm`) should activate `/v1` *and* preserve the master key, avoiding all re-auth. Confirm on local Docker (pre-v1 gateway + a registered secret → in-place image bump keeping both volumes → assert `/v1` serves AND the secret still decrypts/injects) before reusing `migrate-onecli-v1.sh` or upgrading a prod gateway. The script now carries a "do not re-run as-is" header. If a future migration genuinely *must* reset app-data, **back up the master key / `onecli_app-data` first** (a fresh install + `pg_dump` restore does NOT recover it — the dump's ciphertext is keyed to the lost master key).

---

#### 24.128 Stage C — claude-agent-sdk `0.2.128` → `0.3.x` (grounded against 0.3.181 `.d.ts`)

**Target pins.** `container/agent-runner/package.json`: `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` `^0.2.128` → `^0.3.170` (resolves to the highest published 0.3.x — currently 0.3.181 `latest` / 0.3.183 `next`); `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` `^1.12.1` → `^1.29.0`. This is the **container** (bun) dep tree only — independent of the host's OneCLI SDK (Stage B). The consumers are the defensive untyped parsers in `container/agent-runner/src/providers/claude.ts`; they won't *throw* on shape drift but can **silently return wrong data**. The surfaces below were verified against the actual 0.3.181 type defs (`npm pack`, not the changelog summary).

**CONFIRMED SAFE at 0.3.181 (do NOT churn these):**
- `query({ options })` — every option we pass exists: `effort` (`'low'|'medium'|'high'|'xhigh'|'max'|number`), `settingSources`, `systemPrompt: {type:'preset', preset:'claude_code', append}`, `pathToClaudeCodeExecutable`, `allowDangerouslySkipPermissions` (still gates `permissionMode:'bypassPermissions'`), `additionalDirectories`, `allowedTools`/`disallowedTools`, `mcpServers: Record<string, McpServerConfig>`, `resume`, `cwd`, `env`, `hooks`. Compiles.
- `sdkResultToTurnTelemetry` — `SDKResultMessage` keeps `total_cost_usd`, `duration_ms`, `num_turns`, `modelUsage: Record<string, ModelUsage>`; `ModelUsage` keeps `inputTokens`/`outputTokens`/`cacheReadInputTokens`/`cacheCreationInputTokens`/`costUSD` (gains `webSearchRequests`/`contextWindow`/`maxOutputTokens` — additive, ignored).
- `subagentDispatchesFromMessage` / `accumulateTurnSignals` / `sdkMessageToTraceEvents` — `SDKAssistantMessage.message.content[]` tool_use blocks (`id`/`name`/`input.subagent_type`) + `parent_tool_use_id` stable; `Agent` and `Task` both still matched (the `Task→Agent` rename is an alias, we handle both).
- System messages: `init` (carries `session_id`), `compact_boundary` (`compact_metadata.pre_tokens`), `api_retry` (`subtype:'api_retry'`) — stable.
- Hooks: `HookCallback` is now 3-arg but our 1-arg callbacks remain assignable (TS param-count contravariance); `BaseHookInput` keeps `transcript_path`+`session_id` (PreCompact archiving safe); hook output union keeps `continue?`/`decision?:'approve'|'block'`/`stopReason?` (our `{continue:true}` / `{decision:'block',stopReason}` / `{}` all valid — the `as unknown as ReturnType<HookCallback>` cast on the block case may now be droppable).

**CONFIRMED BREAKING (the one real fix):**
- **`rate_limit_event` is a top-level message `type:'rate_limit_event'` (`SDKRateLimitEvent`), not a `system` subtype.** `claude.ts:778` checks `message.type === 'system' && subtype === 'rate_limit_event'` → never matches at 0.3.x → rate-limit classification silently dead. Fix the discriminator to `message.type === 'rate_limit_event'` (carries `rate_limit_info`). Add/extend a unit test in `claude.test.ts`.

**VERIFY AT BUILD (tsc + the box turn will surface; not pre-confirmable from types):**
- `TodoWrite` → `TaskCreate/Update/Get/List` rename (0.3.142): our `TOOL_ALLOWLIST` lists `TodoWrite` + `Task`/`TaskOutput`/`TaskStop`. Confirm against the `init` message's `tools[]` what the 0.3.x names are, and re-confirm whether `allowedTools` still scopes tool *exposure* under `bypassPermissions` (locked decision says `allowedTools` is ignored for *permission* purposes under bypass — but the MCP-namespace derivation at the call site is load-bearing for tool *reachability*; don't regress it).
- The §24.115 streaming invariant ("one assistant message per content block under a shared `message.id`") is **behavioral** — `SDKPartialAssistantMessage` is in the union; the block-id dedup in `accumulateTurnSignals` must still see the dispatch `tool_use`. Box-only.

**D4 HARVEST — adopt in this stage:**
- **`task_notification` is now rich**: `status:'completed'|'failed'|'stopped'` + `tool_use_id?` + `usage:{total_tokens,tool_uses,duration_ms}`. Our handler already reads `.summary`; **enrich it to emit the deterministic "completed" beat** item-#1 Q3 wanted (instead of the flaky prompt-nudged `record_progress`). Keep it additive to the existing `progress` event.
- **`startup()` is exported** (~20× faster first query) — call once at container process start (`container/agent-runner/src/index.ts`), before the poll loop. Cheap, directly eases §24.112/§24.114 cold-spawn pain.

**D4 HARVEST — note now, defer the build:**
- `SDKAssistantMessage` gains top-level `subagent_type?` + `task_description?` ("the subagent that *produced* this message" — the inverse of our dispatch read, and the on-wire discriminator §24.116 was blocked on). Stage C records this is now available; the actual §24.116 "render dispatches as system lifecycle markers" work stays its own slice.
- `getContextUsage()` (a method on the query handle) → the measurement tool for §24.125 **WS2** (which sequences *after* this stage is box-green).
- `SDKModelRefusalFallbackMessage` / `stop_reason:'refusal'` → refusal handling is a future hardening item, not this bump.

**WATCH-ITEM (mitigate in this stage):**
- **`MCP_CONNECTION_NONBLOCKING=1` is the 0.3.x default** — slow MCP servers report `status:'pending'` in `init` and a turn-1 race could leave career-pilot tools absent. `McpServerConfig.alwaysLoad` is available → set `alwaysLoad:true` on our in-process career-pilot MCP servers (preferred over the global `MCP_CONNECTION_NONBLOCKING=0` so unrelated servers keep the fast path). Verify the `init` message's `mcp_servers[].status` is `connected` (not `pending`) for our servers on a cold box turn.

**Build steps (each its own commit when it lands).** (1) bump the two deps in `container/agent-runner/package.json` + `bun install`; (2) fix `rate_limit_event`; (3) `task_notification` completed-beat + `startup()` pre-warm + MCP `alwaysLoad`; (4) `bun run typecheck` + `bun test` (the `collectTurnSignals` seam + new rate-limit/task-notification cases); (5) rebuild the image (`./container/build.sh`) + redeploy.

**DoD + mandatory box-gate (D3).** Unit seams green (`collectTurnSignals`, the rate-limit + completed-beat cases). Then **box-verified on a real container turn** (the only way to validate the 0.3.x wire shape): the §24.115 deterministic "Dispatched by the orchestrator." trace row still lands; `/api/telemetry` `cache_hit_rate` stays non-zero; `init` shows our MCP servers `connected`; the `container/agent-runner/scripts/sdk-signal-probe.ts` probe passes. **Spec deltas:** this §24.128; Stage E later realigns `AGENT_SDK_PATTERNS.md` §0/§1 + `NANOCLAW_INTERNALS.md` §11 to the 0.3.x reality. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

**Deploy coupling discovered at build (2026-06-18) — Stage C's deploy is NOT standalone; it pulls in the claude-code half of Stage D.** The local code build (version bump + `rate_limit_event` fix extracted to the tested pure `sdkSystemMessageToEvent` + `task_notification` status beat + `alwaysLoad` on the `nanoclaw` server) is validated in isolation (bun typecheck 0; 219 tests). But the **image build / deploy** needs three things resolved first, all surfaced by inspecting the 0.3.181 package:
- **SDK↔CLI version parity is lockstep on the trailing number:** agent-sdk `0.3.181` ↔ claude-code `2.1.181` (and `next` `0.3.183`↔`2.1.183`; our current `0.2.128`↔`2.1.128`). The Dockerfile pins `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION=2.1.128`.
- **0.3.x ships its own platform-native binary** (`optionalDependencies: @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk-<platform>@0.3.181`) and uses it as the *built-in executable* **unless `pathToClaudeCodeExecutable` is set** — and `claude.ts` sets it to `/pnpm/claude`. So the external CLI is still the executor for us → its version matters. Two deploy paths: **Path A (minimal, the §24.124 Stage-D "keep ARGs" lean):** keep `/pnpm/claude` + bump the Dockerfile `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` 2.1.128→2.1.181; **Path B (cleaner):** drop `pathToClaudeCodeExecutable`, let the SDK run its bundled 0.3.181 binary (no external-CLI parity, possibly retire the Dockerfile claude-code install) — bigger, the native binary must be validated in-container first.
- **`@anthropic-ai/sdk` peer is `>=0.93.0`** but `0.81.0` resolves in the agent-runner tree (bun "incorrect peer dependency" warning). typecheck passes regardless (`BetaMessage` structurally compatible), but **add a compatible `@anthropic-ai/sdk` to the agent-runner deps to satisfy the peer** — this resolves §24.124 open-decision #2 (the peer makes it *required*, not optional). The MCP bump to `^1.29.0` is *confirmed correct* — it is exactly the SDK's declared MCP peer.

**Decision: Path B, validated (2026-06-18).** Drop `pathToClaudeCodeExecutable` so the SDK runs its bundled binary; retire the Dockerfile `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` ARG + the `pnpm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` block + its `only-built-dependencies` npmrc line (a repo grep confirmed `claude.ts` was the sole `/pnpm/claude` reference); add `@anthropic-ai/sdk ^0.105.0` to satisfy the peer. **Why Path B over A:** the SDK's bundled binary *is* the version-matched Claude Code CLI (`manifest.json: 2.1.181`), so parity is automatic forever — no Dockerfile pin to hand-maintain, one fewer ~200 MB CLI install, and SDK↔CLI can never drift. **Validated on `node:22-slim` (the image base) via local Docker:** `bun add @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.3.181` materialises the glibc `linux-x64` binary and it runs (`claude --version` → `2.1.181`); `bun.lock` carries all eight platform entries so the container build resolves it. bun typecheck 0 + 219 tests with all Path B changes in. **Box-gate adds one check** (beyond §24.115 trace + cache_hit_rate + MCP `connected`): a real turn must spawn via the bundled binary — confirms nothing at runtime relied on `/pnpm/claude` on PATH (source grep says nothing did; the live turn is the proof). Deploy = push the agent-runner + Dockerfile changes (image rebuild) on go-ahead. **✅ SHIPPED + box-verified 2026-06-19** (see [[todo_backlog]]): deploy green, `/pnpm/claude` gone from the image, `STAGEC_OK` turn spawned via the bundled binary, Portkey/Gmail 200, cache_hit_rate 0.733, and the §24.115 `research-company` dispatch row landed. One deploy gotcha en route: `container/agent-runner` is a host pnpm-workspace member, so the root `pnpm-lock.yaml` had to be regenerated alongside `bun.lock` or `setup.sh`'s `--frozen-lockfile` fails.

#### 24.129 Stage D-remainder — Dockerfile CLI reconciliation (claude-code half done by Path B)

Path B (§24.128) already removed claude-code from the image. What's left of the §24.124 Stage-D scope is the other two global CLIs + the `cli-tools.json` adopt-vs-keep call, verified against the upstream `v2.1.17` tag:

| CLI | ours (before) | upstream v2.1.17 (`cli-tools.json`) | action |
|---|---|---|---|
| `agent-browser` | `latest` (unpinned) | `0.27.1` | **pin → `0.27.1`** (the one real reproducibility gap; the agent drives Chromium through it for `research-company`) |
| `vercel` | `52.2.1` | `52.2.1` | **remove** — unused by career-pilot (we deploy via Cloudflare/wrangler, not Vercel; a repo grep finds zero CLI usage, only a chat-lib comment + a test-fixture company name). Deliberate divergence from upstream. |
| `bun` | `1.3.12` | `1.3.12` | no change (already aligned) |

**`cli-tools.json`: keep ARGs (do NOT adopt) — the §24.124 lean, confirmed.** Upstream now `COPY cli-tools.json install-cli-tools.sh` + runs the script (so a skill can json-merge a tool). Our Dockerfile is heavily customised (the BuildKit cache-mounts, the pnpm-version lockstep, and now the claude-code removal), and after pruning vercel we are down to a single pinned global CLI (`agent-browser`) — a whole json+script indirection for one install is churn with no payoff. Keep the ARG. This is a tracked upstream deviation (alongside the persona host-fragments, the pnpm-workspace, the ported upgrade tripwire); Stage E records it in `NANOCLAW_INTERNALS.md` §11.

**DoD.** Dockerfile: `agent-browser` pinned to `0.27.1`; `VERCEL_VERSION` ARG + the `pnpm install -g vercel` block removed (the `only-built-dependencies[]=agent-browser` npmrc line preserved, folded into the agent-browser RUN); bun/pnpm untouched. Box-gate: the image rebuilds, a real turn spawns + replies, and — the only Stage-D-specific check — a `research-company` turn that browses still works (agent-browser present at the pinned version). Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

#### 24.130 WS2 — runtime-preamble audit (measured; lean confirmed; levers are no-ops)

§24.125 WS2 deferred until Stage C was box-green so the preamble is measured against one stable 0.3.x baseline. Done — and the honest outcome is **"measured, already lean, the harvest levers don't move it."**

**Measurement tool (the durable deliverable).** Wired `logContextUsage` in `claude.ts`: on the owner-path `init`, fire the 0.3.x `Query.getContextUsage()` control method (fire-and-forget, never throws into the stream) and log one compact line — the preamble's token cost by category. Ongoing observability for preamble bloat, which is otherwise invisible.

**Finding (box reading).** Static preamble ≈ **57.7K**: System prompt 6.3K + **System tools 18.0K** + MCP tools 11.5K + Custom agents 0.8K + **Memory/persona 21.1K** (live `Messages` 30K + `Autocompact buffer` 33K + free space are not preamble). This **confirms §24.49's ~55K** — the "already lean" claim holds. (An earlier `cache_creation_tokens` reading of ~80K had alarmed me; it folds in ~30K of live messages — not a preamble regression.)

**Levers, evaluated against the data:**
- **Drop unused builtins** (NotebookEdit / TeamCreate / TeamDelete / SendMessage — career-pilot edits no notebooks and uses orchestrator→subagent dispatch, never agent teams). Added to `SDK_DISALLOWED_TOOLS` + removed from the allowlist. **Box-measured: `System tools` stayed 18.0K — NO preamble shrink.** Those tools aren't part of the counted base (or `disallowedTools` doesn't reduce the binary's fixed builtin schema). **Kept as hygiene** (consistent with the existing Cron/Plan/Worktree disallow entries — tools that don't fit our model), explicitly NOT as a shrink.
- **Grep/Glob drop:** moot — the System-tools base is fixed by the Claude Code binary; not reducible from our `allowedTools`/`disallowedTools`.
- **`settingSources` narrow (`user`/`local` → `project`):** **rejected.** The `user` source (`/home/node/.claude` = the mounted `.claude-shared`) carries load-bearing config (git CA, env, hooks per `/init-onecli`); narrowing would break credential/env wiring for a marginal gain.
- **Persona (21K):** the largest static chunk, but intentional — the persona-refactor was DECLINED (§24.125); it's the behavioral contract.

**Conclusion.** No low-risk preamble shrink is available: persona is intentional, the System-tools base is fixed, MCP tools are load-bearing, `settingSources` is load-bearing. WS2's value is the durable `getContextUsage` measurement (catches future bloat) + the confirmation the preamble is lean. **Box-verified:** the measurement logs each owner turn (`total=88K/165K (53%)`); `WS2_OK`/`WS2_TRIM_OK` turns spawn + reply normally. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

#### 24.131 Stage E — spec realignment to the 0.3.x reality (doc-only)

The §24.124 Stage E. No code; realign the spec artifacts that still described the pre-bump world, and record the upgrade's deliberate deviations. **Done (2026-06-19):**
- **`AGENT_SDK_PATTERNS.md`** §0 version caveat + §1 pin block rewritten to `^0.3.170` (0.3.181): the doc was written against 0.3.150, so it is now *aligned*; added the lockfile-sync note, the absorbed 0.2→0.3 deltas, and the Path B bundled-binary executor. Integration note + install line repinned.
- **`NANOCLAW_INTERNALS.md`** §11: the Δ2 discovery block + the Decisions-made Δ2 marked RESOLVED (bumped, the Phase-5+ revisit done); **new Δ13** consolidates the v2.1.17 deviations (pnpm-workspace + the dual-lockfile rule, the ported upgrade tripwire, Path B + claude-code removal, cli-tools.json-not-adopted + vercel prune, the OneCLI `/v1` migration).
- **root `CLAUDE.md`** cribsheet-reading-order note + the locked-decisions Agent-runtime row → `^0.3.170` + Path B.
- **`decision_architecture`** memory pin note → `^0.3.170` + the lockfile gotcha.
- **`STRATEGY.md`** current-pin cross-refs (§5 subagents, §6 MCP integration, §24.49 cache-lever table) repinned; the historical upgrade sections (§24.124–§24.130) + the §24.115 bug description keep their `^0.2.x` references (correct as record).

**Remaining v2.1.17:** only **WS3** (repo-wide deadfile sweep, full-delete-only) — see §24.132. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

#### 24.132 WS3 — repo-wide deadfile sweep (full-delete-only)

The last v2.1.17 item, saved for after the upgrade. Sweep the **whole** tree for nanoclaw-origin files with **zero career-pilot use** and delete them *entirely* — never partial-strip a file career-pilot actually uses (the WS1 §24.125 discipline). The test applied per candidate: a **functional** reference in a surviving file (import / `exec` / CI `uses:` / served index-link) = used → leave; a **prose/comment** mention in a spec/doc = updated by this commit, not a blocker.

**Owner-greenlit the full purge (2026-06-19)** — the surgical-cherry-pick upgrade model (`NANOCLAW_INTERNALS.md` §11 Δ13) means we no longer git-merge upstream `main`, so the "deleting upstream subsystems makes merges conflict-heavy" cost is moot.

**Deleted (44 files):**
- **5 clean orphans (0 refs anywhere):** `docs/upstream-readme-ja.md`, `docs/upstream-readme-zh.md`, `docs/upstream-claude.md`, `docs/BRANCH-FORK-MAINTENANCE.md` (an upstream-*maintainer* fork-sync doc), `scripts/seed-discord.ts` (Discord is V2-deferred).
- **12 unused channel installers:** `setup/install-{discord,gchat,imessage,linear,matrix,resend,signal-cli,slack,teams,webex,whatsapp,whatsapp-cloud}.sh`. Driven only by `migrate-v2.sh:386`'s dynamic `setup/install-${ch}.sh` with a graceful "no install script" branch — no edit needed.
- **9 upstream release/badge files:** `repo-tokens/` (7) + `.github/workflows/{update-tokens,bump-version}.yml`. Both workflows trigger on `push: main`; career-pilot ships `master`/`dev`, so they never fire (`BRANCH-FORK-MAINTENANCE.md` itself said "remove in forks").
- **5 upstream governance/example:** `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `CONTRIBUTORS.md`, `RELEASING.md` (all describe *NanoClaw*, not career-pilot), `config-examples/mount-allowlist.json` (dir emptied).
- **13 v1→v2 migration subsystem:** `migrate-v2.sh`, `migrate-v2-reset.sh`, `setup/migrate-v2/*.ts` (11). career-pilot is a clean v2 rebuild — never ran it. Zero functional TS importers (distinct from the `/migrate-nanoclaw` *skill* — which migrates between NanoClaw *versions*, not v1 data, and was itself retired in the third pass below).

**The one kept-file edit (a conscious micro-exception):** deleting the migration subsystem left a stale 2-line *comment* at `setup/auto.ts:432` (`// v1 → v2 migration is handled by 'bash migrate-v2.sh'…`) — removed. A false comment, not used logic; this is the sole edit to a surviving file.

**Excluded — functional coupling (WS1-style traps):** `setup/{signal-auth,whatsapp-auth}.ts` + `setup/lib/teams-manifest.ts` (live `STEPS` registry / `setup/index.ts`); `setup/install-{node,docker,claude,telegram,github}.sh` (used by `claude-assist.ts`/`container.ts`/`setup.sh`); all **ollama/GLM** files (`add-ollama-*` skills, `docs/ollama.md`, `config/glm-4.7-flash.modelfile` — woven into `rank-leads.ts`/`poll-loop.ts`/`container-config.ts`, in active use); `docs/upstream-readme.md` (root `README.md` MIT-attribution link); `docs/{SPEC,REQUIREMENTS,APPLE-CONTAINER-NETWORKING}.md` + `launchd/` (served by the `docs/README.md` index). Out of scope (career-pilot's own, not nanoclaw): the empty stub `src/channels/portal/sse-output.ts`.

**DoD.** `git grep` confirms no surviving file *functionally* references a deleted path; host `pnpm build` green; a throwaway `tsc -p setup` shows no NEW dangling-import errors beyond WS1's 15 pre-existing latent ones (`setup/` has no CI typecheck); the deletions fire `deploy-backend` (one provision run, owner-accepted) and it goes green on the box. **Spec deltas:** this §24.132; the §"ADD from upstream" inventory gets a WS3 pruned-set note. Memory: [[todo_backlog]].

**WS3 second pass (owner asked to re-verify `.github/`, `assets/`, `launchd/`, root — 2026-06-19).** 14 more deletes: `.github/CODEOWNERS` (NanoClaw maintainers + `/package-lock.json`, we use pnpm) + `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` (upstream skill-contribution template) + `.github/workflows/{ci,label-pr}.yml` (ci fires only on `pull_request: branches:[main]` — career-pilot is `master`, never runs; label-pr reads the upstream template's checkboxes, a no-op); `launchd/com.nanoclaw.plist` (`service.ts` generates the plist *inline*, never reads this template — macOS-only, VM is systemd; dir emptied); `vitest.skills.config.ts` (targets `.claude/skills/**/tests/*.test.ts` — zero such files, zero callers); the 7 NanoClaw branding images (`nanoclaw-{favicon,icon,logo,logo-dark,profile,sales}.*` + `social-preview.jpg`) + `assets/setup-splash.txt` (**owner chose the full branding purge** — the setup screen loses the NanoClaw ASCII splash). **Two used-file edits** (owner-approved, beyond strict full-delete): `nanoclaw.sh` drops the `cat setup-splash.txt` block; `setup/lib/theme.ts` drops the `assets/nanoclaw-logo.png` provenance comment (the hex palette stays). **Kept (verified in-use):** `assets/fonts/Inter-*.woff` (resume-PDF), `nanoclaw.sh`/`setup.sh`/`.mcp.json`/all root configs. DoD: `git grep` clean of functional refs; `bash -n nanoclaw.sh` OK; `pnpm build` + full suite (106/1281) green; box-verified via the `deploy-backend` provision run.

**WS3 third pass — retire the upstream-sync skills + commit to hand-sync (owner posture decision, 2026-06-19).** Deleted `.claude/skills/{update-nanoclaw,migrate-nanoclaw}/` (4 files) + the in-repo `CHANGELOG.md` (a stale NanoClaw snapshot frozen at v2.0.64, read functionally only by those two skills). Rationale: D1 (§24.124) already found both skills mis-fit the diverged fork (no `upstream` remote; a `git merge upstream/main` would be a conflict bloodbath), we hand-synced all of v2.1.17 without them, and "flag this deviation for `/update-nanoclaw`" pointed at a tool we never run. **Posture now LOCKED: upstream sync is manual** — diff against the upstream tag, re-apply our tracked deviations (NANOCLAW_INTERNALS §11). Reworded the ~10 forward-looking refs accordingly (`src/upgrade-state.ts` tripwire message, `init-onecli` skill, `container/skills/.gitkeep`, `AGENT_SDK_PATTERNS.md` §upgrade-discipline, the STRATEGY/NANOCLAW_INTERNALS deviation flags, root `CLAUDE.md`); historical records that *name* the rejected skills as record (this D1, the §24.125 WS1 keep-list) keep them. **Not functional** (dev-facing skills, zero runtime); host `pnpm build` + full suite green; the `git grep` sweep confirms no surviving file functionally references a deleted path or the in-repo `CHANGELOG.md`. Memory: [[decision_architecture]], [[todo_backlog]].

#### 24.133 Repo-structure cleanup (owner ask — "intentional + consistent so I can dive in myself")

A legibility pass over the layout, distinct from the WS3 deadfile sweep:
- **`src/scrape-jobs/` → `src/modules/career-pilot/scrape-jobs/`** — it's career-pilot host code (every importer is `src/modules/career-pilot/*`; the container only *mirrors* its types in comments, it doesn't import it), so it belongs inside the module beside `scrape-jobs-bootstrap.ts` rather than at the `src/` root. Importers rewritten `../../scrape-jobs/` → `./scrape-jobs/`; the container's mirror comment repointed.
- **The WS3-excluded setup channel-auth orphans removed** (the "partial-strip" WS3 deferred under its full-delete-only rule, now owner-greenlit): `setup/{whatsapp-auth,signal-auth}.ts` + `setup/lib/teams-manifest.ts` deleted, their two entries dropped from the `STEPS` map in `setup/index.ts`, and the dead `"auth": "tsx src/whatsapp-auth.ts"` npm script removed (career-pilot is telegram-only).
- **Docs sorted by ownership:** our findings docs `PHASE5_PREP_FINDINGS.md` + `PHASE9_DEPLOY_FINDINGS.md` moved `docs/` → `.specs/` (our authored specs/docs live in `.specs/`; `docs/` is upstream NanoClaw reference). `docs/APPLE-CONTAINER-NETWORKING.md` deleted (Apple-`container`-CLI networking — macOS-only; we run Docker on Ubuntu) along with its `docs/README.md` index row.
- **`.gitignore`:** dropped the upstream `!groups/global/` + `!groups/main/` default-group exceptions so only our named groups (`career-pilot`, `-sandbox`, `_shared-*`) are committed; the local untracked `groups/{main,research-company}` leftovers were deleted off disk. host `pnpm build` + full suite green.

#### 24.134 Morning-ops hardening (three box-observed gaps, 2026-06-19)

One morning's good ops cascade (07:31 funnel-curator → research-company + build-interview-kit; an interview kit built for a `content delivery infrastructure` company) surfaced three independent gaps. Diagnosed against live dev-box state, not inferred. The unifying lesson — already learned in §24.78 — is **don't trust the model to voluntarily do the right thing; back the load-bearing behavior host-side.** Two of the three are that same fragility resurfacing on new surfaces.

##### 24.134a Kit entity-redaction belt (the product-codename leak)

**Observed:** `public_kit_view` for a live (obfuscated) app rendered the `lean-into` ('safe') section with `"[REDACTED:infra-d]'s «codename» (Rust) …"` — Pass 2 redacted the company NAME but a product codename (the target company's public open-source project) sat un-redacted next to the `[REDACTED:…]` marker, which re-identifies the company. `/live` was clean (the `record_progress` strings DO run Pass 3); only the kit path leaked.

**Root cause:** the kit projection (`public-kit-view.ts`) deliberately skips Pass 3 (§24.65 Δ — Haiku *role-plays* kit-length prose on a REWRITE prompt). Its only belt on a 'safe' section is deterministic Pass 1 (PII) + Pass 2 (tracked **company name/alias**) + the `leaksNonPublicCompany` scan. A product/project codename is none of those, so nothing catches it.

**Fix (owner choice: "LLM-detect, targeted").** A new belt module `src/modules/portal/kit-entity-redact.ts`, run per 'safe' section on a LIVE app inside `projectSections`, AFTER the deterministic sanitize + the existing leak scan. It uses a **DETECTION** prompt (list entities, never rewrite — sidesteps the §24.65 role-play failure) via the sanctioned `callPortkeyChat` host path: the model returns a JSON array of remaining substrings that could re-identify the redacted company (products, project/codenames, internal tools, named teams/offices/people, distinctive coined/branded terms). The host then **deterministically** redacts each returned token (word-boundary, → the provenance-distinct `[AI_REDACTED]` token per §24.134d) and re-runs `leaksNonPublicCompany`. The prompt's EXCLUDE list keeps the section useful: widely-known generic technologies (languages/protocols/databases/cloud/common OSS), **generic industry/technical vocabulary (standard acronyms, methodology/architecture names, discipline/role titles, product *categories*)**, AND the candidate's OWN past employers/projects (their résumé — the "targeted" scope, not "redact everything"). The prompt redacts ONLY terms confidently specific to ONE organization (tuned down in §24.134d after the first cut over-redacted generic industry acronyms like DCIM/SOA — the owner's "don't ai-redact basically everything"). No real codename — and no hardcoded keep-term — appears in the prompt.

- **Fail-safe = seal.** Belt-active (`kit_entity_redact_enabled` AND `portkeyConfigured()`) but the call fails / parses to nothing / over budget → the section is SEALED (withheld), consistent with the kit path's existing fail-closed posture. Belt-inactive (CI/local, no Portkey, no real surface) → render deterministic, today's behavior.
- **Safe-by-default.** Unlike `sanitization_pass3_enabled` (opt-in cosmetic rewrite), this is a leak-prevention belt → `kit_entity_redact_enabled` defaults **true** in `config/defaults.json`; it's inert without Portkey, so CI/unit tests (no key) are unaffected. New tunables: `kit_entity_redact_{model,budget_usd_per_day,timeout_ms}`. Content-keyed process cache + per-UTC-day budget guard mirror `sanitizer-pass3.ts`.
- **Backfill.** `resanitizeApplicationAuditTrail` (the operator re-mirror path + the policy-flip hook) now also calls `upsertPublicKitView`, so re-running `scripts/resanitize-application.ts --id <leaked app>` reprojects the kit through the new belt. Used once on the box to scrub the live codename row.

**DoD.** New module never throws; returns null→seal on any failure. `projectSections` async; existing `public-kit-view.test.ts` green unchanged (belt inert without Portkey). New `kit-entity-redact.test.ts`: detect→deterministic-redact with a mocked `callPortkeyChat` (tokens redacted; generic tech + own-employer preserved; empty array = unchanged; failure = null; cache hit). On the box: enable the pref, resanitize the leaked app, confirm `public_kit_view … LIKE '%ingora%'` returns 0 rows and the section still renders its generic-tech text.

##### 24.134b Daily-briefing host backstop (the silent skip)

**Observed:** the `0 8 * * *` briefing task fired + `completed` at 14:00Z; the 13:31 funnel-curator wrote a non-empty `attention_json` (one `action_owed` — "recruiter screen requested"); a kit had been built ~25 min prior — yet the ops session produced **0 owner-facing messages all day** (93 system action-RPCs, none of kind≠system). The orchestrator silent-skipped a briefing its own persona rules say it must send ("attention[] non-empty → you still emit").

**Fix (owner choice: host deterministic backstop, attention-only).** Same shape as §24.78. New `src/modules/career-pilot/briefing-backstop.ts`, invoked from a host-sweep MODULE-HOOK after the recurrence step (`maybeDeliverBriefingBackstop(inDb, outDb, session)`), ops session only:

- **Trigger:** the latest `status='completed'` `daily-briefing` task in the ops inbound, not yet handled.
- **Idempotency:** rides the session's host-owned `delivered` table — a synthetic `briefing-backstop:<taskId>` marker is inserted once the task is handled (delivered / stale / no-news / already-covered), so every later sweep tick short-circuits. No new schema.
- **Never-double-message window heuristic:** count owner-facing (non-`system`) `messages_out` in `[fireTime, fireTime+window_min]`. `>0` → skip (the briefing emitted, OR a coincident killer-match already pinged — an occasional miss of the digest, never a duplicate). `0` → proceed.
- **Stale gate:** a briefing older than `max_age_min` is marked-and-skipped (don't surprise the owner with hours-late news, e.g. after a host restart).
- **Render:** attention-only — a terse plain-text digest from the structured `attention[]` (company / reason / action_hint), headline-free like the persona briefing. Owner-facing Telegram via `getDeliveryAdapter().deliver(...)` (the approvals-module primitive); real names OK, NO sanitizer. No lead re-ranking (the silent-skip cases are attention-driven; a fallback path shouldn't spend an LLM call). Then `mirrorOpsDeliveryToChat` so a "tell me more about #1" reply has its referent (§24.67 D2).
- **Config:** `daily_briefing_backstop_{enabled(true)/window_min(10)/max_age_min(120)}`. Best-effort: never throws; transient failure (no outbound yet, deliver error) DEFERS without marking → next tick retries.

The pure decision (`decideBackstop`) and render (`renderBackstopDigest`) are factored out and unit-tested; the orchestrator is covered with seeded inbound/outbound/central DBs + a stub adapter.

**DoD.** `decideBackstop` returns deliver/mark-skip/defer correctly across stale / no-outbound / owner-message-present / empty-attention / news-present. Orchestrator: delivers once when a completed briefing left attention[] unsurfaced + no owner message in window; never delivers twice (marker); skips when an owner message is in the window (no double); defers (no mark) when outbound is absent. Host build clean; full host suite green. Box: a forced fresh completed-briefing + non-empty attention + quiet window produces exactly one owner Telegram message; a second sweep sends nothing.

##### 24.134c Dispatch trace ordering (marker after the work)

**Observed (live, seq order):** subagent `record_progress` rows (116–122) land first, then the deterministic `"Dispatched by the orchestrator."` rows (123–125) at the turn seal — so the dispatch marker sits AFTER the work it kicked off, and is redundant for any subagent that already logged progress.

**Root cause:** §24.78's deterministic rows are reconstructed at turn-END from the SDK result's `subagent_dispatches`, so they always take the turn's highest seq.

**Fix (owner choice: emit at dispatch-time).** A new `dispatch` `ProviderEvent` (`container/agent-runner/src/providers/types.ts`): in the claude provider's stream loop, the moment a NEW subagent is observed in a message (`subagentDispatchesFromMessage` minus the running set, BEFORE `accumulateTurnSignals` folds it in), yield `{ type: 'dispatch', subagentType }` — owner path only (`!emitTrace`; the sandbox carries dispatch info via the `trace` path, and gating keeps its stream byte-identical). The poll-loop's `handleEvent` turns it into a fire-and-forget `record_dispatch` action. Host `handleRecordDispatch` (registered owner-only) writes the deterministic `subagent_progress` row (public-safe name + the fixed `Dispatched by the orchestrator.` summary, sandbox traffic-class skipped). Because the dispatch action is written to outbound BEFORE the subagent's own `record_progress` calls, its public row takes a LOWER seq → the trace reads "▸ dispatched X" then X's progress. The §24.78 turn-end reconstruction in `handleRecordTurnTelemetry` is REMOVED (the telemetry still carries `subagent_dispatches` as data); a kit/dispatch double-emit can't happen because only one path writes the row now.

**DoD.** Container + host typecheck clean; container `bun test` green; host suite green. `handleRecordTurnTelemetry` no longer writes lifecycle rows (only the turn row); `handleRecordDispatch` writes one row per call, normalizes renamed subagents, honors the emit flag, skips sandbox. Box: a real owner cascade shows the `Dispatched by the orchestrator.` rows with a LOWER seq than the subagent's `record_progress` rows of the same turn (the inverse of the §24.78 box capture).

#### 24.134d Universal redaction language (make the redactions a legible feature)

Follows §24.134a: the entity belt is conservative (owner-accepted "better to over-redact"), so a kit now shows MANY redaction tokens. Owner ask: make them read as deliberate AI rigor, not breakage — *and* be honest about which pass did each one (the `--ai` violet means "an AI did this" per §24.73; painting a regex/DB redaction violet would be a false claim).

**Two honest tiers, one universal primitive.** A new `frontend/src/components/Redaction.tsx` renders every redaction token as a pill chip, with the tier read from the token SHAPE (no new metadata):
- **AI judgment** → violet `--ai` + ✦: the belt's token (now the provenance-distinct `[AI_REDACTED]`, §24.134a delta — the belt stops emitting the bare `[REDACTED]` that collided with Pass-1's URL-query token).
- **Deterministic scrub** → muted: Pass-1 PII (`[EMAIL_REDACTED]` / `[PHONE_REDACTED]` / `[AMOUNT_REDACTED]` / `[SSN_REDACTED]` / bare `[REDACTED]`) and the Pass-2 company token (`[REDACTED:<label>]`, whose chip shows the stable pseudonym).

Each chip is a non-interactive `<span>` with a per-tier `title` (hover) — deliberately NOT a `DisclosureTip` button like `AgentRef`, since a kit has dozens and that many tab-stops/popovers is the obnoxiousness we're avoiding. A one-time `RedactionLegend` on the `/kit` page carries the explanation. Wired universally through the shared `renderInline` (kit dossier + simulator output) and the `/live` feed summary (`LogStream`); the raw `<pre>` anonymization demo stays literal by design. Net effect: the §24.12 layered defense becomes visible — a visitor can see "deterministic scrub here, AI judgment there."

**DoD.** Host suite green (belt token → `[AI_REDACTED]`, updated assertions). Frontend: `Redaction` renders the right tier/glyph/title per token shape; `renderInline` + `LogStream` interleave chips with text without disturbing bold/code/tables; legend renders once on a live kit. Owner sees it locally before ship.

##### 24.134e Over-redaction: model-first, with an optional dev-only keep-list

The belt over-redacted (a kit hit 18 AI redactions, incl. generic terms + the candidate's OWN former employer). Two levers were tried; the decision is **model-first**:

- **Primary (kept): a stronger belt model.** `kit_entity_redact_model` default → **`claude-sonnet-4-6`** (the belt isn't dev-overridden — `dev_model_tier` is container-only — so this applies in dev + prod). Box-verified: Sonnet cut the kit's AI redactions **18 → 5**, KEPT the candidate's former employer (recognized it as their own résumé), and kept generic terms like SOA. The smarter model handles the candidate-owned-term case on its own. Per-belt cost stays negligible (per-kit, cached, fail-safe).
- **`protected_terms` keep-list: kept ONLY as an optional DEV override, invisible to the agent** (owner decision 2026-06-19). `candidate_profile.protected_terms` (migration 141, JSON array) + the belt's `filterProtected` deterministically force-keeps any listed term — inert when empty (the default). It is **NOT agent-visible**: removed from `update_profile_field` (both the container MCP-tool field enum and the host PROFILE_FIELDS), so the agent has no awareness of or tool for it. The owner sets it directly in the DB; the dev-inspector PersonaPanel shows it read-only, badged "dev override". The originally-planned **AI auto-derivation was DROPPED**: it crowded the persona and the orchestrator chronically confabulated (claimed it wrote it / that the field was un-writable) instead of persisting — and Sonnet makes it unnecessary. The escape hatch exists for the rare case the model over-redacts a specific term the owner wants pinned; nothing auto-maintains it.

#### 24.135 `/` hero flare pass (first-impression polish)

The home `/` landed but read flat/cramped (owner: "this home/hero page really needs to grab people"). A focused visual pass over the landing hero + section rhythm, derived from the todo-backlog "more flavor on /" item — NOT new scope. Frontend-only; no backend/data change.

- **Section rhythm.** Hero padding `py-16 → py-20 sm:py-24`; every inter-viewport gap unified to `mt-24` (was a flat `mt-16`/`mt-20` mix) so the five viewports read as distinct beats, not one continuous column. The `LiveTicker`'s own top margin moved with them.
- **Hero entrance — CSS, not `motion/react`, and only on the LIVE chrome.** A one-shot staggered rise-in (the `cp-rise` keyframe): badge (0s) → CTAs (0.08s) → stat line (0.16s). CSS-only is load-bearing: the spec's JS-disabled hero must still render, and `motion/react`'s `initial:{opacity:0}` leaves a no-JS hero invisible; CSS keyframes animate without JS and Playwright's `animations:'disabled'` fast-forwards them to the settled frame (deterministic baselines). The reduced-motion reset (§24.36 36.4) gained `animation-delay:0` so a stagger never holds an invisible frame. The **name/title/hook are deliberately EXCLUDED** from the animation: they're SSR'd for an instant solid first paint (§24.71/§24.36), and a fade from `opacity:0` undercuts that — so the headline is solid the instant the HTML lands while the live bits ease in around it.
- **Count-up stat numbers: considered, DROPPED.** A 0→N tick on the stat line reintroduces a hydration mismatch (SSR renders the final value) AND digit-width growth that shifts the centered line — the exact instability the §24.36 stat-line work eliminated. The entrance fade on the line gives the "live meter" feel without it.
- **Ambient hero backdrop (aurora), HERO-ONLY.** Two faint brand-hue radial washes (`--primary` green + `--accent-cool` cyan, token-driven via relative-color syntax, static) behind the `/` hero for depth. Scoped to the landing hero, NOT site-wide: the ops register ("Bloomberg gut") is a dense instrument aesthetic a colored glow fights, and a wash behind ops text erodes the AA-contrast margin that is itself a credibility signal (the muted-foreground 80% L bump). Used once it's an accent; everywhere it's wallpaper. (Owner decision 2026-06-19: hero-only.)
- **Two section-heading `InfoTip`s.** ⓘ explain-on-tap next to "My job search, live" (privacy/pipeline) and "Agent activity" (the ◆ proactive = autonomous-worker tell). The shared `InfoTip` gained an `align` prop: its §24.86 `-translate-y-px` caps-nudge optically centers next to UPPERCASE labels (every prior usage), but reads 1px high next to these normal-case headings, so they pass `align="text"` (no nudge). Default unchanged → every existing tip is byte-stable.
- **Copy.** The pitch transition "…and you can watch it happen below…" → "Watch it happen below — or run it on your own open role right now." (killed the second consecutive "and"; the parallel 5th step "↻ and learns from every outcome" keeps its "and").
- **A live-action line in the hero — considered, DECLINED.** Promoting the newest agent action above the fold was weighed (it would make "working live" literally true at first paint) but rejected: it crowds the clean hero for a gain the stat line + funnel + ticker already deliver. The hero stays apple-clean.

**DoD.** Frontend `tsc` + `prettier --no-semi` clean; full frontend vitest green (entrance/aurora touch no logic; `InfoTip`/`LiveTicker` units green incl. the new `align` default). No functional e2e asserts on the changed copy/structure (`hero-stats` stays a snapshot mask). The two `@visual` home baselines (`home-chromium-win32.png`, `mobile-home-mobile-chromium-win32.png`) re-blessed (local-only; `@visual` is `--grep-invert`'d in CI). Owner eyeballed on `dev:mock`.

#### 24.136 Production release plan — Phase 9.7 cutover + Phase 10 soft launch (2026-06-20)

**Context.** Everything is built and dev-verified behind the access-gated `dev.hire.<DOMAIN>`; **prod (`hire.<DOMAIN>`) does not exist yet** — it's the deferred "9.4 cutover half" (§24.70 / §24.71 / §24.74 all recorded prod-gated checks "for the cutover"). This section decomposes the path to the first public release: the owner's prod-readiness list + the standing cutover, in three phases. **This is the plan-of-record; the executable step-by-step is the new operator runbook `.specs/PROD_CUTOVER.md`** (twin of RECOVERY.md — filled as the cutover runs). The shape is the §9.1 "gate-before-route" discipline applied to a whole environment: **Phase A** = everything buildable + verifiable on dev now (no prod env needed); **Phase B** (Phase 9.7) = stand up + cut over to prod; **Phase C** (Phase 10) = live-verify + soft launch (the "test in prod before it can affect my life" buffer).

**Locked decisions.**
- **D1 — `/admin` = a full-knob owner control-center, include-by-default.** The §24.74 attribution browser is the seed; finish it into the prod ops surface. **The knob rule is inclusion, not curation** (owner: "I don't want to cut an emergency patch for a knob I forgot"): audit the entire `KNOB_SPECS`/`preferences` set and surface every operationally-meaningful lever (budgets, per-IP/concurrency caps, pause + kill-switches, quiet hours, idle-timeout, retention windows, system modes, sender policy, telemetry capture, `simulator_enabled`, the §24.121 contact knobs). The **deny-list is short and explicit**: the recruiter-sim controls, the `?__state`/mock seams, and the data-reset (dev-only or destructive); a *useful* destructive control (kill-switch) is included but behind a confirm. Worst case = a rarely-touched knob is present, never a missing lever at 2am.
- **D2 — `/admin` design = a tabbed dashboard, not the `/dev` debug-scroll** (owner: "I don't mind this being fancy"). A control-center IA — Overview (health · pause/mode · live cost) / Pipeline / Visitors (attribution, already built) / Contacts (§24.121 store) / System (telemetry rollups · container pool · the knobs) — in the existing brand language (cards, `Sparkline`, the aurora vocabulary). Reads like a product, not a debug page.
- **D3 — `/admin` access = a dedicated prod Cloudflare Access app + origin-JWT fail-closed + the `admin_api_enabled` flip** (the §24.74 deferred-admin resume plan, unchanged). The public prod site is open; `/admin*` is a path on the same host behind its OWN Access app (owner email only), and `/api/admin/*` validates the Access-JWT and is default-off. **The panels build + verify NOW on the already-owner-gated dev `/admin`; only the prod Access app is cutover-gated.**
- **D4 — versioning = product semver from `v1.0.0`, manual `v*` tag, CHANGELOG-driven release.** A fresh product `CHANGELOG.md` (the old one was deleted in §24.132 — a stale NanoClaw snapshot); semver is the PRODUCT version, NOT `package.json`'s `2.0.70` (the NanoClaw-fork version — never conflate). A `release.yml` cuts a GitHub Release from the CHANGELOG section on a `v*` tag (manual tag = the owner controls when a release is cut). The build injects **`VITE_APP_VERSION`** — **prod = the tag** (a footer chip links to `/releases/tag/<v>`), **dev = `dev · <short-sha>`**. The chip lives in `SiteFooter` next to the build credit — distinct from the *retired* status/SHA/cache metadata footer (§24.35), not a resurrection of it.
- **D5 — security = a structured threat-model pass, not ad-hoc.** A threat-model doc (every public surface → its mitigations → its gaps), then `/security-review` on the public surface, then targeted hardening, then the live abuse-sim at cutover. Surfaces: the **simulator** (the only money-spend path) + **prompt-injection / the sandbox agent on visitor input** (the hardest — red-team the `disallowedTools`/no-Gmail/`maxTurns`/budget/container-boundary defense) + the **`/contact` relay** (Turnstile/RL/flood-cap exist; re-check flood ceilings, Telegram markdown-injection, payload limits) + the **API perimeter** (state + confirm the strength: browser→Worker-only, service-token + origin-JWT fail-closed; then per-endpoint RL, input validation, SSE limits, the `/r` redirect, error-message leakage).
- **D6 — identity = the real master résumé seeded into the runtime `candidate_profile` ONLY, never a tracked file.** It's real PII (name/employers/projects) → per [[feedback_no_personal_data]] it never touches the repo, not even a test fixture. It lives in the box DB (seeded via onboarding or a one-off script); working drafts stay in chat or a gitignored scratch path. Dress-rehearsed on dev in Phase A, becomes the real identity at the Phase-B cutover. It is load-bearing across the app (agent self-knowledge / `/work` composition / résumé PDF / per-role tailoring), so it earns a dedicated content session.
- **D7 — sequence = A (dev) → B (cutover) → C (soft launch)**, with the **Gmail consent-screen → Production started early / in parallel** (a Google Console action with a possible review lag; prod Gmail dies weekly on a Testing-mode screen — see the [[feedback_tailscale_auth_link]]-adjacent 7-day-token pain).

**Phase A — final dev-side prep (buildable + verifiable now).**
- **A0 — finishing touches.** The owner's loose-ends list (TBD — captured at session start); each scoped per item. Done before the environment changes underneath us.
- **A1 — `/admin` control-center** (D1/D2/D3 panels half). First step = the `KNOB_SPECS` audit (enumerate every group, mark include/deny). Then build the tabbed panels over the §17.2 prod-safe reads (`runHealthChecks`, `getTelemetry`/`request_telemetry` rollups, the funnel read-model, the §24.121 contacts store, attribution, container-pool/system) + the included knobs (preferences tier, confirm-gated destructives). Owner-gated on dev. `/admin` E2E.
- **A2 — version/release tooling** (D4). `CHANGELOG.md` + `release.yml` + `VITE_APP_VERSION` injection + the `SiteFooter` chip (prod tag→release link / dev `dev · sha`).
- **A2.5 — model-choice audit** (§24.140, owner-added). Audit every Haiku/Sonnet/Opus choice vs purpose; drop the public visitor sandbox Opus→Sonnet via a prod-safe `sandbox_model_tier` knob (owner agent stays Opus); knob-ify `win_confidence_model`; keep the kit-redact Sonnet belt. Answers the "should models / per-IP be knobs?" question (per-IP caps already are).
- **A3 — security** (D5, §24.141). The threat-model doc (`.specs/THREAT_MODEL.md`, surfaces S1–S6) → `/security-review` → targeted hardening of the fix-now backlog (the money-path budget leak S1-1, the cap re-tune S1-2, SSRF S2-1, free-text share S2-2, Telegram MD-injection S3-1) → the prompt-injection/sandbox red-team (R1–R6 on dev). (The live abuse-sim + origin-JWT-forgery check run in Phase C.)
- **A4 — master résumé + identity dress-rehearsal** (D6). **A4.0 FIRST (owner, 2026-06-20, post-dev-smoke-test): investigate sandbox tailored-résumé quality.** The sandbox-run tailored résumés look off — "as if two tailor-resume subagents drifted apart," though the subagent prompt is SHARED (`_shared-subagents/tailor-resume.md` feeds both groups via `composeSubagentDefinitions`). Lead hypotheses, to test against the owner's two PDFs (the master résumé + a sandbox tailored example): (a) the **§24.140 sandbox→Sonnet** model drop (owner stays Opus) degraded tailoring quality — prime suspect since the smoke test is post-A2.5; (b) the sandbox-only **`tailored-resume-json` → `validateTailoredResume` → PDF** path diverges from the owner agent's output (the owner's tailor-resume emits chat bullets, no PDF — §24.137), so the résumé-block instructions live in the sandbox PERSONA + `validateTailoredResume`'s master-forced fields, not the shared subagent; (c) the per-group `agents-src` overlay or a persona résumé-block instruction. Then the content session: stage the real `candidate_profile` on the dev box (DB-only); confirm `/work`, the PDF, and tailoring render it well.
- **A5 — launch-state & site lifecycle** (§24.149, owner-added 2026-06-21). The site has a *lifecycle* and only the active steady-state is designed for: polish the **cold-start** so an empty pipeline reads as fresh, not broken (subtle freshness cues, no launch banner — D2), and build the **concluded-search retrospective** behind a manual `/admin` toggle (`site_lifecycle_state`, never auto — D3). No fake prod data, ever (D1). The Phase-C quiet-observation window doubles as the pipeline **warm-up window** (D5).

**Phase B — prod cutover (Phase 9.7).** Detailed in `.specs/PROD_CUTOVER.md`; the slices: prod backend service + prod tunnel + prod Access posture on the VM · prod DNS (`hire.<DOMAIN>` + `api.hire.<DOMAIN>`) + prod Worker route · **Terraform prod apply** (the §24.70 Commit-4 WAF/Bot-Fight edge posture, never live-applied) · **real Turnstile widget** (swap the dev always-pass test keys) · prod GH env secrets/vars (prod `PORTAL_PUBLIC_URL`, a distinct `VISIT_IP_HASH_SALT`, the real Turnstile secret, the Access service token, `VITE_APP_VERSION`=tag) · prod `/admin*` Access app + flip `admin_api_enabled` · seed the **real identity** (D6) · **Gmail consent-screen → Production** (start early, D7).

**Phase C — live-verify + soft launch (Phase 10).** The "recorded, not skipped" checks that need a public surface to mean anything (§24.70 / §24.71 / §24.74): a real Turnstile challenge from a real browser · live Bot Fight Mode · a public abuse simulation (per-IP cap, global $-budget, Workers RL all engage) · origin-JWT rejects a forged `Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion` · end-to-end smoke. Then a **quiet observation window** in prod (the system runs, the owner watches, the link goes nowhere yet) → a go/no-go checklist → publish. This window is also the pipeline **warm-up window** (§24.149 D5): the real agent does its private/reversible work (scrape, research, draft, build pipeline rows) so the pipeline is genuinely populated by the time the link goes out, with irreversible outreach **sends gated** until the owner is confident — no fake data, just the real search run a few days ahead of publish.

**Definition of done.**
- **A1:** `/admin` shows every included knob (the deny-list is the only exclusion) + the prod-safe read panels, tabbed; owner-gated on dev; destructive controls confirm-gated; E2E + host/FE tsc + suites green; `@visual` re-blessed.
- **A2:** a `v*` tag cuts a GitHub Release from `CHANGELOG.md`; the footer chip renders the version (prod tag link / dev `dev · sha`) and is byte-stable (no layout shift); no resurrection of the retired metadata footer.
- **A3:** the threat-model doc enumerates every public surface with mitigations + residual gaps; `/security-review` run and findings triaged (fixed or explicitly accepted); the sandbox prompt-injection red-team documented.
- **A4:** the real `candidate_profile` renders correctly through `/work` + the PDF + a tailoring run on dev; **`git grep` confirms zero real-identity strings in tracked files** (the [[feedback_no_personal_data]] sweep).
- **A5:** the cold-start reads fresh-not-broken on an empty pipeline (no perpetual skeleton); `site_lifecycle_state` flips `/` + `/pipeline` into the anonymized retrospective from `/admin`; per §24.149 DoD.
- **B/C:** every `PROD_CUTOVER.md` step checked off; the Phase-C live checks pass and are recorded; the go/no-go is green before the link is shared.

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.136; the new `.specs/PROD_CUTOVER.md` runbook; the §24.38 Phase-9 table repointed (the 9.4 cutover row → here as 9.7, + Phase 10 soft launch → Phase C); the §24.74 "Deferred admin work — resume plan" absorbed into A1/B; PORTAL gets an `/admin` page note + the footer version-chip note when A1/A2 build. Memory: [[status_current]] RESUME-HERE → "prod release plan = §24.136; focused session pending", [[todo_backlog]].

## §24.137 — Phase A0: `/` + pipeline + dashboard finishing touches (pre-cutover polish)

The first slice of the §24.136 **Phase A** — owner-driven finishing touches on the public surface before the prod cutover. No new capability; UX polish, one anonymization-consistency pass, and a polling tune. Owner's A0 list, drilled and shipped on `dev`:

1. **`/` work-steps (wrap + de-number).** The four "what the agent does" steps moved from a `flex-wrap` row to a **2-col grid** — the §24.135 "3 on a line, 4 orphaned, closer on a third line" wrap is now structurally impossible. The ordinal **1–4 numerals became a uniform brand dot**: the steps run organically, not in a fixed sequence, so an ordinal implied an order that isn't real. The `↻ "and learns from every outcome"` closer keeps its own line + loop glyph (the §24.111 meta-capability).
2. **`/` dropped the "Watch it happen below…" transition line** — filler that half-restated the bullets above it; cutting it lets the bullets land and makes "Read the full story →" the clean next beat. (Every line on `/` earns its place.)
3. **Polling tune: `DEFAULT_POLL_MS` 4s → 20s** (`use-polled-json.ts`, drives pipeline/telemetry/architecture/observability). The portal's live data moves at human pace (recruiter replies over days); the "alive" feeling is carried by the **real-time SSE trace, not these JSON polls** — so a slow poll is plenty and spares the box a needless ~5×/min request per open tab.
4. **Pipeline win-confidence `~%` → `✦%`.** The bare tilde read as an approximation glyph; the AI sparkle `✦` (the cast's mark, AI-violet) reads as "AI-generated win odds" — tying the figure to the AI-authorship language used everywhere else.
5. **Pipeline anonymized-company chip (the "looks bugged" fix).** Obfuscated company handles rendered as raw `[infra-e]` brackets, which read as a rendering bug. A shared **`CompanyHandle`** now renders them in the §24.134d **company-tier redaction language** (muted fill, inset ring, hover title) on the board card + the drawer title; a once-per-page **`CompanyHandleLegend`** under the `/pipeline` header decodes the convention before a visitor clicks. **Register boundary (deliberate):** the `[ref]` *linked* form stays on the agent traces + the dashboard "Recent outcomes" — those are clickable cross-links into the drawer (the navigation register). So the **chip = a standalone anonymized *identifier label*; the bracket-link = a clickable *ref***. Two registers, each internally consistent (the traces were already the owner's cited "good" example).
6. **Pipeline drawer Stage/Status.** The two usually carry the same word (one cased, one not), reading as a doubled fact. They now **collapse to one when equal**, spending the freed cell on "Last activity" (a real freshness signal); both show only when Status genuinely differs from Stage (e.g. stage `tech` vs status `TECH_SCREEN`).
7. **`/dashboard` leads with the live trace at every width.** The `lg:order` swap that floated the four KPI stat tiles above the trace on desktop is gone — the trace + pipeline rail now lead at all widths, KPI tiles follow. The abstract numbers no longer front-run the "agent working now" wow on what is a likely first-impression page (the prominent `/` "See it work →" lands here).
8. **Header: a discoverable `/about` entry.** A quiet, **`lg`-gated** "What is this?" pill (lucide `Info` glyph) sits next to the wordmark — a permanent, low-key way into the `/about` explainer non-technical visitors found most useful (it had only the footer link + the `/` deep-link before). Gated `lg` because below that the nav row is already tight against a long `VITE_PERSON_NAME`; **phones reach it via the hamburger menu** (added as the first item), the 640–1024 band via the footer. **Deliberate deviation** from the prior "`/about` is a footer link, not a header item" (PORTAL §8.1/§8.2) — recorded here.
9. **Hero stat count-up — considered, DROPPED.** A load-time count-up runs as a JS `requestAnimationFrame` animation, which the visual harness does **not** freeze (it freezes only CSS via `animations:'disabled'`) → a flaky hero `@visual` baseline; it also brushes the §24.36 dimensional-stability doctrine the stat line is built on. The reward (two ticking numbers) didn't justify the fragility going into a cutover. A visual-safe **"tick-on-live-change"** variant (animate only when live data genuinely changes a number; never animates against the static E2E seed) remains an option if motion is wanted later.

**No-emoji rule (owner, reaffirmed).** Colorable typographic glyphs (`✦ ◆ ↻ ▤ ✎`) or inline SVG (lucide) only — literal emoji (🔒 etc.) can't inherit the theme's colors, so they're out everywhere on the visitor surface.

**Deferred to feature work (from the #3 capability review — NOT A0; also NOT A1/A2, which are `/admin` + version tooling).** The owner's #3 asked whether "tailor résumé" + "draft outreach" close the loop. They partly don't, and the gaps are net-new capability, captured here as their own future slices:
- **Tailored-résumé artifact.** `tailor-resume` outputs **chat bullets, not a document** — the candidate isn't handed a tailored PDF (the only PDF is the static *master* résumé).
