Mirror the interview-kit **writer pattern** (a new `persist_*` tool) to land a tailored variant as a Drive Doc / generated PDF. Own feature slice.
- **Contacts model.** There is **no contact↔lead association** in the schema today; the outreach recipient is **owner-supplied or an inbound portal contact** (`read_contacts`) — the agent does not *discover* contacts. A `contacts` table linked to applications/leads (so the agent remembers who it's emailed per company) is the higher-value, lower-risk add; autonomous recipient **discovery** stays in V2 (the persona already calls it "a separate subagent on the roadmap").

**Definition of done.** Board + drawer unit tests green (the chip renders the muted pill — not `[brackets]` — for obfuscated rows and the plain name on reveal; `data-testid="company-handle"` present); the `CompanyHandleLegend` renders on `/pipeline`; the drawer shows "Last activity" when stage≡status; `/dashboard` DOM leads with the trace (no `lg:order` swap); the header "What is this?" pill renders at `lg` and the entry rides the phone menu; win-confidence reads `✦N%`; `DEFAULT_POLL_MS === 20000`. Full frontend `vitest` + `tsc` + `prettier --no-semi` clean; the e2e/`@visual` baselines re-blessed for `/`, `/pipeline`, `/dashboard`, and the shared header (intended visual deltas, local-only). Box-observed on `dev` via the seeded mock harness (screenshots reviewed with the owner).

## §24.138 — Phase A1: `/admin` control-center (D1/D2/D3 — the panels half)

The second slice of §24.136 **Phase A**. Finish the §24.74 attribution browser into the prod ops surface: a tabbed, owner-only control-center that exposes **every operationally-meaningful lever** (D1 — inclusion, not curation) over the §17.2 prod-safe reads, built + verified now on the already-owner-gated dev `/admin` (D3 — only the prod Access app is cutover-gated). No new agent capability; this is the operator's cockpit.

### The KNOB_SPECS audit (D1 — inclusion, not curation)

**One canonical registry, deny-set derivation.** The dev-inspector's curated `KNOB_SPECS` (~30 specs in `dev-inspector.ts`) is promoted into a single prod-safe registry (`src/modules/portal/knob-registry.ts`) covering the **whole `preferences` universe** (config/defaults.json, ~110 keys). `/admin`'s editable set = **registry − `ADMIN_DENY`**; the deny-list is the *only* exclusion. The dev inspector keeps writing the full registry (a strict superset — it gains the newly-spec'd knobs); `/admin` writes the prod-safe subset. The `KnobSpec` type, `validateKnobWrite`, and the `KnobView` builder move to the shared registry; dev-inspector and admin both import them.

**A coverage test is the teeth behind "don't forget a knob."** A unit test asserts every `config/defaults.json` `preferences` key is **either** spec'd in the registry **or** on an explicit `UNSPEC_KNOBS` deny-with-reason map. Adding a new default without a conscious include/deny decision fails CI. This operationalizes the owner's rule ("I don't want to cut an emergency patch for a knob I forgot") — the worst case becomes a rarely-touched knob being *present*, never a missing lever at 2am.

**INCLUDE** — every lever below, grouped (the System tab, except the modes/kill-switches which lead the Overview):
- **Budgets & caps** — `owner_daily_llm_budget_usd`, `sandbox_daily_global_budget_usd`, `sandbox_per_ip_daily_run_cap`, `simulator_max_budget_usd`, `daily_briefing_max_cost_usd`, `sanitization_pass3_budget_usd_per_day`, `kit_entity_redact_budget_usd_per_day`.
- **Modes & kill-switches (Overview controls)** — `live_mode` (toggle; confirm + the `_required_before_live_mode` profile gate), pause/resume + kill-switch (the `kill-switch.ts` control plane; kill-switch is the confirm-gated *useful destructive* per D1), `telemetry_capture`, `owner_subagent_trace_emit_enabled`.
- **Public simulator** — `simulator_enabled`, `simulator_max_turns`, `simulator_idle_timeout_ms`, `simulator_hard_wall_ms`, `simulator_abandon_grace_ms`, `simulator_results_ttl_days`, `simulator_recent_limit`, `sandbox_session_reap_idle_sec`.
- **Contact relay** — `contact_relay_enabled`, `contact_relay_max_per_window`, `contact_relay_window_sec`, `contact_message_max_chars`, `contact_retention_max`, `contact_dedup_window_sec`.
- **Briefing** — `daily_briefing_enabled`, `daily_briefing_time` (cron), `daily_briefing_min_llm_score`, `daily_briefing_top_n`, `daily_briefing_backstop_enabled`, `daily_briefing_backstop_window_min`, `daily_briefing_backstop_max_age_min`.
- **Scouting / killer-match** — `job_scrape_enabled`, `job_scrape_cron`, `killer_match_enabled`, `killer_match_cron`, `killer_match_min_rules_score`, `killer_match_recency_window_hours`, `killer_match_max_per_fire`.
- **Pipeline curator** — `funnel_curator_enabled`, `funnel_curator_cron`, `funnel_curator_gmail_lookback_days`, `funnel_curator_max_narratives`, `funnel_curator_max_attention_items`, `funnel_curator_skip_if_no_deltas`, `funnel_curator_skip_classified_messages`, `close_detection_enabled`, `close_detection_cron`, `close_detection_threshold_days`.
- **Interview kits** — `interview_kit_auto_generate`, `interview_kit_folder_name`, `interview_kit_drive_folder_id`, `interview_kit_drive_archive_folder_id`, `interview_kit_cleanup_enabled`, `interview_kit_stale_days`.
- **Sanitization / redaction** — `sanitization_llm_review_threshold_chars`, `sanitization_llm_review_aggressiveness`, `sanitization_pass3_enabled`, `sanitization_pass3_model`, `sanitization_pass3_timeout_ms`, `sanitization_public_summary_max_chars`, `sanitization_audit_drop_on_unmatched_company`, `sanitization_resanitize_on_application_update`, `kit_entity_redact_enabled`, `kit_entity_redact_model`, `kit_entity_redact_timeout_ms`.
- **Telemetry & health** — `request_telemetry_retention_days`, `request_telemetry_prune_interval_sec`, `visit_telemetry_retention_days`, `visit_telemetry_prune_interval_sec`, `health_check_interval_sec`, `health_stale_pending_threshold_sec`, `health_series_overdue_threshold_sec`, `health_orphan_response_warn_count`, `health_outbound_backlog_warn_count`, `health_failure_streak_threshold`, `health_surface_stale_hours`, `health_cascade_silent_window_hours`.
- **Containers & sessions** — `container_idle_timeout_sec`, `ops_container_idle_timeout_sec`, `container_orphan_reap_grace_sec`, `container_max_concurrent`, `container_memory_mb`, `container_cpu`, `ops_transcript_rotate_bytes`, `ops_transcript_rotate_age_days`, `ops_mirror_to_chat`, `ops_bootstrap_min_interval_sec`.
- **Notify / quiet hours** — `quiet_hours`, `quiet_hours_tz`, `telegram_proactive_frequency_cap_per_day`, `auto_research_threshold`.
- **Polling** — `gmail_poll_interval_sec`, `calendar_poll_interval_sec` (the §24.105 "no live consumer today" note carried forward).
- **Portal perf / internals (Advanced)** — `portal_sse_tail_interval_ms`, `portal_sse_keepalive_ms`, `portal_telemetry_cache_ms`, `portal_architecture_cache_ms`, `portal_observability_cache_ms`, `arch_provider_error_rate_degraded`, `arch_sweep_stale_sec`, `action_response_orphan_ttl_sec`, `llm_fetch_timeout_ms`.

**DENY (`ADMIN_DENY`) — short + explicit:**
| Class | Keys | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Recruiter-sim dial | the 12 `recruiter_sim_*` keys (incl. `recruiter_sim_pace_presets`) | Dev-only simulator — never a prod lever. |
| Dev model tier | `dev_model_tier` | Dev/container-only model downgrade. |
| Self-referential gates | `admin_api_enabled`, `origin_jwt_validation_enabled` | Flipping these *from inside* the admin surface locks the owner out / weakens the gate that protects the surface. Managed via env (`CF_ACCESS_*`) / Telegram / CI only. |
| Boot & deploy identity | `portal_api_port`, `portal_cors_origins`, `portal_public_url` | Bind-time (no runtime effect once bound) or deploy-managed; a wrong `portal_public_url` silently breaks every minted `/r/<code>` link. Set via GH env at cutover. |
| Non-scalar (object/array) | `channel_pref_by_class`, `approval_scope`, `briefing_schedule`, `funnel_curator_ghosting_thresholds_days`, `killer_match_source_allow_list`, `llm_pricing_usd_per_mtok` | The knob primitive is scalar (boolean/number/cron/enum); these need a structured editor — out of A1 scope. Surfaced read-only on the System tab; their scalar siblings (e.g. `daily_briefing_time`) *are* editable. |
| Mock seams / data-reset | the `?__state` frontend seam; the dev-inspector reset scopes (`funnel-data`/`conversation`/`profile`/`everything`) | Test-only seam / destructive dev-only ops — stay on `/dev` + Telegram + CI. |

`system_modes`-tier values (`live_mode`, `pause_state`/`pause_reason`) are **not** registry knobs — they're written through `setLiveMode` / `setPauseState` + the kill-switch control plane (the Overview controls), not the preferences knob-write path. `pause_reason` is control-plane-derived (never directly edited).

### IA — a tabbed control-center (D2)

Brand language (cards, `Sparkline`, the aurora vocabulary), not the `/dev` debug-scroll. Tabs:
- **Overview** — system mode (LIVE/SHADOW + pause state) with the pause/resume + kill-switch + live-mode controls; the `runHealthChecks` rollup (ok/warn/critical counts + top findings, each with its `next_step`); 24h cost-by-class (from `getTelemetry`/`request_telemetry`); the container pool (n/capacity). The cockpit's at-a-glance.
- **Pipeline** — the funnel read-model summary (counts by stage + attention/ghosting items). Owner-gated → may show real names (no public anonymization here).
- **Visitors** — the existing attribution browser (`buildAttributionReport`), moved verbatim into a tab.
- **Contacts** — the §24.121 `contact_submissions` store (inbound portal recruiters).
- **System** — the included-knob grid (grouped, the `GROUP_ORDER`/`GROUP_META` vocabulary extended for the new groups), telemetry rollups, container-pool detail, the read-only non-scalar knobs, and an "edge-tier lives in `wrangler.jsonc`" note (Turnstile/Workers-RL are not host knobs — §24.70 D4).

### Endpoint contracts (all under the existing `adminEnabled()` gate — D3)

`/api/admin/*` already 404s unless `adminEnabled()` (dev → open; prod → `admin_api_enabled` AND origin-JWT). New routes:
- `GET /api/admin/summary` → the Overview rollup `{ mode, health, cost24h, pool, headline }`.
- `GET /api/admin/attribution` → (existing) Visitors.
- `GET /api/admin/contacts` → the contact-submissions list.
- `GET /api/admin/pipeline` → the funnel summary.
- `GET /api/admin/knobs` → `buildAdminKnobs` over `ADMIN_KNOB_KEYS`.
- `POST /api/admin/knobs` → `{ key, value } | { key, reset } | { resetAll }`; reuses `validateKnobWrite`/`applyKnobWrite` **but rejects any key in `ADMIN_DENY` with 403** (defense-in-depth behind the not-rendered UI). Writes the `preferences` tier — prod-capable (gated by `adminEnabled`, **not** `isDevEnv`).
- `POST /api/admin/control` → `{ action: 'pause' | 'resume' | 'killswitch' | 'set_live_mode', confirm?, on? }`. `pause`/`resume` reuse `executeControlCommand`; `killswitch` and `set_live_mode:true` require `confirm: true` (400 without); `set_live_mode` calls `setLiveMode` after the `_required_before_live_mode` profile gate.

### Decomposition (DD cadence)

- **Commit 1 — spec (no deploy).** This §24.138 (the audit, encoded); PORTAL.md `/admin` page note (the tabbed control-center + the owner-only/Access gating); the §24.74 "deferred admin work" pointer → here.
- **Commit 2 — backend.** Extract `knob-registry.ts` (types + validation + `KnobView` builder + the full spec set, expanded to the ~80 included keys with new `KnobGroup`s); `ADMIN_DENY` + `ADMIN_KNOB_KEYS` + `UNSPEC_KNOBS` + the coverage test; `buildAdminSummary`/`buildAdminContacts`/`buildAdminPipeline` read builders (reusing `runHealthChecks`/`getTelemetry`/system-status/the funnel read-model/the contacts store); the `/api/admin/{summary,contacts,pipeline,knobs}` + `/api/admin/control` handlers + the deny-on-write guard. Host vitest + tsc green.
- **Commit 3 — frontend.** Rebuild `admin.tsx` as the tabbed control-center; extend `use-admin.ts` (summary/knobs/contacts/pipeline/control hooks); a prod-safe `AdminKnobControls` sharing the dev knob-grid group rendering (extend the frontend `KnobGroup` + `GROUP_ORDER`/`GROUP_META`); confirm dialogs on kill-switch + live-mode flip. Frontend vitest + tsc + `prettier --no-semi` clean; `/admin` E2E; `@visual` re-blessed.

### Definition of done

`/admin` renders, tabbed, in the brand language; every included knob is present (the deny-list is the only exclusion) and writable on dev with live effect; the Overview shows health + cost + pool + mode from the prod-safe reads; destructive controls (kill-switch, live-mode-on) are confirm-gated; the deny-list is enforced on the write endpoint (403), not just hidden; the **coverage test passes** (every `defaults.json` preferences key is spec'd or explicitly unspec'd); host + frontend `tsc` + suites green; `/admin` E2E covers the tabs + a knob round-trip + a gated-control confirm; the changed/added `@visual` baselines re-blessed (local-only). Owner-gated on dev; the prod Access app stays cutover-gated (B).

**A1 polish (owner feedback on the live dev `/admin`, 2026-06-20).** A small UX pass, no new capability: (1) the tab `<nav>` is **sticky** under the site header (`top-14`) so a long System tab no longer forces a scroll back up to switch tabs; (2) long-text **overflow handling** across the panels — the Contacts message clamps to 3 lines (full text on hover) and emails/referrers/company+role cells truncate or `break-all`, so one verbose row can't blow out a table; (3) **inline descriptions** on the Overview — a legend under the mode controls (Mode / Run state / Pause spend / Kill switch) + a one-line caption on each of Health / 24h spend / Container pool; (4) two **knob-note honesty fixes** — `simulator_recent_limit` now says the "Recent runs" strip only renders in the `/watch` fallback (paused/disabled), and `sanitization_pass3_model` clarifies it's Haiku + off by default and is NOT the Sonnet kit-redact belt (`kit_entity_redact_model`). The owner-feedback item "is this handled elsewhere?" was the prompt for (2)'s audit across all four admin tables.

**A0-continued finishing touches (owner list after A1, 2026-06-20).** A second polish pass on the public surface, no new capability: (1) `/pipeline` "Bookmarked & closed" was an unbounded vertically-wrapping grid (the other lanes scroll, it grew the page down without limit) → reworked to a **single horizontal filmstrip** — uniform fixed-width cards in one sideways-scrolling row, sorted newest-activity-first, so the strip stays one card tall however many close; (2) the `/` hero CTA "See it work →" — owner-reviewed, **left as-is**; (3) `/about` gained a final FAQ entry on the **dev-environment / staged-rollout process** ("Changes ship through a separate, access-gated copy of the whole system first … shadow mode lets it dry-run an action before that action is ever armed") — deliberately does NOT name the `/admin` page; (4) a `/watch` copy audit fixed a stale "plus pitch bullets" line (the bullets were removed) → the "What happens" step now names the two real gifts (a tailored résumé to download + a personalized cold-outreach email).

## §24.139 — Phase A2: version / release tooling (D4 — product semver, CHANGELOG-cut releases, the footer version chip)

The third slice of §24.136 **Phase A**. A2 gives the product a **version identity** distinct from the NanoClaw-fork `package.json` version, a **one-tag release ritual** the owner controls, and a quiet **footer chip** that tells any visitor (and the owner) exactly what's deployed. No new product capability; this is release hygiene the public surface earns before cutover.

**Why now (Phase A, pre-cutover).** The mechanism must exist and be verified on dev *before* the first `v1.0.0` is cut at the Phase-B cutover — the cutover should *use* this tooling, not invent it. Everything here is buildable + verifiable now: the chip renders on dev as `dev · <sha>`, the CHANGELOG extractor is unit-tested locally, and `release.yml` is first exercised when the owner pushes a `v*` tag (at cutover).

**Locked decisions (continue §24.136 D4).**

- **D4-1 — two version namespaces, never conflated.** `package.json` `version` (`2.0.70`) is the **NanoClaw-fork** version (upstream-sync bookkeeping — §24.128). The **product** version is independent, semver from **`v1.0.0`**, and lives in `CHANGELOG.md` + the git `v*` tag only. Nothing reads `package.json.version` for the product version; nothing writes the product version into `package.json`.
- **D4-2 — `CHANGELOG.md` = Keep-a-Changelog, the release-notes source.** A fresh root `CHANGELOG.md` (the old one was a stale NanoClaw snapshot, deleted §24.132). Top `## [Unreleased]`, then released sections `## [x.y.z] - YYYY-MM-DD`. Identity-free (a tracked, public file — placeholder discipline, no real name/employer/sim-target). Seeded with a `## [1.0.0]` launch section (a product-level summary); the owner finalizes its date at the cutover tag.
- **D4-3 — `release.yml` cuts a GitHub Release from the tagged CHANGELOG section.** Triggers **only** on a `v*` tag push (manual tag = owner controls timing). It runs a **pure, unit-tested extractor** that pulls the `## [x.y.z]` section body for the tag, then `gh release create <tag> --notes-file …`. **Guard:** an empty/missing section **fails** the job (no empty release for an un-changelogged tag). `contents: write` is the only added permission.
- **D4-4 — `VITE_APP_VERSION` is build-time, resolved in `deploy-frontend.yml`.** Two build vars feed the chip, both with deterministic local fallbacks:
  - `VITE_APP_VERSION` — the display label.
  - `VITE_APP_VERSION_REF` — a **repo-relative path** the chip links to (`releases/tag/v1.0.0` or `commit/<full-sha>`); empty → the chip renders as plain text. The frontend composes the href as `${REPO_URL}/${REF}` so `REPO_URL` (site.ts) stays the **single source** for the host part.
  - **master (prod):** label = the nearest `v*` tag (`git describe --tags --match 'v*' --abbrev=0`); REF = `releases/tag/<tag>` **iff** HEAD is exactly that tag (`git tag --points-at HEAD`), else `commit/<sha>` (an honest "prod is N commits past the last release"). Pre-first-tag fallback: label = short-sha, REF = `commit/<sha>`. Checkout uses `fetch-depth: 0` so tags are visible to `git describe`.
  - **dev:** label = `dev · <short-sha>`, REF = `commit/<full-sha>`.
  - **local / unset (`dev:mock`, `vite dev`, the @visual harness):** label = `dev`, REF = empty → a deterministic, link-free chip (no sha leaks into a visual baseline).
- **D4-5 — the chip lives in `SiteFooter`, one token, byte-stable, NOT a resurrection of the §24.35 metadata footer.** A single muted version token next to the "Built with" credit (label, optionally linked). It carries **only** the version — no live status, no cache state, no build metadata (that multi-field footer was retired §24.35 and stays retired). `appVersion()` reads `import.meta.env` at **call time** (not module-eval) so it's `vi.stubEnv`-testable.
- **D4-6 — release ↔ deploy stay decoupled (documented, not auto-wired).** `release.yml` (tag → Release) and `deploy-frontend.yml` (branch push → deploy) are separate concerns. At cutover the owner pushes the master commit **and** the tag together (`git push --follow-tags`): the branch push redeploys prod (the chip picks up `git describe` = the new tag) and the tag push cuts the Release. A **tag-only re-release** (no code change) does not auto-redeploy — the owner re-runs `deploy-frontend` via its existing `workflow_dispatch`. This is the lowest-complexity correct model (no fragile tag-push `paths` interaction); recorded in `PROD_CUTOVER.md`.

**Decomposition (DD cadence).**
- **Commit 1 — spec (no deploy).** This §24.139; the §24.38 Phase-9 table repoint (the 9.4 cutover row → §24.136 Phase B/9.7 + Phase 10 → Phase C); PORTAL.md §8.2 footer version-chip note.
- **Commit 2 — version chip + extractor + CHANGELOG.** Root `CHANGELOG.md`; `scripts/changelog-section.ts` (a pure extractor + a CLI shim) + its host unit test; `site.ts` `appVersion()` + the two env vars; the `SiteFooter` chip; frontend unit + e2e; `@visual` re-bless (the footer chip now shows `dev`).
- **Commit 3 — CI wiring.** `release.yml` (tag → extractor → `gh release create`, empty-section guard); `deploy-frontend.yml` `VITE_APP_VERSION`/`_REF` computation (master `git describe` + `fetch-depth: 0`, dev `dev · sha`). Both are deploy-infra; the deploy-frontend change ships on the next dev push and the chip goes live.

**Definition of done.**
- `package.json.version` is untouched; the product version exists only in `CHANGELOG.md` + the `v*` tag (no conflation).
- The `changelog-section` extractor is unit-tested (correct section body for a present version; empty for an absent one; date-format-agnostic).
- `release.yml` cuts a GitHub Release from the matching CHANGELOG section on a `v*` tag and **fails** on an empty/missing section (the extractor is green now; the workflow is exercised at the first real tag at cutover).
- The footer chip renders the version, links to the release tag (prod) / commit (dev) / nothing (local), is **byte-stable** (deterministic `dev` under the seed — no layout shift, no sha in the baseline), and does not reintroduce the retired §24.35 metadata footer.
- host + frontend `tsc` + suites green; the footer e2e covers the chip; `@visual` re-blessed (local-only).

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.139; §24.38 Phase-9 table repoint (9.4 → §24.136 B/9.7, Phase 10 → C); PORTAL §8.2 (footer version-chip note). Memory: [[status_current]].

## §24.140 — Phase A2.5: model-choice audit + the sandbox cost lever (cheaper visitor path; host-call model knobs)

A pre-cutover audit of every model choice (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) against its purpose — an owner pre-prod TODO, run before A3. Output: **one behavior change** (the public visitor path drops Opus→Sonnet) + **two new prod-safe knobs** that also answer the owner's "should model choices be knobs?".

**The map (as audited).** *Agent runtime (in-container):* owner orchestrator + owner subagents (research-company / tailor-resume / draft-outreach / scrape-jobs / build-interview-kit) = **Opus**; funnel-curator / pipeline-scribe = **Sonnet**. The **public sandbox group** orchestrator + its 3 subagents = **Opus**. *Host `callPortkeyChat` (4 total):* kit-entity-redact = **Sonnet** (the §24.134e leak belt), win-confidence = **Haiku**, sanitizer pass-3 = **Haiku** (off by default), recruiter-sim prose = **Haiku** (dev fixture). WebFetch/WebSearch internal summarization = Haiku (Claude Code internal).

**Decisions (owner, 2026-06-20).**
- **D1 — the public "Watch it work" sandbox runs Sonnet, not Opus.** It's the only path strangers can spend money on; Sonnet is ~40% cheaper (3×/15× vs 5×/25×) and more abuse-resilient, with a marginal quality delta on bounded, capped runs. The owner's REAL search is untouched (a separate agent group).
- **D2 — the owner agent stays Opus.** Low volume, real outcomes, quality-first. No owner-side trims (scrape-jobs was considered, kept Opus).
- **D3 — kit-entity-redact stays Sonnet — do NOT downgrade.** Security-load-bearing (§24.134e; Haiku *leaked* company tokens on the public kit surface).
- **D4 — the lever is a per-group alias-retarget, NOT frontmatter edits.** The rendered `groups/<g>/.claude/agents/*.md` are regenerated every spawn from the SHARED `_shared-subagents/*.md` (one source feeds BOTH owner + sandbox via `composeSubagentDefinitions`), so editing frontmatter either doesn't stick or downgrades the owner too. Instead the sandbox container retargets its `model: opus` alias (`ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL`) + the orchestrator `config.model` to Sonnet — the same mechanism `dev_model_tier` uses (`applyModelTier`, renamed from `applyDevModelTier`), but applied **unconditionally** (prod too — unlike the `ENVIRONMENT==='dev'`-only `dev_model_tier` branch) and **scoped to `group.folder === 'career-pilot-sandbox'`**.
- **D5 — expose it as a prod-safe knob: `sandbox_model_tier` (default `'sonnet'`).** This IS the answer to "should model choices be knobs": the visitor model tier becomes an `/admin` + `/dev` lever — and it is **NOT deny-listed**, because it only affects the public sandbox, never the owner orchestrator (contrast `dev_model_tier`, which retargets the owner agent and stays dev-only / ADMIN_DENY). Values map through `applyModelTier`: `default` = Opus / `sonnet` = Sonnet (the default) / `haiku` = Haiku. Applied before the `dev_model_tier` branch so a dev experiment can still override it on dev.
- **D6 — knob-ify the one prod host call worth it: `win_confidence_model` (default Haiku).** Mirrors the existing `sanitization_pass3_model` / `kit_entity_redact_model` knobs. recruiter-sim prose stays hardcoded Haiku (a dev-only fixture; not worth a knob, and it would land in the deny-listed recruiter_sim cluster).
- **D7 — subagent/orchestrator models stay committed in frontmatter/config, NOT per-subagent admin dials.** A quality-affecting model choice is a reviewed code change with a test, not a runtime dial flippable mid-demo; the coarse tiers (`dev_model_tier` for dev, `sandbox_model_tier` for the prod sandbox) cover the legitimate runtime levers.

**Per-IP note (the owner's adjacent question).** No new work: the simulator + contact per-IP/budget caps are ALREADY host-side knobs (`sandbox_per_ip_daily_run_cap`, `sandbox_daily_global_budget_usd`, `simulator_max_budget_usd`, `contact_relay_max_per_window` / `_window_sec`); the only non-knob is the EDGE Workers-RL burst, which can't read the DB by architecture (§24.70 D4 — already noted on the `/admin` System tab).

**Decomposition.**
- **Commit 1 — spec (this; no deploy).**
- **Commit 2 — build.** `applyDevModelTier`→`applyModelTier` (pure tier mapping, honest name; update its call site + the unit test); the sandbox-group tier hook in `materializeContainerJson` (prod + dev, before the `dev_model_tier` branch); `sandbox_model_tier` + `win_confidence_model` in `config/defaults.json` + `knob-registry.ts` (group `models`; neither deny-listed); `win-confidence.ts` reads `win_confidence_model`; the coverage test auto-enforces the two new keys. Host vitest + tsc green.

**Definition of done.** The sandbox group's container config resolves `model: opus` + the orchestrator to Sonnet by default — the `applyModelTier` mapping is unit-tested, and the sandbox-branch wiring follows the established `materializeContainerJson` coverage (the agent-runner config round-trip, not a host unit test — `container-config.test.ts` deliberately skips the FS+DB materialize path); the owner group is unchanged (still Opus); `sandbox_model_tier` + `win_confidence_model` render on `/admin` (System → models) + `/dev` and are NOT in `ADMIN_DENY`; win-confidence reads the knob; the coverage test passes; kit-entity-redact still Sonnet. Host `tsc` + suites green. No frontend code change (the knob grid is registry-driven); no `@visual` change.

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.140; the §24.136 Phase-A list gains A2.5; `config/defaults.json` + `knob-registry.ts`. Memory: [[status_current]].

## §24.141 — Phase A3: security — the threat-model pass (D5)

The fourth slice of §24.136 **Phase A**. Security as a *structured* pass, not ad-hoc: enumerate every public surface, state what protects it and what doesn't, then fix the gaps that matter before the prod surface exists. The artifact is the new **`.specs/THREAT_MODEL.md`** (twin of RECOVERY/PROD_CUTOVER) — this section is the plan-of-record + the decision/triage log behind it. No new product capability; this is the hardening the public surface earns before cutover.

**The four steps (D5).** (1) the threat-model doc — surfaces → assets → threats → existing mitigations → residual gaps; (2) `/security-review` on the public surface, findings triaged into the doc's backlog; (3) targeted hardening of the fix-now findings; (4) the sandbox prompt-injection red-team (the hardest surface) documented. The **live** abuse-sim (per-IP cap, global $-budget, Workers-RL, origin-JWT-rejects-forgery all engaging on the real surface) is Phase C — it needs prod to mean anything.

**Surfaces (as modeled).** S1 the **simulator** (the only money path); S2 **prompt injection / the sandbox agent on visitor input** (the hardest); S3 the **`/contact` relay** (no money — pure delivery); S4 the **API perimeter** (browser→Worker-only + service token + origin-JWT); S5 the **`/r` redirect** (closed — DB-controlled, `/`-relative, `//` rejected); S6 **owner `/admin`+`/dev`** (boundary, not a fuzz target). The full per-surface analysis lives in the doc.

**The investigation (2026-06-20) corrected the original pass.** Tracing the caps + the sandbox tool palette end-to-end falsified two assumed mitigations and surfaced a Critical containment gap:
- The intended **in-SDK per-run caps were never wired.** `claude.ts:785` sets no `maxTurns`/`maxBudgetUsd`; `simulator_max_turns` is a dead knob (no runtime consumer); `simulator_max_budget_usd` is only the host-side in-flight *estimate*. The only per-run bound is the **300s hard-wall** (which does force-kill the container). The cribsheet (`AGENT_SDK_PATTERNS.md §6`) specs both caps — spec↔code drift.
- The **public sandbox agent retains `Bash` + `Write` + `Edit`** (only the 21 private `mcp__nanoclaw__*` tools are disallowed; `TOOL_ALLOWLIST` keeps Bash/Write/Edit), and the **container runs on the default Docker bridge with full egress, no resource caps, no cap-drops** (`blockedHosts` is a hostname blackhole, IP-bypassable). The cribsheet specs `disallowedTools: ["Bash","Write","Edit",…]` for the sandbox — never implemented.

**Findings triage (the doc's §5 backlog) + owner dispositions (2026-06-20).** Disposition = now / cutover / accept.
- **S2-0 (CRITICAL, now) — the sandbox tool + container containment gap.** A successfully-injected visitor can run arbitrary code + arbitrary outbound calls inside an internet-connected container on the VM; the container boundary + the 300s kill are the only containment. **Owner-approved:** ① disallow `Bash`/`Write`/`Edit` in the sandbox (it needs only `WebSearch`/`WebFetch`/`Read`); ② harden the container — block the metadata IP / egress policy + `--memory`/`--cpus`/`--pids-limit` + `--cap-drop=ALL` + read-only rootfs where feasible.
- **S1-1 (H, now) — the per-run caps are unwired + the estimate is 3.5× low.** Real ~$0.35/run (owner-measured; the WebFetch/WebSearch majority is Haiku-hardcoded by the SDK, so A2.5's Opus→Sonnet trimmed only the reasoning minority). **Direction:** wire `maxTurns`/`maxBudgetUsd` into the sandbox query so the dead knobs become real, and fix the in-flight estimate `0.1 → 0.35`. NB `maxBudgetUsd` enforces on the SDK's *estimated* cost and may not meter the Haiku web-search/fetch spend → keep the hard-wall + global budget as the real $-backstop.
- **S1-2 (M, now) — re-tune the daily caps.** $5/day ÷ ~$0.35 ≈ 14 runs; one IP at cap-5 = $1.75. **Owner-approved:** `sandbox_per_ip_daily_run_cap` **5 → 3**, `sandbox_daily_global_budget_usd` **$5 → $10** (both knobs — easily reverted via `/admin`). Note the ceilings on the `/admin` System note.
- **S2-1 (H, now) — SSRF blast-radius (GCP metadata → SA token).** `blockedHosts` won't stop the IP. **Owner-approved:** measure on the dev box (container→metadata reachability + the VM SA scopes), then constrain (mostly subsumed by S2-0).
- **S2-2 (M, now) — un-guardrailed free-text share output.** Only the résumé block is honesty-anchored; the free-text chat is published to the 30-day share page un-checked. **Owner decision pending:** accept (ephemeral) vs. a light output check / shorter TTL / résumé-block-only sharing.
- **S3-1 (L, now) — Telegram markdown link-injection.** Escape MD specials in `buildContactNotification`'s visitor fields (or send plain text).
- **A-1 (H, cutover) — the origin-JWT is the only backend auth and is inert pre-cutover.** Phase B confirms `origin_jwt_validation_enabled=true` + `CF_ACCESS_*`; Phase C records a forged assertion getting a 403.
- **Accepted (recorded):** S1-3 (`checkSimulatorAllowed` fails open — the hard-wall + global budget still bound), S3-2 (email not format-validated — cosmetic), A-2 (no per-connection SSE cap — bounded by the edge + read-only).

**Red-team plan (doc §6).** R1 reach a private tool (expect: two-layer isolation blocks) · R2 exfil PII-beyond-public (expect: sandbox self-knowledge is the public profile only) · R3 SSRF (expect: egress containment — the S2-1 unknown) · R4 defamatory output (expect: per the S2-2 bar) · R5 turn/budget exhaustion (expect: maxTurns/maxBudgetUsd/hard-wall bind — ties to S1-1) · R6 cross-visitor leakage (expect: per-thread isolation). Run on the **dev** sandbox (real container/tools; dev's always-pass Turnstile lets the edge through); FAIL → a fix-now finding.

**Decomposition (DD cadence) — re-scoped after the investigation.**
- **Commit 1 — spec (shipped, no deploy).** §24.141 + `.specs/THREAT_MODEL.md` (new) — then **corrected** with the investigation findings (S2-0 + the unwired-caps reality) + the owner dispositions.
- **Box check (no commit) — measure S2-1.** On the dev box: can a sandbox-image container reach `169.254.169.254`/metadata, and what are the VM service-account scopes? Informs how hard the egress block must be. Relay any Tailscale auth link.
- **Commit 2 — S2-0 the containment fix (the lead).** ① Sandbox tool lockdown: add `Bash`/`Write`/`Edit` to the sandbox disallow set (`SANDBOX_DISALLOWED_TOOLS` / the sandbox `extraDisallowedTools`). ② Container hardening in the run-args builder (`container-runner.ts`): block the metadata IP / egress, `--memory`/`--cpus`/`--pids-limit`, `--cap-drop=ALL`, read-only rootfs where feasible — applied to the sandbox group (verify it doesn't break the owner agent's Bash-using subagents). Tests for the disallow-set + the args builder.
- **Commit 3 — S1-1/S1-2 the money path.** Wire `maxTurns`/`maxBudgetUsd` into the sandbox SDK query (plumb the two knobs through the provider); estimate `0.1 → 0.35`; `sandbox_per_ip_daily_run_cap` `5 → 3`; `sandbox_daily_global_budget_usd` `5 → 10`. Tests; correct the now-inaccurate `simulator.ts` comments.
- **Commit 4 — S3-1 + `/security-review`.** Escape MD specials in `buildContactNotification` (S3-1); run `/security-review` on the public surface + fold any mechanical findings into the backlog. (S2-2 awaits the owner's share-bar call — may land here or be accepted.)
- **Commit 5 — the red-team execution** (R1–R7 on dev) recorded in the doc's changelog; R3/R7 run before+after the S2-0 lockdown; FAILs folded back as fixes.

**Owner decisions — RESOLVED 2026-06-20** (① disallow Bash/Write/Edit: yes; ② box-check metadata: yes; ④ per-IP→3, global→$10 knob-reversible: yes). **Still pending:** the S2-2 free-text share bar (accept vs. guardrail/TTL/résumé-only).

**Definition of done** (matches §24.136 A3). The threat-model doc enumerates every public surface with mitigations + residual gaps (✅, corrected); the S2-0 containment fix + the money-path fixes (S1-1/S1-2) land with tests; `/security-review` is run and its findings triaged (fixed or explicitly accepted with a reason); the sandbox prompt-injection red-team (R1–R7) is documented (S2-0 confirmed closed by R3/R7 after-runs). Host + frontend `tsc` + suites green for any code touched. The cutover-disposition findings (A-1) carry forward into `PROD_CUTOVER.md` / Phase C.

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.141; `.specs/THREAT_MODEL.md` (new); the §24.136 Phase-A A3 bullet refined. Memory: [[status_current]].

---

## §24.142 — A4.0: sandbox tailored-résumé quality — the model split

First slice of §24.136 **Phase A** A4 (before the master-résumé content pass). Triggered by a dev smoke test where sandbox tailored résumés looked "off". The investigation (box-verified 2026-06-21) found a self-inflicted regression on top of a genuine quality gap.

**Root cause — a §24.141 (A3) regression.** A3 wired `maxBudgetUsd` into the sandbox SDK query and set `simulator_max_budget_usd=$0.50`. The SDK HALTS the agent loop the instant cumulative cost crosses the cap. A Sonnet `research-company` alone costs ~$0.50, so every Sonnet sandbox run died right after research (`num_turns:1`, no tailor/draft/deliver — the visitor got a bare "On it" ack). Haiku runs (~$0.37) stayed under the cap → completed. That single number was the entire Haiku-completes-vs-Sonnet-stalls split; it was NOT a prompt problem. Confirmed by raising the cap → the full chain ran ($0.81, 299.6s — 0.4s under the 300s hard-wall).

**The fix — a surgical model split (sandbox-only).** A uniform-Sonnet sandbox is too slow (~300s, at the hard-wall) and too costly (~$0.81) — the latency hog is `research-company`'s web fan-out (12 calls). So the sandbox now runs its **orchestrator on Sonnet** (it writes the `tailored_resume_json` bio — the one quality lever the visitor sees) and **all subagents on Haiku** (research = retrieval/summarization; `tailor-resume`'s bullets are snapped to the master verbatim by `validateTailoredResume` so its model barely affects output; draft = a sample email). Near full-Haiku latency, Sonnet-grade bio. Implemented as `applySandboxModelSplit(config, orchestratorModel, subagentModel)` in `container-config.ts` (config.model = orchestrator; the `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL` aliases carry the `model: opus` subagents to the subagent tier). Knob-driven: `sandbox_orchestrator_model` (default `claude-sonnet-4-6`) / `sandbox_subagent_model` (default `claude-haiku-4-5`) — replaces the §24.140 `sandbox_model_tier` knob. `dev_model_tier` is now **owner-scoped** so the sandbox runs identically on dev and prod. Caps relaxed: `simulator_max_budget_usd` `0.5 → 1.0`, `simulator_hard_wall_ms` `300000 → 360000`.

**Persona hardening (kept, not the root fix).** The sandbox persona (`.claude-host-fragments/persona.md`) gained a one-shot turn-discipline guard — no status acks, "subagent dispatch doesn't end your turn", deliver once — which killed a wasteful "On it" `send_message`.

**Still open (A4 remainder).** The **bio honesty-floor**: even on a fully-completed run the bio floors to master verbatim (`validateTailoredResume` reverts the bio to master when it's a stub OR cites any number not in the master — the "approximate metric" pattern; `snapBullets` forces experience bullets to master). Visible tailoring collapses to `lookingFor`. Fix candidates: bio-fallback telemetry + persona "describe impact in words, not an unverifiable number" + soften the unverified-number rule. The `projectsFirst` lever (Projects-above-Experience) is untested until delivery is reliable. Then the master-résumé content pass (real `candidate_profile`, DB-only).

**Definition of done.** Sandbox delivers a full tailored résumé on the split tier within the hard-wall, box-verified for latency (target <3 min) + cost + a non-floored bio where honest; `container-config` + `knob-registry` suites green (the defaults↔registry coverage test covers the knob swap); `dev_model_tier` owner-scoping verified to not clobber the sandbox. Memory: [[a40-sandbox-tailoring-investigation]] [[status_current]].

**Spec deltas.** This §24.142; the §24.136 A4 bullet gains the A4.0 sub-item. No THREAT_MODEL change.

---

## §24.143 — A4 #2: the tailored-bio honesty floor (telemetry + the anti-fabrication scrub)

Second A4 slice (after §24.142's model split). The owner's "tailored résumé reads like the master" symptom traced to the bio honesty floor firing *correctly*: the model fabricated a metric (it shrank a real, unusually large speed-up figure into a made-up "60%"), and `validateTailoredResume` reverted the bio to the honest master — so the visible "untailored" output is the safety net working, not a bug. The same fabrication leaks into the un-guardrailed free-text bullets + cold-email (the A3 S2-2 surface), and §24.142's move of the subagents to Haiku raises that risk.

The floor is correct (softening it would publish the fabrication), so the fix targets the *source*:
- **Telemetry (make the silent revert visible).** `validateTailoredResume` now returns `bioOutcome` (`tailored` / `fallback_stub` / `fallback_unverified_number`) + the offending `bioUnverifiedNumbers`; the simulator caller logs the non-`tailored` cases ("tailored bio fell back to master" with the reason + the fabricated tokens). Floor frequency + cause are now greppable.
- **Persona scrub.** The sandbox persona's numbers rule already forbade "60%"; the model did it anyway (a Haiku subagent fabricating, surfaced unscrubbed). Hardened: the orchestrator is the LAST check on every number in the final message (bullets + email + JSON) — use the real figures verbatim (large/unusual ones EXACTLY, never shrunk) or words-only; strip a subagent's approximated metric before delivering.

**Open / escalation.** Prompt-only is unproven against a determined Haiku fabrication; the telemetry is the gate — if the floor keeps firing post-deploy, escalate to a code belt on the free-text (mirror the number check on the displayed bullets + email) or move the honesty-critical subagent up a tier. `projectsFirst` verification + the master-résumé content pass still follow.

**Update (§24.143b) — the real root cause was CONTENT + a stubbed bio, not fabrication.** The eerily-identical recurring figure was the candidate's OWN real data (an approved figure in `master_resume`, not the model improvising), faithfully reproduced — never a fabrication. And `master_resume` (what `tailor-resume` reads via `candidate.md`) is a thinner, internally-inconsistent draft vs the polished `work_profile` (the /work page + the PDF-validation master, which carries the candidate's stronger real metrics), so the PDF snaps/floors to the polished master and reads as untailored. The bio specifically floors because the orchestrator writes a tailored summary as prose but STUBS the JSON `bio` (telemetry: `reason=fallback_stub`). Fix: the persona now LEADS the deliverable with a `## Summary` prose section, and the simulator back-fills a stubbed JSON `bio` from that prose via `extractSummarySection` (re-validated for honesty by `validateTailoredResume`) — so a tailored summary lands on the PDF without depending on the model filling the JSON field. The thin-vs-rich profile split is the owner's master-résumé content pass; pointing the sandbox tailor at `work_profile` is the candidate technical follow-on.

**Definition of done.** Bio-floor telemetry lands with tests (`bioOutcome` asserted across stub / fabrication / honest); the persona scrub ships; the `## Summary` → `bio` back-fill (§24.143b) ships with `extractSummarySection` tests; box-verified — a re-run shows a role-specific (non-master) bio on the PDF. Host tsc + the tailored-resume/simulator suites green.

**Spec deltas.** This §24.143. No THREAT_MODEL change (S2-2 disposition unchanged — prompt-first per the owner's A3 lean, with the code belt as the data-gated escalation).

---

## §24.144 — A4 #3: structured output for the tailored résumé (the `emit_tailored_resume` tool)

Third A4 slice. §24.143b's `## Summary` → `bio` back-fill (`4b843f7`) **failed box-verification** (`sb-c6563a37`): the model emitted `## Summary`, the lift code deployed, yet the bio still floored `fallback_stub`. The owner's call: the hand-written-JSON-fence transport is fragile ("ghetto") — replace it with **structured output** so a non-empty `bio` is forced by construction, finishing the `emit_tailored_resume` tool migration 135 always referenced but never built (the ```tailored-resume-json fence is the shortcut that shipped).

**Mechanism — tool-use structured output, NOT the Messages-API `output_config`.** Two distinct features share the name "structured output"; only one fits our architecture:
- The Messages-API `output_config.format` / `messages.parse()` / `strict` constrains a *single direct API call's* JSON response. The sandbox is **not** one call — it's a Claude Code agent loop dispatching subagents and producing a multi-part deliverable (chat + bullets + outreach + résumé). There is no single JSON response to constrain. Inapplicable (and absent from our stack — `AGENT_SDK_PATTERNS.md` has zero `output_config` references).
- The applicable mechanism is **a tool with a strict input schema + handler validation + the `isError` retry loop.** Verified against the authoritative Agent-SDK custom-tools doc: a handler returning `isError: true` → *"Agent loop continues. Claude sees the error as data and can retry, try a different tool, or explain the failure."* The doc's own example rejects bad input so the model corrects *"rather than treating a failure as a normal result."* A stubbed bio is **rejected and re-emitted**, not silently floored — the forcing function the fence never had.

**Design (sandbox-only).**
- **Container side** — a new `emit_tailored_resume` MCP tool (mirrors the existing `set_work_profile`, which already takes a structured `{ profile }`). `inputSchema` declares the tailored shape with `bio` **required**; the handler hard-validates (bio present + ≥80 chars of real summary, ≥1 experience) and on a stub returns `isError` with an actionable message → the model re-emits. The tool becomes the **only** path to a downloadable résumé, so the model can't stub-and-escape into free text.
- **Host side (as shipped)** — the profile rides a **PLAIN-registered, sandbox-safe `career_pilot.emit_tailored_resume` action** (`sendAction`): the handler resolves the run via `session.thread_id == runId` and stashes the payload into `acc.tailoredProfile` before `persistRun`'s single INSERT, which runs the **same** `validateTailoredResume` against the master. Replaces the regex fence-parse (`extractTailoredResumeBlock`).
- **Wiring decision — a sandbox-safe action, NOT capture-from-stream.** Capture-from-stream was the first design, but the existing sandbox trace event carries only a PII-safe `input_summary` (`claude.ts` `sdkMessageToTraceEvents`), not the full profile, so harvesting the tool input from the stream would need a dedicated runner emission — high blast radius on the upstream-synced runner/output-protocol. The sandbox is also FORBIDDEN from owner-only actions (`registerOwnerOnly`+`denyIfNotOwner`), so the chosen path registers `emit_tailored_resume` **PLAIN** and makes it sandbox-safe by construction (writes only the caller's own in-flight simulator run, host-validated against the public master) — the §24.68 D-C telemetry-action precedent. Reuses proven `sendAction` + delivery-action infra; touches zero runner code.

**Keep / kill.**
- **KEEP** `validateTailoredResume` — the host-side honesty backstop (employer/number checks, master-floor), unchanged. It is the safety net, not the fence: a model that exhausts retries still yields the master, never nothing.
- **KILL** the §24.143b `## Summary` back-fill + `extractSummarySection` (`4b843f7`, superseded) and the `extractTailoredResumeBlock` / fence-parse path.

**Definition of done.** The sandbox emits its tailored résumé via `emit_tailored_resume`; a stubbed/empty bio is rejected and re-emitted (handler `isError`, container-side); the host reads the structured tool input (no fence) and persists the guardrail-validated profile; the §24.143b hack + fence path are removed. Container + host tsc green; agent-runner + tailored-resume/simulator suites green (fence tests replaced by tool-input + handler-validation tests). Box-verified: a real sandbox run yields a **role-specific (non-master) bio** on the PDF, within the hard-wall, on the §24.142 split tier. Memory: [[a40-sandbox-tailoring-investigation]] [[status_current]].

**Box-verified (`sb-0c6e2597`, 2026-06-21).** Structured output confirmed working — the tool round-tripped, `tailored_resume_json` persisted, and the Sonnet orchestrator wrote a genuinely role-tailored summary with NO stub. The agent-runner is bind-mounted RO from the host (`container/Dockerfile` L6 / `container-runner.ts:446`), so the tool went live with the host deploy — no image rebuild. The run surfaced the *remaining* blocker (→ §24.145, NOT structured output): the bio still floored `fallback_unverified_number` because the model tailors from the thin `master_resume` (whose figure for one accomplishment differs from the polished profile's) while the floor + PDF validate against `work_profile_json` (which expresses that same accomplishment with a different, stronger figure). The forcing function worked; the residue is a content-source mismatch.

**Spec deltas.** This §24.144; the §24.136 A4 line gains the #3 sub-item. Migration 135's header (`emit_tailored_resume`) becomes accurate (built, not aspirational). THREAT_MODEL: one new sandbox-reachable action (`career_pilot.emit_tailored_resume`), registered PLAIN but sandbox-safe by construction (writes only the caller's own simulator run, host-validated against the public master) — same disposition as the §24.68 telemetry actions; no new trust boundary.

---

## §24.145 — A4 #4: the sandbox tailor reads `work_profile`, not the thin `master_resume`

Fourth A4 slice, isolated by §24.144's box-verify. Structured output works, but the tailored bio still floors `fallback_unverified_number` because of a **content-source mismatch**, not the tool. The sandbox candidate context (`render-persona.ts:renderSandboxCandidate` → `candidate.md`) sources its `## Master resume` section + the `## Approved figures` honesty list from `candidate_profile.master_resume` — a thinner, internally-inconsistent draft. But **every consumer that renders or validates a résumé uses `work_profile_json`** instead:

| Consumer | Source |
|---|---|
| Master résumé download (`GET /api/resume.pdf`, Experience page) | `work_profile_json` (`api.ts` → `getPublicProfile().profile` → `renderResumePdf`) |
| Tailored résumé PDF (`/api/simulator/results/<id>/resume.pdf`) | `work_profile_json` (validated tailored profile, same engine) |
| Honesty floor (`validateTailoredResume`) | `work_profile_json` (`getPublicProfile().profile`) |
| **Sandbox tailor** (`candidate.md`) | **`master_resume`** ← the lone outlier |

So the model tailors from the thin draft's weaker figure while the floor + both PDFs use the polished profile's stronger figure for the same accomplishment → the floor reverts the bio. This is a genuine **code inconsistency** (the sandbox reads a non-canonical source), which is why the fix is code, not a hand-edit of the owner's résumé.

**The fix (sandbox-only).** In `renderSandboxCandidate`, when `work_profile_json` is present, render the `## Master resume` section AND derive the `## Approved figures` allow-list from the composed `WorkProfile` (parsed via `projectWorkProfile`) instead of `master_resume`. Then the sandbox reads the same canonical source the download/PDF/floor use → the model cites the strong, verifiable figures (which pass the floor by construction) → a tailored summary finally lands on the PDF, and the tailoring is *stronger* (the polished profile's figures read better than the thin draft's). Fall back to `master_resume` when no `work_profile_json` exists (a profile that hasn't composed a /work page yet), so nothing regresses. `master_resume` stays the raw onboarding paste + the owner-persona source; only the **public sandbox** switches. Host change; `candidate.md` re-renders at the next spawn (no image rebuild — the fragment is host-written).

**Definition of done.** `renderSandboxCandidate` sources `## Master resume` + `## Approved figures` from `work_profile_json` when present (else `master_resume`); `CandidateProfile` gains the `work_profile_json` field; render-persona tests cover the work_profile path (rich content rendered, approved figures from the WorkProfile, master_resume fallback when absent) + host tsc + suite green. Box-verified: a real no-JD sandbox run yields a tailored, **non-floored** bio on the downloadable PDF (the telemetry no longer logs `fell back to master` for the bio). Memory: [[a40-sandbox-tailoring-investigation]].

**Spec deltas.** This §24.145; the §24.136 A4 line gains the #4 sub-item. No THREAT_MODEL change (the public sandbox already exposes the same résumé content via the /work page + the master-résumé download; this just makes the sandbox tailor read it). No migration (the column exists; `readCandidateProfile`'s `SELECT *` already fetches it).

---

## §24.146 — A4 #5: structured cold-outreach email (both gifts forced + surfaced)

Fifth A4 slice, prompted by the §24.145 box-verify (`sb-5e7cd0d5`): the visitor's **second gift — the cold outreach email — vanished**. The orchestrator wrapped only a meta-status in its `<message>` ("Pitch delivered and PDF emitted. The deliverable leads with…") and left the real deliverable unwrapped, so the visitor got 495 chars of narration instead of the pitch. Root cause is structural, not a one-off: the **résumé is structured output** (the `emit_tailored_resume` tool — forced by construction) but the **cold email is plain free text inside the chat message** — the one deliverable nothing forces. So it is the one that drops. A persona band-aid ("always put the email in the message") was explicitly rejected — it keeps the fragile free-text path. **Do it the right way: make the email structured output too, symmetric with the résumé.**

The summary + bullets are *already* structured — they live inside `emit_tailored_resume` (summary = `bio`, bullets = `experience`) for the PDF; the chat stream is just a human-readable echo of them. **The cold email is the lone remaining free-text deliverable.** Structuring it makes both gifts forced, persisted, and independently surfaceable.

**Design (sandbox-only).**
- **Container tool `emit_cold_email`** (mirrors `emit_tailored_resume`): `inputSchema` { `subject` (required, non-empty), `body` (required, ≥ a substance floor) }; the handler rejects a stub subject/body with `isError` → the agent re-emits. On pass → a PLAIN-registered, sandbox-safe `career_pilot.emit_cold_email` action (`sendAction`) — same disposition as `emit_tailored_resume` (§24.144): writes only the caller's own run, resolved via `session.thread_id == runId`, stashed into `acc.coldEmail` before `persistRun`.
- **Persist** to the EXISTING `simulator_runs.outreach_draft` column (migration 107, `null` since 5.5c — built for exactly this) as JSON `{ subject, body }`. `handleSimulatorResult` already ships `outreach_draft` to the client.
- **Share page** (`watch.results.$id.tsx` / the results route): render `outreach_draft` as its own **"Cold outreach email" gift card** (subject + body + copy), symmetric with the résumé-download button. The chat stream (`tailored_resume`) now carries the `## Summary` + bullets only.
- **Persona rework (replaces the rejected band-aid).** The `<message>` carries the human-readable summary + bullets; the TWO gifts (résumé, email) go through their tools — neither is embedded in the message, and meta-narration is forbidden. Also folds in the **first-person summary** fix (the §24.145 follow-on nit: the tailored summary/bio must be in the candidate's own first-person voice, never third-person).

**Out of scope (tracked).** The email **number-honesty belt** — mirroring the bio's `masterNumbers` check on the email body so a fabricated metric can't ride the free-prose email (the §24.143 S2-2 surface) — stays prompt-first per the owner's A3 lean; revisit if telemetry shows leakage. This slice is about STRUCTURE (force + surface the email), not the number floor.

**Definition of done.** `emit_cold_email` forces a non-stub subject+body (handler `isError`); the host persists `{subject, body}` to `outreach_draft`; the share page renders the email as its own gift card; the chat no longer carries the email; the persona is reworked (no band-aid — calls the tool; first-person summary). Container + host + frontend tsc/lint green; agent-runner + portal suites green. Box-verified: a real no-JD run produces BOTH gifts — the downloadable PDF AND a present, non-narrated cold-email card — with a first-person summary.

**Spec deltas.** This §24.146; the §24.136 A4 line gains the #5 sub-item. THREAT_MODEL: one more sandbox-reachable-but-safe action (`career_pilot.emit_cold_email`), same disposition as `emit_tailored_resume`/the §24.68 telemetry actions. No migration (the `outreach_draft` column exists).

## §24.147 — A0 finishing touches (continuation): four frontend-polish slices

The §24.136 Phase-A "A0 touch-ups" list, second batch (owner, 2026-06-21). Four small, independent frontend slices; no backend, no migration. All on `dev`.

1. **Recent-outcomes day stamp.** Each `/dashboard` Recent-outcomes row gains a leading **date-only** label (`outcomeDate` → "Jun 18"), sourced from `last_activity_at`. Date-only is deliberate: the precise time of a recorded outcome often just reflects when the scribe processed it, so the DAY is the honest, useful unit (the owner's steer). Rendered in the **viewer's local timezone** (a recruiter reads it in their own day) — safe because the panel is client-only (`usePipeline` is empty on SSR, so no date renders server-side → no hydration mismatch). Fixed-width column (`w-12`) so a 1-digit and a 2-digit day align (every ref starts at the same x). The seed's `last_activity_at` is relative (`isoDaysAgo`) → drifts daily → the stamp carries `data-testid="recent-outcome-date"` and is masked in the `live` + `mobile-live` visual baselines (the format is covered by a timezone-robust panels unit test).

2. **`/` scroll reveal.** Below-the-fold home sections (pitch, pipeline strip, ticker, watch CTA, teaser) RISE into place as they enter the viewport, via a new `useReveal` IntersectionObserver hook (`src/lib/use-reveal.ts`) + `.cp-reveal` CSS. **Transform-only, never opacity** — the §24.135 constraint, reconfirmed here: a section armed at `opacity:0` is sampled by axe as a 1:1-contrast violation in a top-of-page scan (reproduced), and an opacity/`visibility` hide also breaks the smoke `toBeVisible()` ticker assertions. A translate-only rise satisfies axe, `toBeVisible`, the a11y tree, and the no-JS principle (progressive enhancement: the armed class is JS-applied; SSR / reduced-motion / no-IO render solid). The owner asked for a "fade"; this ships the codebase-consistent rise instead, grounded in the reproduced axe failure — surfaced for the owner. Visual baselines capture under `reducedMotion:'reduce'` (reveal disabled → deterministic, no IO-timing dependence under `captureBeyondViewport`); the reveal's real behavior is guarded by a mobile functional test.

3. **Redaction legend as a real component.** `RedactionLegend` becomes a bordered, titled "Redaction key" box (was a loose run of text) — it reads as a deliberate legend the chips map back to.

4. **Bold redaction token leak (bugfix).** `renderInline`'s `**bold**` branch only unescaped its text, so a redaction token inside bold (`**[REDACTED:infra-d] …**`) rendered as the raw literal instead of a provenance chip. The branch now runs `splitRedactionParts` (shared `renderWithRedactions` helper), so a token chips identically whether or not it sits inside bold.

5. **Pipeline `CompanyHandleLegend` unified.** The pipeline-page twin of the redaction legend was still a loose `<p>` after slice 3 restyled `RedactionLegend` — owner flagged the inconsistency. It now matches: the same bordered, titled box ("Company handles"), so the two anonymization legends read as siblings.

**Definition of done.** Frontend tsc + prettier `--no-semi` clean; the full vitest suite green (incl. new tests: bold-token chip, legend structure, recent-outcome date, the `useReveal` SSR-visible contract); mobile functional + axe green (the reveal is axe-clean); desktop smoke green (ticker `toBeVisible`); the affected `@visual` baselines regenerated (`live`, `mobile-live` for the date column; `funnel` + its loading/empty/error states + `mobile-pipeline` for the legend box; `kit-sealed`/`mobile-kit-sealed` for the legend; `home`/`mobile-home` were pixel-identical for the reveal).

6. **Child-stagger on the list-like sections.** The pitch's capability list and the teaser's three columns now CASCADE their items in (per-item delay via an inline `--cp-i` index) rather than the section rising as one block — a staggering section marks itself `cp-still` (cancels its block move) and tags its items `cp-stagger-item`. Same transform-only / reduced-motion / in-view-at-load-stays-solid contract as the block reveal. The reveal trigger lives in the observed element's class (the block doesn't move on a stagger element), so the reveal functional test asserts the `cp-reveal-in` class, not the section transform. **The reveal is observed on the items' own container** — the pitch list, NOT the pitch section: the section's top is the tall intro paragraph, so observing the section fired the cascade while the list was still below the fold (items animated unseen — owner-reported). The `useReveal` trip point was also moved from `-10%` to `-20%` bottom margin so the reveal plays while the element is comfortably on screen, not the instant it peeks past the fold.

**Resolved design questions (owner, 2026-06-21).** (a) Inter-section spacing: **left as-is** (`mt-24` is already generous). (b) Per-section bespoke entrances vs. uniform: **kept uniform** (consistency reads as intentional; bespoke multiplies the transform-only/a11y surface) — but the **gentle child-stagger** (slice 6) was added as the tasteful middle option. (c) An on-screen-at-load section not animating (a taller phone shows it before any scroll): **left by design** — animating content already in view is the post-load jump the SSR-solid hero contract avoids; smaller phones still animate it on scroll. The `useReveal` arm threshold (`top < innerHeight * 0.85`) is the boundary that encodes this.

## §24.148 — Gmail OAuth consent screen → Production (self-use path) + the `/privacy` policy page

Phase-B prep, buildable now (owner, 2026-06-21): get the career-account Gmail OAuth off the **Testing**-mode 7-day refresh-token clock that kills the token weekly (the dev box included).

**The scopes (confirmed on the Console's Data Access page after publishing).** The app's OAuth grant is `gmail.modify` (**restricted** — read/compose/send), `calendar.events.owned` (**sensitive** — schedule interviews), `drive.file` (**non-sensitive** — the interview-prep Docs the agent itself creates) + `openid`/`userinfo.email`/`userinfo.profile` (sign-in). `gmail.modify` is the restricted scope that gates this. (Correction: an earlier draft of this section wrongly listed three separate Gmail scopes AND claimed Drive/Calendar weren't on this screen — they ARE; the agent schedules interviews and writes interview-prep Docs in the candidate's own Drive via `drive.file`.) Restricted/sensitive both carry the same "verification not mandatory for self-use" conclusion below.

**Decision — Path A (self-use, unverified production).** Confirmed against Google's primary docs (Manage App Audience + restricted-scope verification): the 7-day refresh-token expiry is a **Testing-status** artifact; moving the consent screen to **In production** removes it, and for restricted scopes verification is **"not mandatory to continue operating."** Unverified, the app shows a one-time "unverified app" warning on consent and caps *new* authorizations at 100 users — both irrelevant for a single-user personal agent. So self-use needs **no Google review, no CASA security assessment, and no cost** — flip the publishing status + re-auth once. **This corrects the §24.136 / PROD_CUTOVER premise that the consent screen carried a "review lag":** the lag applies only to **full verification** (Path B = brand review + a paid annual third-party CASA assessment), which we are NOT pursuing (only needed to drop the warning for *other* users / go multi-user). (Path C — a Workspace account + "Internal" user type — removes the warning entirely with no verification, but needs a paid Workspace + a mailbox migration; declined.)

**Not IaC.** There is no Terraform/`gcloud` for the OAuth consent screen, its scopes, or the publishing status (`google_iap_brand` covers only IAP-*internal* screens). It's a **Console-only, owner-login** action; dev-side prep is all we can own.

**The `/privacy` + `/terms` pages (dev-side prep, BUILT).** Google's consent screen wants a privacy-policy URL (required for these scopes) and a terms-of-service URL (optional) on the app's domain. Both new pages ride the **shared `LongformDoc` scaffold** (§24.83 — masthead + sticky scroll-spy TOC, like `/about` + `/experience`), so they read as site documents, not one-offs:
- `privacy.tsx` — the FORMAL policy, distinct from the visitor-privacy NARRATIVE at `/about#privacy` (which stays): it carries the **Limited Use disclosure** for all three scope tiers (Gmail / Calendar / Drive — data used only to operate the owner's own features; may transit Anthropic/Portkey for drafting, not trained on; stored in the owner's own DB; never sold or ad-shared; revocable from Google account settings).
- `terms.tsx` — governs use of the public showcase (the simulator + contact form): use rules, the simulator-is-a-demo-not-an-offer disclaimer, an as-is/no-warranty clause.
Identity-safe (operator = build-time `PERSON_NAME` placeholder; contact via `/contact`; no hardcoded address/domain). Footer "Privacy" repointed `/about#privacy` → `/privacy`; `/terms` is **cross-linked from `/privacy` (and vice-versa), deliberately NOT footer-linked** — a new footer link is on every page's `fullPage` capture and would force an ~18-baseline @visual re-bless for a secondary legal page. OAuth URLs: privacy = `https://hire.<DOMAIN>/privacy`, ToS = `https://hire.<DOMAIN>/terms`.

**Owner's part (Console, ~5 min):** OAuth consent screen → publishing status **In production** → re-authorize the career account once (click through the unverified warning) → drop the fresh non-expiring token into OneCLI. Also fixes the dev box's weekly Gmail death (same project).

**Definition of done.** `/privacy` + `/terms` render on the `LongformDoc` scaffold; the footer e2e asserts the repointed Privacy link + the `privacy-google-data` section + "Limited Use"; no mobile h-scroll (`/privacy` + `/terms` added to the sweep); FE tsc/prettier/vitest green; route tree regenerated. PROD_CUTOVER §3 Gmail TODO resolved to Path A. Owner-gated: the Console publish + the live "no 7-day death" check (PROD_CUTOVER §6) is the owner's step.

## §24.149 — Launch-state & site lifecycle (cold-start framing + the concluded-search retrospective)

A new Phase-A pre-prod concern (owner, 2026-06-21): at the first public release the pipeline is empty — no applications, no outcomes, no captured "fuel" — so an early recruiter could land on a showcase that reads as **broken** rather than **new**; and at the other end of the search, an accepted offer has **no handling at all**. The site has a *lifecycle*, and today only the steady state (an active search with live data) is designed for. This slice defines the lifecycle, polishes the cold-start so newness reads as freshness, and builds the concluded-search retrospective.

**Audit (the gap is smaller than it looks).** The §24.36 (36.1) async-state discipline already gives every async surface a loading / empty / error treatment behind the `?__state=` mock seam (`states.spec.ts`): `/pipeline` empty already reads *"No applications in the search yet — the first agents are warming up"*; `/dashboard` quiet shows a connected-but-idle trace stream; LLM telemetry/spend show *"Awaiting the first…"* / *"No LLM spend…"*; the container/session panels render `0`/`—` with a *"0 at rest is normal"* explain-on-tap; the **simulator is fully data-independent** (the day-one carrier). So nothing renders bare-blank. The **real gaps**: (1) the empty copy is locally scoped and uneven in register — `RecentOutcomesPanel` just says *"No activity yet."*; (2) the home hero stat seed (`hero-seed-loader.ts` → `counts:[]` on an empty/failed fetch) must be confirmed to *settle* into an honest line rather than skeleton-forever (the one plausible "looks broken" bug); (3) **no concluded/offer-accepted state exists**; (4) the cold-start **sequencing** lever isn't written down.

**The lifecycle (the taxonomy).**

| State | What the public site shows | Designed for today? |
|---|---|---|
| **Cold launch** | empty pipeline; the simulator + evergreen pages carry it | partially (per-widget empty states) |
| **Active search** | the steady state — live pipeline, outcomes, fuel | yes |
| **Concluded (offer accepted)** | a retrospective case study — the journey, framed as done | **no** |
| **Dormant / archived** | long-after portfolio piece | later concern (not this slice) |

The **evergreen showcase** — `/about`, `/experience`, `/architecture`, the simulator — is **lifecycle-invariant by design**: it carries day one and survives the concluded flip (it's the candidate's #1 résumé item). Only the **job-search-data surfaces** (`/`, `/pipeline`, the `/dashboard` widgets) change with the lifecycle.

**Decisions.**
- **D1 — No fake data on the public prod surface, ever.** Emptiness handled honestly beats fullness faked; a recruiter who sensed padded activity would torch the credibility the rest of the site earns. The seeded dev/demo harness ([[project_dev_demo_harness]]) stays dev-only; prod shows only real agent activity.
- **D2 — Cold-start framing = subtle freshness cues, NOT a launch banner** (owner, 2026-06-21). Launch-aware empty copy in a consistent register + honest "live since / last activity" timestamps. **No** explicit "we just launched / day N" meta-banner — it advertises immaturity, and the SSR-solid hero contract (§24.135) already avoids meta-chrome. Newness reads through *freshness* (recent real timestamps, present-tense "the search just went live"), not an announcement.
- **D3 — Concluded state = a manual `/admin` toggle, never auto-inferred** (owner, 2026-06-21). A preference-tier `site_lifecycle_state` (`active` default | `concluded`), flipped deliberately from the §24.136 A1 `/admin` control-center; the public system-status read-model carries the current value; the frontend gates the retrospective framing on it. **Never** derived from a terminal stage or a win-confidence threshold — flipping the public site to "I got a job" on a data misread is exactly the failure a manual gate prevents.
- **D4 — Anonymization holds in the retrospective.** The accepted company stays an obfuscated handle by default — the existing `/pipeline` rule ("revealed only post-close with the company's awareness"). "Landed a role at `[infra-x]`" copy never leaks the employer unless the owner separately reveals it and the company is aware.
- **D5 — Sequencing: the quiet-observation window is the pipeline warm-up window** (the free, honest cold-start lever — runbook delta, below).

**Build (DD: this is the spec commit; build commits follow).**
- **L1 — Cold-start polish (frontend-only, subtle freshness).** Unify the launch-aware empty copy across the job-search widgets to the `/pipeline` register (bring `RecentOutcomesPanel`'s *"No activity yet."* up to a launch-aware line); confirm + fix the hero empty-seed so an empty pipeline settles into an honest stat line (or an honest "just went live" line), never a perpetual skeleton; add an honest "live since"/last-activity freshness signal where it reads naturally. No banner (D2). Covered by the existing `?__state=empty` seam + `states.spec.ts` extensions.
- **L2 — Concluded/retrospective mode.** The `site_lifecycle_state` preference + `getConfig` wiring + the §24.136 A1 `/admin` System-tab control (**confirm-gated** — it changes the *public* face); the public read-model field (rides the existing system-status/mode read that `ModeBanner`/`SystemStatusStrip` already consume); the frontend retrospective framing on `/` + `/pipeline` — a calm "search concluded — landed a role at `[handle]`" header, past-tense copy, the offer card as the journey's resolution; evergreen pages untouched; the simulator stays live; anonymization preserved (D4). The `?__state=` seam gains a `concluded` value so the mode is reachable in E2E/visual without a DB flip.
- **L3 — Sequencing runbook deltas.** `PROD_CUTOVER.md` §0/§7 + §24.136 Phase C: the quiet-observation window is also the warm-up window — run the real agent's **private/reversible** work freely (scrape, research, draft, build pipeline rows) so the pipeline is genuinely populated by publish time, with **irreversible outreach sends gated** until the owner is confident. (Recent *outcomes* are the slowest to fill — replies/interviews take real-world weeks — so the cold-start polish matters most there.)

**Definition of done.**
- **L1:** every job-search empty surface reads in one launch-aware register; the home hero never renders a perpetual skeleton on an empty pipeline (an `?__state=empty` home test proves the settled line); FE tsc + prettier `--no-semi` + vitest + the `states.spec.ts` extension green; affected `@visual` empty-state baselines re-blessed.
- **L2:** `site_lifecycle_state` is a `getConfig`-backed preference surfaced (confirm-gated) in `/admin`; flipping it to `concluded` flips `/` + `/pipeline` into the retrospective (header + past-tense copy + offer-as-resolution) with the accepted company still anonymized, and leaves `/about`/`/experience`/`/architecture`/the simulator unchanged; the `?__state=concluded` seam renders it deterministically; host + FE tsc + suites + an E2E for the concluded framing green; `@visual` concluded baselines added.
- **L3:** `PROD_CUTOVER.md` §0/§7 + §24.136 Phase C carry the warm-up-window + gated-sends sequencing; no code.

**Spec deltas (this drill-in).** This §24.149; the §24.136 Phase-A list gains **A5 (launch-state & lifecycle)** + the Phase-C "warm-up window" note; `PROD_CUTOVER.md` §0 (pre-flight gate) + §7 (soft-launch warm-up) sequencing deltas. **To land with the build:** PORTAL.md (the lifecycle-states note on `/` + `/pipeline` §5.4 + the concluded retrospective); `config/defaults.json` (`site_lifecycle_state` default `active` + the manual-toggle note); THREAT_MODEL (the `/admin` lifecycle toggle is an owner-only control that changes the public face — no new *public* surface; confirm-gated like the other destructive-ish admin controls). **Tracked follow-up (separate slice, owner-flagged 2026-06-21):** a **v1.0 doc/terminology-accuracy sweep** — README + root `CLAUDE.md` + the specs refreshed to v1.0 reality, plus the residual *internal* `funnel`→`pipeline` rename (the `public_funnel_view` read-model, the `/api/funnel` route, `record_funnel_event`/`persist_funnel_state`, the `funnel-*` test-ids, the `SimStatePanel` "funnel board" dev copy); visitor-facing prose is already "pipeline" (§24.77 D3). Memory: [[status_current]], [[todo_backlog]].

---

## §24.150 — Simulator "degradation as a feature" (branded unavailable states)

**Origin** (owner, 2026-06-21). Reviewing the `/architecture` Web-sandbox node throttling itself at the daily budget cap, the owner saw the opening: *"if we actually run out of budget and the sandbox ('watch it work') they're trying to use is no longer available, conditionally guide them to /dashboard or /architecture so they can see why — it flips that experience from disappointed to impressed; failure/degradation/unavailability as a feature."* Today the simulator's three distinct "can't run now" reasons collapse into two flat fallbacks: the kill switch → a generic *"taking a breather,"* and **both** the daily-budget cap and the per-IP cap → a generic *"that run didn't finish"* error. None of them point the dead-ended visitor at the surfaces that *explain the very cap they just hit* — which is the most on-brand content the site has.

**The reframe.** A visitor who hits the cap has just learned the showcase is real enough to **cost real money and run out** — among the most credible signals the site can send. So the unavailable state shouldn't apologize; it should **show them the meter**: the live LLM-spend ledger (`/dashboard`) and the self-throttling sandbox node on the system map (`/architecture`). The thing that blocked them becomes the proof it's real.

**Decisions.**
- **D1 — Branded by *reason*, not one generic wall.** `checkSimulatorAllowed` already computes the granular gate reason (`budget_exceeded` / `rate_limited_ip` / `simulator_disabled`); it's collapsed at the `SimulatorStartResult.error.code` boundary (`budget_exceeded`+`rate_limited_ip`→`RATE_LIMITED`→429; `simulator_disabled`+backend-not-ready→`UNAVAILABLE`→503). Surface the reason through to the frontend (a `reason` field on the non-ok `/api/simulator` JSON, alongside the **unchanged** HTTP status + `code` for back-compat) so each gets tailored copy + CTAs.
- **D2 — Three branded variants + one honest generic.**
  - `budget_exceeded` (the showcase) → *"The agent's been busy today."* It spent today's sandbox budget running live for other visitors; resets tomorrow → **See where it went → `/dashboard`** (the spend ledger) + **Watch it throttle → `/architecture`** (the Web-sandbox node).
  - `rate_limited_ip` (you personally) → *"You've used today's runs."* Per-visitor cap so one person can't drain it for everyone; resets tomorrow → **See the real pipeline → `/pipeline`** + the recent-runs list already surfaced below.
  - `simulator_disabled` (paused) → *"The live sandbox is paused for review."* The rest of the system is fully live → **See the pipeline → `/pipeline`** + **System map → `/architecture`**.
  - `backend_not_ready` / network / stream-drop → the existing honest generic ("taking a breather" / "that run didn't finish"). A real fault, not an intentional cap — no "feature" spin, no false reassurance.
- **D3 — "Talk to me →" stays on every variant.** The always-open conversion path is never removed; the branded CTAs are *added* alongside it.
- **D4 — Dev seam to reach the states without exhausting the budget.** A `?__sim=<reason>` URL override on `/watch`, gated to dev / `VITE_MOCK_SEAM` (mirrors `?__state` / `?__lifecycle`), short-circuits the run state to each fallback for visual/E2E coverage + the owner's screenshot. Prod ignores it (the server is the perimeter; this is a client-only render branch).

**Build (DD: this is the spec commit; build follows).**
- Backend: add `reason?: 'budget_exceeded' | 'rate_limited_ip' | 'simulator_disabled' | 'backend_not_ready'` to `SimulatorStartResult.error`; carry it in the `/api/simulator` non-ok JSON body. HTTP status + `code` unchanged.
- Frontend: `use-simulator-run` maps the body `reason` → a `degradeReason` on the run state (429 → `budget`/`rate_limit`; 503 → `disabled`, else generic). `SimFallback` renders the per-reason title/body + tailored CTAs (typed `<Link>`s, `data-testid`-hooked). `?__sim=` dev seam on `/watch`.
- Tests: `SimFallback.test.tsx` per-reason CTA assertions; `use-simulator-run` 429/503-reason mapping; a `@visual` baseline per branded variant via `?__sim=`.

**Definition of done.** Each of the three caps renders its own headline + CTA set pointing at the surface that explains it; the generic fault path is byte-unchanged; "Talk to me →" present on all variants; `?__sim=` reaches every state in dev/E2E; host + FE tsc + prettier `--no-semi` + suites green; `@visual` branded variants blessed. Memory: [[status_current]], [[todo_backlog]].

---

1. **Where exactly do we host OneCLI?** It runs as a local proxy at `127.0.0.1:10254` on the host. For local dev: same. For prod: it must run as a sidecar service or as a container on the VM. NanoClaw's `/init-onecli` skill handles this — assume their docs cover it, verify during Phase 0.

2. **Cloudflare Tunnel + SSE longevity:** Cloudflare Tunnel works for SSE but has connection-idle timeouts. Need to verify the default timeout is >5 minutes (our session ceiling) or configure keep-alives. Verify during Phase 4. **Resolution (§24.39, D9):** settled in the deployed dev env (Sub-milestone 9.2) against the live tunnel — the browser's direct SSE connection bypasses the Worker (and `EventSource` can't set headers), so it passes via the **Access session cookie** (`CF_Authorization`) instead of the Service-Auth header; the exact cross-host priming + the tunnel idle-timeout/keep-alive are verified against primary CF docs at build time.

3. **TanStack Start version pin:** ~~RC churn risk~~ — **resolved:** v1.0 shipped (2026-03). Pin the exact v1 minor we scaffold with; don't auto-update; re-evaluate upgrades at end of Phase 7. (Canonical stack captured in §24.23.)

4. **Portkey free tier ceiling:** ✅ RESOLVED (§24.44, 2026-06-05) — and the premise was wrong. Portkey bills on **recorded logs, not requests**: the free Developer tier caps at 10k *logs*/mo (3-day retention), but **the gateway keeps routing past the cap — it just stops recording new logs**. So the agent never throttles on the free tier; we only lose observability beyond 10k logs. Production is **$49/mo** (not $99): 100k logs + **semantic caching** + 30-day retention + alerts. Plan: route everything through Portkey on free first (§24.44), upgrade to Production reactively when observability/caching savings justify it.

5. **What's the URL for the public Telegram bot for visitors?** PORTAL.md §5.7 mentions one as an alternative contact path. Do we actually want a public Telegram bot, or drop it and rely only on the contact form? Recommendation: drop for v1 (the contact form covers it).

6. **Ollama in local dev vs eval-quality testing:** Llama 3.2 vs Claude output quality is night-and-day for nuanced tasks. We'll need a small budget for "real" testing of resume tailoring quality. Recommend $20/mo Anthropic budget for dev/testing.

7. **Initial obfuscated_label assignment:** ✅ RESOLVED (Phase 1) — and the answer is *deterministic*, not the LLM call this question guessed. At application creation, `deriveIndustry()` reuses `jd_analyzed.role_category` (the `analyze_jd` output already carried on the `update_application` patch; falls back to `misc`), and `nextObfuscatedLabel(industry)` appends the next free `<industry>-<letter>` by scanning existing labels for that industry. See `src/modules/career-pilot/actions.ts` (`deriveIndustry` / `nextObfuscatedLabel`) and the Part II schema note above; covered by `actions.test.ts` + `actions.integration.test.ts`.

8. **Headshot for /work:** If the candidate has one, easy. If not, we'll need a clean illustration or skip the headshot block. Owner decision pre-Phase 8.

---

## Part VII: What's next after STRATEGY.md

This doc is the architectural plan. The next concrete deliverables:

1. **CLAUDE.md (repo root) — rewritten** for the new structure. Orientation doc for any Claude Code session opening this repo: where things live, what tooling we use, what conventions we follow. Replaces the current `CLAUDE.md` which is stale.

2. **README.md — rewritten** for the new structure. Generic-by-design (career-pilot is meant to be forkable). Points to PORTAL.md and STRATEGY.md for depth.

3. **Phase 0 execution** — fork NanoClaw into this branch. The actual code-landing-on-disk step. Will be a meaningfully large commit (~150 files from NanoClaw + our scaffolding). Stop for review after fork lands.

4. **Migration files for the 8 new tables.** Phase 1's prerequisite.

5. **`groups/career-pilot/CLAUDE.md`** — the owner agent's persona doc. The single most important piece of writing in the system, because it shapes every agent decision. Worth its own focused writing session.

After STRATEGY.md sign-off, the order of operations is: rewrite root CLAUDE.md + README → Phase 0 fork → Phase 1 schema + first MCP tools → Phase 2... working forward.

Estimated total work to LIVE_MODE=true: ~10-11 weeks of focused part-time effort (assumes ~15-20 hours/week). Tighter if full-time; longer if intermittent.
